1352.title: Süleyman Pasha
1352.body: Süleyman Pasha (son of Orhan) is probably granted control of Çimpe Castle on the Gallipoli peninsula by Emperor John VI Kantakouzenos, the first territory west of the Bosporus held by the Ottoman Empire.

1352.title: Dragoş
1352.body: Coming from Hungary, the noble Vlach Dragoş becomes the first voivode of Moldova, being seen as the founder of this principality (some scholars place this moment as early as 1345).

1352.title: Ibn Battuta
1352.body: Moroccan traveller Ibn Battuta reports the existence of the ngoni and balafon musical instruments, at the court of Mansa Musa.

1352.title: Chinese Buddhism
1352.body: After years of begging and being a Buddhist monk, the penniless Chinese peasant Zhu Yuanzhang joins the Red Turban Rebellions against the Mongol-led Yuan dynasty of China; he will later become the first emperor of the Ming dynasty.

1352.title: Corpus Christi College, Cambridge
1352.body: Corpus Christi College is founded as a College of the University of Cambridge in England, by the Guilds of Corpus Christi and the Blessed Virgin Mary.

1353.title: Moroccan people
1353.body: The Moroccan traveler Ibn Battuta makes the first recorded visit to Timbuktu and Kabara, when returning from a stay in the capital of the Mali Empire.

1353.title: The Decameron
1353.body: The Decameron is finished by Giovanni Boccaccio.

1353.title: Canton of Bern
1353.body: Bern signs an alliance with the Old Swiss Confederacy.

1353.title: Lao people
1353.body: The Lao kingdom of Lan Xang is founded by Fa Ngum.

1354.title: Ottoman Empire
1354.body: March - Within a month after the devastating earthquake the Ottomans besieged and captured the town of Gallipoli, making it the first Ottoman stronghold in Europe and the staging area for Ottoman expansion across the Balkans.

1354.title: Ibn Battuta
1354.body: Early in the year – Ibn Battuta returns from his travels at the command of Abu Inan Faris, sultan of Morocco, who appoints a scribe to write an account of the adventures.

1354.title: John VI Kantakouzenos
1354.body: The reign of John VI Kantakouzenos as Byzantine Emperor is ended, after John V Palaiologos retakes Constantinople and is restored as sole emperor.

1354.title: Cola di Rienzo
1354.body: Cola di Rienzo, self-proclaimed "tribune" of Rome, is killed by an angry mob.

1354.title: War of the Straits
1354.body: War of the Straits: The Venetian-ruled town of Poreč is sacked by the Genoese under Paganino Doria, who carry off the relics of saints Eleutherius and Maurus of Parentium to Genoa, where they were deposited at the church of San Matteo.

1355.title: Charles IV, Holy Roman Emperor
1355.body: Charles IV is crowned Holy Roman Emperor in Rome.

1355.title: chevauchée
1355.body: Hundred Years' War: Black Prince's chevauchée of 1355 – A large mounted Anglo-Gascon force under the command of Edward the Black Prince marches from Bordeaux in English-held Gascony 300 miles (480 km) south to Narbonne and back, devastating a wide swathe of French territory.

1355.title: Venice
1355.body: In Venice, the Council of Ten beheads Doge Marin Falier, for conspiring to kill them.

1355.title: St Scholastica Day riot
1355.body: St Scholastica Day riot in Oxford, England, breaks out, leaving 63 scholars and perhaps 30 locals dead in two days.

1355.title: Red Turban Rebellions
1355.body: Red Turban Rebellions: Han Lin'er, a claimed descendant of Emperor Huizong of Song, is proclaimed emperor of the restored Song dynasty in Bozhou.

1356.title: Charles IV, Holy Roman Emperor
1356.body: Charles IV, Holy Roman Emperor, promulgates the Golden Bull, a constitution for his empire.

1356.title: Hundred Years' War – Battle of Poitiers
1356.body: Hundred Years' War – Battle of Poitiers: The English, commanded by Edward the Black Prince, defeat the French. The greatly outnumbered English forces not only rout the French, but capture the French king John II of France and his youngest son, the future Philip the Bold of Burgundy.

1356.title: Great Pyramid of Giza
1356.body: The majority of the Great Pyramid of Giza's limestone casing stones are removed by Bahri Sultan An-Nasir Hasan, to build fortresses and mosques in the nearby city of Cairo, leaving the first of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World in the step-stone condition in which it remains into modern times.

1356.title: Edward Balliol
1356.body: Edward Balliol surrenders his title as King of Scotland, to Edward III of England.

1356.title: February – Burnt Candlemas
1356.body: February – Burnt Candlemas: Edward III of England burns down every town and village in Lothian, Scotland.

1357.title: Shroud of Turin
1357.body: The first public exhibition of the Shroud of Turin is recorded.

1357.title: Charles Bridge
1357.body: Construction formally begins on Charles Bridge in Prague.

1357.title: Estates General
1357.body: The Estates General in France meets and passes Étienne Marcel's Great Ordinance in an attempt to impose limits on the monarchy, in particular in fiscal and monetary matters.

1357.title: Influenza
1357.body: Influenza is first identified as a disease.

1357.title: Peter I of Portugal
1357.body: Peter I becomes King of Portugal, after the death of his father, Alfonso IV.

1358.title: Marinid dynasty
1358.body: Muhammad II as Said becomes ruler of the Marinid dynasty in modern-day Morocco after the assassination of Abu Inan Faris.

1358.title: Haakon VI of Norway
1358.body: King Haakon VI of Norway designates the city of Skien as a city with trading privileges, making it the sixth town with city status in Norway.

1358.title: Hundred Years' War
1358.body: Hundred Years' War: The Jacquerie – A peasant rebellion begins in France, which consumes the Beauvais, and allies with Étienne Marcel's seizure of Paris.

1358.title: Treaty of Zadar
1358.body: Treaty of Zadar, between Louis I of Hungary/Croatia and the Republic of Venice: The Venetians lose influence over their former Dalmatian holdings.

1358.title: Republic of Ragusa
1358.body: The Republic of Ragusa is founded, after being freed from the Republic of Venice.

1359.title: Ismail II of Granada
1359.body: Ismail II overthrows his uncle, Muhammed V, as Sultan of Granada (in modern-day Spain).

1359.title: Marinid dynasty
1359.body: Abu Salim Ali II overthrows Muhammad II as-Said as ruler of the Marinid dynasty, in modern-day Morocco.

1359.title: Eric XII of Sweden
1359.body: Upon the death of Erik Magnusson, his claims to the Swedish throne die with him, and power is restored undivided to his father, King Magnus. With this unexpected death of Erik Magnusson, a previous promise to give Helsingborg to Denmark was reneged on by Magnus IV Eriksson. But Valdemar IV Atterdag was far too ambitious a ruler to have his plan to reassemble the Danish kingdom fall into pieces, and so he proceeded to invade Scania the next year with his mercenary army.

1359.title: Zayyanid dynasty
1359.body: The Zayanids under Abu Hamuw II recapture Kingdom of Tlemcen in Algeria.

1360.title: Kırkpınar
1360.body: Earliest known Kırkpınar oil wrestling tournament in the Ottoman Empire, which will still be staged into the 21st century.

1360.title: Muhammad VI of Granada
1360.body: Muhammed VI overthrows his brother-in-law, Ismail II, as King of Granada (in modern-day Spain).

1360.title: Valdemar IV Atterdag
1360.body: King Valdemar IV Atterdag of Denmark reconquers Scania, which has been in Swedish possession since 1332.

1360.title: Treaty of Brétigny
1360.body: The Treaty of Brétigny is ratified at Calais, marking the end of the first phase of the Hundred Years' War. Under its terms, Edward III of England gives up his claim to the French throne, and releases King John II of France in return for French land, including Calais and Gascony.

1360.title: Red Turban Rebellions
1360.body: Red Turban Rebellions: Chen Youliang murders Xu Shouhui and proclaims himself the emperor of Han in Wuchang before unsuccessfully attempting to capture Nanjing from Zhu Yuanzhang.

1361.title: Marinid
1361.body: In the Marinid Empire in modern-day Morocco, Abu Salim Ibrahim is overthrown by Abu Umar, who is in turn overthrown by Abu Zayyan.

1361.title: Edward the Black Prince
1361.body: Edward the Black Prince marries Joan of Kent at Windsor Castle.

1361.title: Goryeo
1361.body: Chinese rebels capture the Goryeo capital.

1361.title: An-Nasir Hasan
1361.body: An-Nasir Hasan, Mamluk Sultan of Egypt, is killed by one of his own mamluks, Yalbugha al-Umari, who, with the senior Mamluk emirs, has al-Mansur Muhammad installed as the new sultan.

1361.title: Battle of Visby
1361.body: Battle of Visby: King Valdemar IV of Denmark conquers the city of Visby by defeating the defending Gutnish country yeomen, and takes Gotland.

1362.title: Murad
1362.body: March – Murad I succeeds his father Orhan as sultan of the Ottoman Empire.

1362.title: Edward III of England
1362.body: The Chancellor of England for the first time opens Parliament with a speech in English. Under Edward III of England, the Pleading in English Act makes English rather than Law French the official language in law courts.

1362.title: Grote Mandrenke
1362.body: The "Grote Mandrenke" storm tide strikes the Netherlands, England, Germany and Denmark, destroying the Danish settlement of Rungholt in the Duchy of Schleswig, and the Humber estuary port of Ravenser Odd in England. The East Frisian island of Buise is broken into two by North Sea floods.

1362.title: Haakon VI of Norway
1362.body: King Haakon VI of Norway, son of Magnus IV of Sweden, proclaims himself king of Sweden in opposition to his father. However, later in the year, father and son are reconciled and rule Sweden together.

1362.title: Ottoman Turks
1362.body: The Ottomans capture Philippopolis (or in 1364, 1371) and Adrianopole (the modern-day city of Edirne, or in 1369) from the Byzantine Empire, reducing its territory to the city of Constantinople, part of the Peloponessus, and some islands.

1363.title: Al-Afdal al-Abbas
1363.body: Al-Afdal al-Abbas succeeds Al-Mujahid Ali as Rasulid Sultan of Yemen.

1363.title: Mosque-Madrassa of Sultan Hassan
1363.body: The Mosque-Madrassa of Sultan Hassan is completed in Cairo, Egypt.

1363.title: Revolt of Saint Titus
1363.body: August – The Revolt of Saint Titus, against the rule of the Republic of Venice in the Kingdom of Candia (island of Crete), begins.

1363.title: Battle of Lake Poyang
1363.body: Battle of Lake Poyang: The Dahan rebel forces of Chen Youliang are defeated by the Red Turban Rebel forces of Zhu Yuanzhang, during the final decade of Yuan dynasty control over China. Zhu's naval forces of 200,000 are pitted against Chen's naval forces of 650,000 troops, in what is not only the largest naval battle of the medieval age, but also one of the largest naval battles in history.

1363.title: Ottoman Turks
1363.body: Ottoman Turks seize Filibe (Philippopolis) in Thrace in the Byzantine–Ottoman wars.

1364.title: Magnus IV of Sweden
1364.body: Joint kings Magnus Eriksson and Haakon Magnusson of Sweden are both deposed by noblemen, who instead elect Magnus's nephew Albrekt of Mecklenburg the new king of Sweden.

1364.title: Reddy
1364.body: Anavema Reddy succeeds Anavota Reddy, as ruler of the Reddy Dynasty in Andhra Pradesh (part of modern-day southern India).

1364.title: Charles V of France
1364.body: Charles V becomes King of France.

1364.title: Jagiellonian University
1364.body: The Jagiellonian University is founded in Kraków.

1364.title: Vladislav I of Wallachia
1364.body: Vladislav I (also known as Vlaicu-Vodă) becomes voivode of Wallachia.

1365.title: Muhammad V of Granada
1365.body: Muhammed V begins building the Maristan of Granada (a hospital) in Granada (in modern-day Spain).

1365.title: October – Alexandrian Crusade
1365.body: October – Alexandrian Crusade: The city of Alexandria in Egypt is sacked by an allied force of Peter I of Cyprus and the knights of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem.

1365.title: Sukhothai Kingdom
1365.body: The Sukhothai Kingdom in northern Thailand becomes a tributary state of the Ayutthaya Kingdom.

1365.title: Battle of Gataskogen
1365.body: Battle of Gataskogen: Albert of Mecklenburg defeats and captures Magnus Eriksson, obtaining the throne of Sweden.

1365.title: Bahmani Sultan
1365.body: In modern-day southern India, Bahmani Sultan Mohammed Shah I invades the Vijayanagara Empire.

1366.title: Abu Faris Abd al-Aziz I of Morocco
1366.body: Abu Faris Abd al-Aziz I of Morocco succeeds assassinated Abu Zayyan as Sultan of the Marinid Empire in Morocco.

1366.title: Hongwu Emperor
1366.body: Zhu Yuanzhang, leader of the Red Turban Rebellion that will overthrow the Yuan dynasty and establish the Ming dynasty two years later, begins building the walls for a new capital city at Nanjing.

1366.title: 55P/Tempel–Tuttle
1366.body: Comet 55P/Tempel–Tuttle passes 0.0229 AU (3,430,000 km; 2,130,000 mi) from Earth.

1366.title: Henry II of Castile
1366.body: Henry II deposes his half-brother, Pedro of Castile, to become King of Castile.

1366.title: Brouwerij Artois
1366.body: The Den Hoorn brewery is founded at Leuven in the Low Countries. In 1717 this will be renamed the Brouwerij Artois, and later releases a beer in 1926 named Stella Artois.

1367.title: University
1367.body: The first university in Pécs, Hungary, is founded by King Louis I.

1367.title: Battle of Nájera
1367.body: Battle of Nájera: Pedro of Castile is restored as King of Castile (in modern-day Spain) after defeating his half-brother, Henry II. Pedro is aided in the battle by the English under Edward the Black Prince, and Henry by the French.

1367.title: Red Turban Rebellions
1367.body: Red Turban Rebellions: Zhu Yuanzhang takes Suzhou from Zhang Shicheng, who unsuccessfully attempts suicide before being captured and taken to Nanjing, where he dies.

1367.title: Pope Urban
1367.body: Pope Urban V makes the first attempt to move the Papacy back to Rome from Avignon. This move is reversed in 1370, when he is forced to return to Avignon, and shortly afterwards dies.

1367.title: Moscow Kremlin Wall
1367.body: Winter – Construction of a stone Moscow Kremlin Wall around the city is begun to resist invasion by the Grand Duchy of Lithuania.

1368.title: Maha Thammaracha
1368.body: Maha Thammaracha II becomes ruler of the Sukhothai Kingdom (in modern-day northern Thailand) after the death of Maha Thammaracha I.

1368.title: Norway
1368.body: The King of Norway sends the last Royal Ship from Norway to the Greenland Eastern Settlement. This event is part of both the Norse colonization of the Americas, and of the History of Greenland.

1368.title: Timur
1368.body: Timur ascends the throne of Samarkand (in modern-day Uzbekistan).

1368.title: Moscow
1368.body: Moscow attacks Tver, which counter-attacks with the aid of Lithuania and the Blue Horde.

1368.title: Hongwu Emperor
1368.body: The Hongwu Emperor (Zhu Yuanzhang) establishes the Ming dynasty in China, after the disintegration of the Mongol Yuan dynasty. The new dynasty will last until 1644. The Emperor immediately orders every county magistrate to set up four granaries, and halts government taxation on books.

1369.title: Thailand
1369.body: The Thai Ayutthaya Kingdom conquers Cambodia for a second time.

1369.title: Vladislav I of Wallachia
1369.body: February – Vladislav I of Wallachia liberates Vidin from the Hungarians, resulting in the restoration of Ivan Sratsimir on the throne of Bulgaria, in the autumn.

1369.title: Hugues Aubriot
1369.body: Charles V of France orders Hugues Aubriot to construct the fortress of the Bastille in Paris.

1369.title: Owain Lawgoch
1369.body: December – Financed by Charles V of France, Welshman Owain Lawgoch launches an invasion fleet against the English, in an attempt to claim the throne of Wales. A storm causes Owain to abandon the invasion.

1370.title: Trần Nghệ Tông
1370.body: Trần Nghệ Tông deposes Dương Nhật Lễ as emperor of Đại Việt, modern-day Vietnam.

1370.title: Casimir III the Great
1370.body: Casimir III the Great, king of Poland, dies as the result of a hunting accident, and is succeeded jointly by his sister, Elizabeth of Kujavia, and her son, Louis I of Hungary, beginning the rule of the country by the Capet-Anjou family.

1370.title: Treaty of Stralsund
1370.body: The Treaty of Stralsund ends the war between Denmark and the Hanseatic League.

1370.title: Hugues Aubriot
1370.body: Hugues Aubriot begins construction of the fortress of the Bastille in Paris.

1370.title: Pope Gregory
1370.body: Pope Gregory XI succeeds Pope Urban V, as the 201st pope.

1371.title: Byzantine
1371.body: Byzantine co-emperor John V Palaiologos pledges loyalty to the Ottoman Empire, to prevent the Turks from invading Constantinople.

1371.title: Playing cards
1371.body: The first widely accepted historical reference is made to playing cards (in Spain).

1371.title: Cambodia
1371.body: Kalamegha claims the vacant title of King of Cambodia after the power of the Thai invaders from Ayutthaya begins to weaken. The Ayutthayans are finally expelled in 1375.

1371.title: Lazar of Serbia
1371.body: December – Lazar succeeds his distant cousin, Stefan Uroš V, as ruler of Serbia, but declines the title of Tsar.

1371.title: Ivan Sratsimir of Bulgaria
1371.body: Rival brothers Ivan Sratsimir and Ivan Shishman become co-Emperors of Bulgaria after the death of their father, Ivan Alexander. Bulgaria is weakened by the split.

1372.title: Trần Duệ Tông
1372.body: Trần Duệ Tông succeeds his brother Trần Nghệ Tông as King of Vietnam.

1372.title: Owain Lawgoch
1372.body: May – Owain Lawgoch makes a second attempt to take the throne of Wales, sailing with French support from Harfleur. Whilst attacking the island of Guernsey, he abandons the invasion in order to fight for France at La Rochelle.

1372.title: Chūzan
1372.body: The Kingdom of Chūzan (in modern-day southern Japan) enters tributary relations with Ming dynasty China.

1372.title: Bridget of Sweden
1372.body: Saint Bridget sets out on a pilgrimage to the Holy Land, staying in Cyprus until May 12. In August, she is in Bethlehem where she sees a vision of Jesus' birth. In September, she sets sail for Naples, where she arrives in December.

1372.title: Battle of La Rochelle
1372.body: Battle of La Rochelle: The French and the Castilians defeat the English. The French gain control of the English Channel for the first time since 1340.

1373.title: Byzantine
1373.body: Byzantine co-emperor Andronikos IV Palaiologos rebels against his father, John V Palaiologos, for agreeing to let Constantinople become a vassal of the Ottoman Empire. After the rebellion fails, Ottoman Emperor Murad I commands John V Palaiologos to blind his son.

1373.title: Muhammad III ibn Abd al-Aziz
1373.body: The death of Sultan Muhammad III ibn Abd al-Aziz begins a period of political instability in Morocco.

1373.title: Philip II, Prince of Taranto
1373.body: November? – Philip II, Prince of Taranto hands over the rule of Achaea (modern-day southern Greece) to his cousin, Joanna I of Naples.

1373.title: Phnom Penh
1373.body: The city of Phnom Penh (modern-day capital city of Cambodia) is founded.

1373.title: Hundred Years' War
1373.body: Hundred Years' War: The French re-capture most of Brittany from the English, but are unable to take Brest.

1374.title: Shaikh Hasan Jalayir
1374.body: Shaikh Hasan Jalayir succeeds his father, Shaykh Uways Jalayir, as ruler of the Jalayirid Sultanate in modern-day Iraq and western Iran. Hasan proves to be an unpopular ruler and is executed on October 9 and succeeded by his brother, Shaikh Hussain Jalayir.

1374.title: James of Baux
1374.body: James of Baux succeeds his uncle, Philip II, as Prince of Taranto (modern-day eastern Italy) and titular ruler of the Latin Empire (northern Greece and western Turkey).

1374.title: Nasrid dynasty
1374.body: Princes from the Kingdom of Granada choose Abu al-Abbas Ahmad to succeed Muhammad as-Said, as Sultan of the Marinid Empire in Morocco. The Empire is split into the Kingdom of Fez and the Kingdom of Marrakesh.

1374.title: Musa
1374.body: Musa II succeeds his father, Mari Djata II, as Mansa of the Mali Empire (modern-day Mali and Senegal).

1374.title: Robert de Juilly
1374.body: Robert de Juilly succeeds Raymond Berenger as Grand Master of the Knights Hospitaller.

1375.title: Mamluks
1375.body: The Mamluks from Egypt complete their conquest of the Armenian Kingdom of Cilicia. Levon V Lusignan of Armenia is imprisoned for several years in Cairo, until a ransom is paid by King John I of Castile.

1375.title: Petru II of Moldavia
1375.body: Petru succeeds as Voivode (ruler) of Moldavia (modern-day Moldova & eastern Romania). He is the first ruler from the dynastic House of Bogdan.

1375.title: Margaret I of Denmark
1375.body: Margaret I of Denmark becomes Regent of Denmark after the death of her father, Valdemar IV.

1375.title: Heirin-ji
1375.body: Heirin-ji Temple is founded near Tokyo.

1375.title: Hundred Years' War
1375.body: Hundred Years' War: The English, weakened by the plague, lose so much ground to the French that they agree to sign the Treaty of Bruges, leaving them with only the coastal towns of Calais, Bordeaux and Bayonne.

1376.title: Republic of Genoa
1376.body: With the help of the Genoese, Byzantine co-emperor Andronicus IV Palaeologus invades Constantinople and dethrones his father, John V Palaeologus, as co-emperor. John V Palaeologus is taken prisoner.

1376.title: Catherine of Siena
1376.body: June – Catherine of Siena visits Pope Gregory XI in Avignon, to attempt to persuade him to make peace with Florence, and move the Papacy back to Rome.

1376.title: Edward the Black Prince
1376.body: The dying Prince Edward of Wales summons his father, Edward III, and brother, John of Gaunt, and makes them swear to uphold the claim to the throne of his son Richard; Edward is the first "English" Prince of Wales not to become King of England.

1376.title: Kingdom of England
1376.body: March – The peace treaty between England and France is extended until April, 1377.

1376.title: Richard II of England
1376.body: Richard of Bordeaux, son of the Black Prince, is created Prince of Wales in succession to his father.

1377.title: Pope Gregory
1377.body: Pope Gregory XI issues five Bulls condemning the opinion of John Wycliffe, that Catholic priests should live in poverty, like the twelve disciples of Jesus.

1377.title: January – Battle of Đồ Bàn
1377.body: January – Battle of Đồ Bàn: Trần Duệ Tông, Trần dynasty Emperor of Đại Việt (Vietnam), is killed.

1377.title: Sayf ad-Din Barquq
1377.body: Sayf ad-Din Barquq leads a revolt against the Mamluk Sultan of Egypt, Alah-ad-Din Ali.

1377.title: Radu I of Wallachia
1377.body: Radu I succeeds Vladislav I as Prince of Wallachia (modern-day southern Romania).

1377.title: Urus Khan
1377.body: Informed that Khan Urus of the White Horde has died, Timur of the Timurid Empire sends Tokhtamysh to take the Horde throne, but is defeated by Urus' son, Timur Malik.

1378.title: Uthman Beg
1378.body: Uthman Beg establishes the Aq Qoyunlu (Turkomans of the White Sheep) dynasty at Diyarbakır, in modern-day southeast Turkey.

1378.title: Fondi
1378.body: Unhappy with Pope Urban's critical attitude towards them, the majority of the cardinals meet at Fondi, elect Clement VII as antipope, and establish a rival papal court at Avignon. This split within the Catholic Church becomes known as the Western Schism, also known as the Great Schism. France, Aragon, Castile and León, Cyprus, Burgundy, Savoy, Naples and Scotland choose to recognise Antipope Clement VII.

1378.title: Mong Mao
1378.body: Tai Bian succeeds Zhao Bing Fa as King of Mong Mao (modern-day northern Myanmar).

1378.title: Ottoman Turks
1378.body: Ottoman Turks capture the town of Ihtiman in west Bulgaria.

1378.title: Balša
1378.body: Balša II succeeds his brother, Durađ I, as ruler of Lower Zeta (modern-day Montenegro).

1379.title: Kara Koyunlu
1379.body: Bairam Khawaja establishes the independent principality of the Kara Koyunlu (Turkomans of the Black Sheep Empire), in modern-day Armenia.

1379.title: Treaty of Neuberg
1379.body: The Treaty of Neuberg is signed, splitting the Austrian Habsburg lands between brothers Albert III and Leopold III. Albert III retains the title of Duke of Austria.

1379.title: Republic of Venice
1379.body: Forces of the Republic of Venice and Ottoman Turks, having invaded Constantinople, restore John V Palaiologos as Byzantine co-emperor. Andronikos IV Palaiologos is allowed to remain as co-emperor, but is confined to the city of Silivri for the remainder of his life.

1379.title: Dmitry Donskoy
1379.body: Dmitry Donskoy of Moscow raids Estonia.

1379.title: Hundred Years' War
1379.body: In the Hundred Years' War, the French lose control of most of Brittany to the English.

1380.title: Olaf II of Denmark
1380.body: February – Olaf II of Denmark also becomes Olaf IV of Norway, with his mother Margrete (Margaret) as regent. Iceland and the Faroe Islands, as parts of Norway, pass under the Danish crown.

1380.title: Battle of Kulikovo
1380.body: Battle of Kulikovo: Russian forces under Grand Prince Dmitry Donskoy of Moscow resist a large invasion by the Blue Horde, Lithuania and Ryazan, stopping their advance.

1380.title: Hongwu Emperor
1380.body: The Hongwu Emperor purges the chancellor of China, Hu Weiyong, and abolishes that office, as he imposes direct imperial rule over the six ministries of central government, for the Ming Empire.

1380.title: Tokhtamysh
1380.body: Khan Tokhtamysh of the White Horde dethrones Mamai of the Blue Horde. The two hordes unite to form the Golden Horde.

1380.title: Polynesia
1380.body: The last islands of Polynesia are discovered and inhabited.

1381.title: Mamluk Sultanate
1381.body: Hajji I succeeds Alah-ad-Din Ali as Mamluk Sultan of Egypt. The Egyptian government continues to be controlled by rebel leader Barquq.

1381.title: Kęstutis
1381.body: August – Kęstutis overthrows his nephew, Jogaila, as Grand Duke of Lithuania. Jogaila is allowed to remain as governor of eastern Lithuania. This marks the beginning of the Lithuanian Civil War (1381–84).

1381.title: Timur
1381.body: Timur conquers east Persia, ending the rule of the Sarbadar dynasty.

1381.title: Chioggia
1381.body: Chioggia concludes an alliance with Zadar and Trogir against Venice, which becomes changed in 1412 in Šibenik.

1381.title: Venice
1381.body: After a naval battle, Venice wins the three-year War of Chioggia against Genoa. The Genoans are permanently weakened by the conflict.

1382.title: James I of Cyprus
1382.body: October – James I succeeds his nephew, Peter II, as King of Cyprus.

1382.title: Black Madonna of Częstochowa
1382.body: August – The iconic painting the Black Madonna of Częstochowa is brought from Jerusalem, to the Jasna Góra Monastery in Poland.

1382.title: Ibrahim I of Shirvan
1382.body: Ibrahim I is selected to succeed Husheng, as Shah of Shirvan (now Azerbaijan).

1382.title: Balša
1382.body: Balša II of Zeta conquers Albania.

1382.title: Tokhtamysh
1382.body: Khan Tokhtamysh of the Golden Horde overruns Muscovy, as punishment for Grand Prince Dmitry Donskoy's resistance to Khan Mamai of the Blue Horde in the 1370s. Dmitry Donskoy pledges his loyalty to Tokhtamysh, and is allowed to remain as ruler of Moscow and Vladimir.

1383.title: James of Baux
1383.body: James of Baux, ruler of Taranto and Achaea, and last titular Latin Emperor, dies childless. As a result: Charles III of Naples becomes ruler of Achaea (modern-day southern Greece). Otto, Duke of Brunswick-Grubenhagen, the widower of Joanna I of Naples, becomes ruler of Taranto (eastern Italy).

1383.title: Louis I, Duke of Anjou
1383.body: Louis I, Duke of Anjou inherits the claim to the Latin Empire (western Turkey), but never uses the title of Emperor.

1383.title: Wat Phra That Doi Suthep
1383.body: The Wat Phra That Doi Suthep Temple is built in modern-day Thailand, by King Kuena of Lanna.

1383.title: Teutonic Knights
1383.body: The Teutonic Knights recommence war against pagan Lithuania.

1383.title: Bastille
1383.body: Construction of the Bastille fortress is completed in Paris, France.

1384.title: Kara Koyunlu
1384.body: Qara Muhammad succeeds Bairam Khawaja, as ruler of the Kara Koyunlu ("Black Sheep Turkomans"), in modern-day Armenia and northern Iraq.

1384.title: Jadwiga of Poland
1384.body: At the age of 10, Jadwiga is crowned "King" of Poland in Kraków following the 1382 death of her father, King Louis.

1384.title: Nasrid dynasty
1384.body: The Nasrid princes of Al-Andalus replace Abu al-Abbas with Abu Faris Musa ibn Faris, as ruler of the Marinid dynasty in modern-day Morocco.

1384.title: Timur
1384.body: Timur conquers the northern territories of the Jalayirid Empire, in western Persia.

1384.title: Antoniotto I Adorno
1384.body: Antoniotto I Adorno is elected as Doge of the Republic of Genoa following the death of the Doge Leonardo Montaldo from bubonic plague the day before. Adorno serves as the Republic's chief executive until 1390.

1385.title: Battle of Savra
1385.body: Battle of Savra: Serbian forces under Balša II and Ivaniš Mrnjavčević are defeated by Ottoman commander Hayreddin Pasha, near Berat.

1385.title: North Sea
1385.body: The two-day Battle of Sluys concludes on the North Sea off of the coast of the Netherlands as England's Royal Navy, led by the Admiral, Thomas Percy, 1st Earl of Worcester attempts to blockade the Flemish port of Sluis and more than 100 ships stationed there. Jean de Vienne, Admiral of France, makes a successful counter-attack and the Royal Navy ships sail back to England to defend London.

1385.title: List of rulers of Kano
1385.body: February – Bugaya becomes the new Sultan of Kano in what is now northern Nigeria, upon the death of his brother Yaji I.

1385.title: Olav IV of Norway
1385.body: Olav IV of Norway is elected as titular King of Sweden, in opposition to the unpopular King Albert.

1385.title: English invasion of Scotland (1385)
1385.body: English invasion of Scotland (1385): Preparing to lead invade Scotland as soon as the truce between England and Scotland expires on July 15, King Richard II of England assembles an army at Nottingham.

1386.title: Turco-Mongol
1386.body: The Mongol conqueror Timur, called "Tamerlane" in the west, captures and sacks the Georgian capital of Tbilisi, taking King Bagrat V prisoner.

1386.title: Marinid dynasty
1386.body: Abu al-Abbas is reinstated as ruler of the Marinid dynasty in modern-day Morocco.

1386.title: Dan I of Wallachia
1386.body: Dan I of Wallachia (modern-day southern Romania) is killed in battle against the Bulgarians and is succeeded by Mircea the Elder, one of the greatest rulers of Wallachia.

1386.title: Union of Krewo
1386.body: Union of Krewo: A delegation of Poland's nobles visits the Grand Duke Jogaila of Lithuania at Valkaviskas (now Vawkavysk in Belarus and informs him that they will elect him as the new King of Poland on the condition that he convert from Lithuania's polytheistic religion to Christianity, and that he marry the Queen of Jadwiga of Poland. Jogaila agrees to the terms.

1386.title: Brancacci Chapel
1386.body: Construction begins on the Brancacci Chapel in Florence.

1387.title: Elizabeth of Bosnia
1387.body: Elizabeta Kotromanic, the regent of Hungary for her daughter, Queen Mary, is murdered in prison by Croatian rebels.

1387.title: Timur
1387.body: Timur conquers the Muzaffarid Empire in central Persia, and appoints three puppet rulers.

1387.title: Hongwu Emperor
1387.body: November – The Emperor Zhu Yuanzhang of China directs General Lan Yu to attack the Mongol forces led by Tögüs Temür, whom the Emperor Zhu had deposed. The confrontation between Lan Yu and Tögüs Temür will culminate six months later at the Battle of Buir Lake, 500 miles (800 km) north of Beijing.

1387.title: Tokhtamysh
1387.body: Khan Tokhtamysh of the Golden Horde invades the Timurid Empire, but has to withdraw soon after, due to heavy snow.

1387.title: John Holland, 1st Duke of Exeter
1387.body: John Holland, a maternal half-brother of Richard II of England, is created Earl of Huntingdon.

1388.title: Battle of Bileća
1388.body: Battle of Bileća: The Bosnians check the Ottoman advance.

1388.title: Eleanor of Arborea
1388.body: Eleanor of Arborea, regent for her son Marianus V of the independent Judicate of Arborea, on the island of Sardinia, signs a peace agreement with the Crown of Aragon in Spain, ending a war between the two nations.

1388.title: Ramesuan
1388.body: Ramesuan is reinstated as King of Ayutthaya (modern-day southern Thailand), after dethroning and executing 17-year-old King Thong Lan.

1388.title: Kanem Empire
1388.body: Omar I is succeeded by Sa'id, as King of the Kanem-Bornu Empire (modern-day east Chad and Nigeria). Sa'id is succeeded in the same year by Kade Alunu. Omar and Sa'id are both killed by Bilala invaders from the west.

1388.title: Ljubostinja
1388.body: Ljubostinja Monastery is built in Serbia.

1389.title: Battle of Kosovo
1389.body: At the Battle of Kosovo, The Ottoman Empire and the Serbs fight an inconclusive clash, with both sides suffering heavy losses. Both Sultan Murad I and Serbian Prince Lazar Hrebeljanovic are killed in the battle.

1389.title: Mamluk Sultanate
1389.body: Hadji II is restored as Mamluk Sultan of Egypt, after overthrowing Sultan Barquq.

1389.title: Kanem Empire
1389.body: Biri II succeeds Kade Alunu as King of the Kanem-Bornu Empire (now eastern Chad and Nigeria), and the Empire loses its land in present-day Chad to the Bilala.

1389.title: Ziyanid
1389.body: Abd ar-Rahmân II succeeds Musa II as ruler of the Ziyanid Dynasty, in present-day western Algeria.

1389.title: Abu Hammu
1389.body: Abu Tashufin II succeeds his nephew, Abu Hammu II, as ruler of the Abdalwadid Dynasty in present-day eastern Algeria.

1390.title: Manuel III of Trebizond
1390.body: Manuel III succeeds his father, Alexios III, as Emperor of Trebizond (now north eastern Turkey).

1390.title: Fall of Philadelphia
1390.body: Fall of Philadelphia– The Ottomans take Philadelphia, the last Byzantine enclave of any significance in Anatolia.

1390.title: Candi Surawana
1390.body: The Candi Surawana Temple is built in the Majapahit Kingdom (now Indonesia).

1390.title: Che Bong Nga
1390.body: Ko Cheng succeeds Che Bong Nga, as King of Champa (now eastern Vietnam).

1390.title: Barquq
1390.body: Barquq is restored as Mamluk Sultan of Egypt, after overthrowing Sultan Hadji II.

1391.title: Manuel II Palaiologos
1391.body: Manuel II Palaiologos becomes Byzantine emperor after his father, John V Palaiologos, dies from a nervous breakdown, due to his continued humiliation by the Ottoman Empire.

1391.title: Al-Nasir Muhammad Salah al-Din
1391.body: Al-Nasir Muhammad Salah al-Din, Imam of the Zaydi sect of Shia Islam in Yemen, dies at San'a from injuries sustained from being thrown off of his mule. Nasir's death is not announced for two months while his son Al-Mansur Ali bin Salah ad-Din becomes the new Imam but three other claimants attempt to claim the office as well.

1391.title: Suceava
1391.body: December – At Suceava, Roman I succeeds Petru Mușat, as Prince of Moldavia (now Moldova and northeastern Romania).

1391.title: Vytautas the Great
1391.body: Vytautas the Great, claimant to the throne of Lithuania, forges an alliance with the Grand Duchy of Moscow as his daughter, Sophia marries Vasily, Grand Prince of Muscovy.

1391.title: Henry I Sinclair, Earl of Orkney
1391.body: Henry I Sinclair, Earl of Orkney, takes control of the Shetland Islands and the Faroe Islands.

1392.title: Afyonkarahisar
1392.body: The city of Afyonkarahisar (in modern-day western Turkey) is conquered by Sultan Beyazid I, of the Ottoman Empire.

1392.title: Sanjak
1392.body: The semi-independent Serbian state (sanjak) of Uskup is annexed by the Ottoman Empire and General Pasha Yiğit Bey becomes the first Ottoman governor of the new province.

1392.title: Fleet Street riot
1392.body: After the Fleet Street riot in London, King Richard II of England summons Lord Mayor of London, John Hende, along with the London sheriff and deputies, and aldermen to appear before him at Nottingham Castle on June 25 for a hearing on unspecified charges.

1392.title: Jogaila
1392.body: King Jogaila of Poland and Lithuania appoints his cousin Vytautas the Great as regent of Lithuania, in return for Vytautas giving up his claim to the Lithuanian throne. Vytautas replaces Jogaila's unpopular brother Skirgaila as regent.

1392.title: Kings of East Breifne
1392.body: Seoan mac Pilib succeeds Tomas mor mac Mathghamhna as King of East Breifne, in north-central Ireland.

1393.title: Bosnia
1393.body: Bosnia resists an invasion by the Ottoman Empire.

1393.title: Ottoman Empire
1393.body: The Ottoman Turks capture Turnovgrad (now Veliko Tarnovo), the capital city of east Bulgaria. Emperor Ivan Shishman is allowed to remain as puppet ruler of east Bulgaria.

1393.title: Byzantium
1393.body: Byzantium loses Thessaly to the growing Ottoman Empire.

1393.title: Vytautas the Great
1393.body: Vytautas the Great, Regent of the Duchy of Lithuania for Wladyslaw II Jagiello, King of Poland, begins a peace conference with the Teutonic Knights, convening in Thorn in the Holy Roman Empire (now Toruń in Poland)

1393.title: James I of Cyprus
1393.body: King James I of Cyprus inherits the title of King of Armenia, after the death of his distant cousin Leo VI (although the Mamluk conquerors from Egypt remain the true rulers).

1394.title: Battle of Karanovasa
1394.body: At the Battle of Karanovasa, Wallachia (now southern Romania) resists an invasion by the army Ottoman Empire and its Serb and Bulgarian vassals.

1394.title: Ottoman Empire
1394.body: The Ottomans conquer Thessaly (now eastern Greece) and begin an eight-year siege of Constantinople, in the Byzantine Empire. In the same year, they begin building the Anadoluhisarı fortress to defend themselves during the siege.

1394.title: Stephen I of Moldavia
1394.body: Ştefan I succeeds Roman I, as Prince of Moldavia (now Moldova and eastern Romania).

1394.title: Tunis
1394.body: At Tunis, Abu Faris Abd al-Aziz al-Mutawakkil becomes the new of Caliph of the Hafsid Sultanate of Ifriqiya in North Africa (now part of Algeria, Tunisia and Libya after the death of his father Abu al-Abbas Ahmad II,

1394.title: Abdalwadid
1394.body: Abu Zayyan II succeeds his brother, Abul Hadjdjadj I, as ruler of the Abdalwadid dynasty in present-day eastern Algeria.

1395.title: Bayezid
1395.body: Sultan Bayezid I of the Ottoman Empire has the former Bulgarian Emperor Ivan Shishman beheaded after Shishman is accused of collaborating with the Principality of Wallachia during the 1394 Battle of Karanovasa.

1395.title: Stjepan Dabiša
1395.body: The death of King Stjepan Dabiša leads to the election of his wife Jelena Gruba as Queen of Bosnia. However, most of the Bosnian land is soon appropriated by King Sigismund of Hungary.

1395.title: University of Óbuda
1395.body: The University of Óbuda, the second in Hungary and the first in the capital, Budapest, is given a deed of foundation by Pope Boniface IX after the requests of King Sigismund of Hungary.

1395.title: John Rykener
1395.body: John Rykener, also known as Johannes Richer and Eleanor, a transvestite prostitute working mainly in London (near Cheapside), but also active in Oxford, is arrested for cross-dressing and interrogated on December 11, 1394. The records have survived, the only surviving legal records from this age which mention same-sex intercourse.

1395.title: Ramaracha
1395.body: Ramaracha succeeds Ramesuan as ruler of the Ayutthaya Kingdom in present-day southern Thailand.

1396.title: Timur
1396.body: Timur appoints his son Miran Shah, as Timurid viceroy of present-day Azerbaijan.

1396.title: Marinid dynasty
1396.body: Abu Amir succeeds Abdul Aziz II as ruler of the Marinid dynasty, in modern-day Morocco.

1396.title: Knights Hospitaler
1396.body: The Crusaders, with more than 17,000 troops from France, Hungary, the Knights Hospitaler, Wallachia and the Holy Roman Empire , arrive at the outskirts of Nicopolis (now Nikopol in Bulgaria).

1396.title: Battle of Nicopolis
1396.body: Battle of Nicopolis: The Ottomans defeat a joint crusade by Hungary, France, the Holy Roman Empire, and Wallachia, led by King Sigismund of Hungary. This is the last large-scale crusade of the Middle Ages.

1396.title: Margaret I of Denmark
1396.body: Queen Margaret I of Denmark, Norway and Sweden publishes the Treaty of Kalmar, proposing the personal union of the three kingdoms of Denmark, Norway (with Iceland, Greenland, the Faroe Islands, Shetland and Orkney) and Sweden (including Finland and Åland).

1397.title: Malta
1397.body: The Università, a form of local government, is established in Malta.

1397.title: Holy Roman Empire
1397.body: As part of a division of the state of Holstein, the northernmost member of the Holy Roman Empire, between Count Albert II and his brother Count Gerhard VI (following the death of their uncle, Nicholas, Gerhard receives the Duchy of Schleswig and nearly all of Rendsburg, while Albert receives HOlstein-Segeberg.

1397.title: de facto
1397.body: Eric of Pomerania is crowned in Kalmar (Sweden) as ruler of the Kalmar Union, a personal union of the three kingdoms of Denmark, Norway (with Iceland, Greenland, the Faroe Islands, Shetland and Orkney) and Sweden (including Finland and Åland) engineered by Queen Margaret I of Denmark, his great-aunt and adoptive mother, who retains de facto power in the realm.

1397.title: Ottoman Turks
1397.body: The Ottomans capture the town of Vidin, the capital of the Tsardom of Vidin, the only remaining independent Bulgarian state. Emperor Ivan Sratsimir of Vidin is taken prisoner by early this year and later disappears while his son Constantine II becomes Emperor in his place.

1397.title: Callistus II of Constantinople
1397.body: Callistus II Xanthopoulos becomes the new Patriarch to lead the Eastern Orthodox Church after the death of the previous Patriarch Antony IV.

1398.title: Turkey
1398.body: In what is now central Turkey, Kadi Burhan al-Din, the vizier of and regent for the Sultanate of Eretnia, on a campaign to conquer the Emirate of Erzincan (eastern Turkey and western Armenia) and capture its Emir Mutahharten, is killed in battle by Qara Yuluk Uthman Beg.

1398.title: Janus of Cyprus
1398.body: Janus succeeds his father, James I, as King of Cyprus and claimant to the throne of Armenian Cilicia.

1398.title: Trần Thuận Tông
1398.body: Trần Thuận Tông is forced to abdicate as ruler of the Trần dynasty in modern-day Vietnam, in favour of his three-year-old son Trần Thiếu Đế.

1398.title: Dao Ganmeng rebellion
1398.body: January – The Dao Ganmeng rebellion in the Chinese vassal state of Möng Mao,started in December by Dao Genmeng, is brought to an end in what is now Myanmar two months after Dao Ganmeng's takeover. With the intervention of the Chinese Empire, led by the Ming dynasty Emperor Zhu Yuanzhang, the deposed ruler Si Lunfa is restored to leadership.

1398.title: Glendalough
1398.body: Glendalough monastery in Wicklow, Ireland is destroyed by English troops.

1399.title: Bayezid
1399.body: Sultan Bayezid I of the Ottoman Empire invades Mamluk-occupied Syria. A rift forms between Sultan Bayezid and Timur of the Timurid Empire, who also wanted to conquer Syria.

1399.title: Principality of Achaea
1399.body: The Principality of Achaea (now southern Greece) resists an invasion by the Ottoman Empire.

1399.title: Richard II of England
1399.body: While Richard II of England is away on a military campaign in Ireland, Henry Bolingbroke, with exiled former archbishop of Canterbury Thomas Arundel as an advisor, returns to England and begins a military campaign to reclaim his confiscated lands.

1399.title: Battle of the Vorskla River
1399.body: At the Battle of the Vorskla River, Golden Horde forces, led by Mongol Khan Temür Qutlugh and Emir Edigu, annihilate a crusading army led by former Golden Horde Khan Tokhtamysh, and Grand Duke Vytautas of Lithuania..

1399.title: Martin of Aragon
1399.body: The coronation of Martin of Aragon as King takes place in Barcelona. His wife Maria de Luna is crowned as the Queen consort takes place on April 23.

1400.title: Timur
1400.body: Timur conquers the Empire of The Black Sheep Turkomans, in present-day Azerbaijan, and the Jalayirid dynasty in present-day Iraq. Black Sheep ruler Qara Yusuf and Jalayirid Sultan Ahmad flee, and take refuge with the Ottoman Sultan Bayezid I.

1400.title: Trần Thiếu Đế
1400.body: Five-year-old Trần Thiếu Đế is forced to abdicate as ruler of Đại Việt (modern-day Vietnam), in favour of his maternal grandfather and court official Hồ Quý Ly, ending the Trần dynasty after 175 years and starting the Hồ dynasty. Hồ Quý Ly subsequently changes the country's name to Đại Ngu.

1400.title: Parameswara
1400.body: Prince Parameswara establishes the Malacca Sultanate, in present-day western Malaysia and northern Sumatra.

1400.title: Burmese calendar
1400.body: (9th waxing of Nadaw, 730 ME) Minkhaung I becomes the new King of Ava, the largest kingdom in what is now northern Myanmar, after a battle for power that follows the assassination of the erratic King Tarabya.

1400.title: Frankfurt
1400.body: Meeting in Frankfurt, three of the prince-electors of the Holy Roman Empire (Rupert, elector of the Palatinate, Rudolf III, Duke of Saxe-Wittenberg, elector of Saxony, and Jobst of Moravia, elector of Brandenburg) meet in an attempt to replace the Emperor, Wenceslaus, King of the Romans because of his failure to stamp out civil unrest or to resolve the Western Schism. They select Frederick I, Duke of Brunswick-Lüneburg as the replacement for Wenceslaus.

1401.title: Conwy Castle
1401.body: Conwy Castle in English-ruled North Wales, is captured on Good Friday by trickery by two Welsh brothers, Rhys ap Tudur and Gwilym ap Tudur of the Island of Anglesey, in support of the anti-English rebellion led by Owain Glyndŵr.

1401.title: Hồ Quý
1401.body: Emperor Hồ Quý Ly of Dai Ngu (now Vietnam) passes the throne to his son, Hồ Hán Thương.

1401.title: Vytautas
1401.body: The Samogitians, supported by Grand Duke Vytautas of Lithuania, rebel against the Teutonic knights and burn two castles. Vytautas is granted increased autonomy by King Jogaila of the Poland–Lithuania union.

1401.title: Battle of Tuthill
1401.body: The Battle of Tuthill takes place at Caernarfon in Wales as Owain Glyndŵr loses 300 soldiers in an attack by the English Army.

1401.title: Royal assent
1401.body: As the English Parliament session closes, King Henry IV gives royal assent to the Suppression of Heresy Act, permitting secular authorities to carry out punishment for religious crimes. The assent comes after the Archbishop of Canterbury pressures King Henry to outlaw the Lollards, followers of John Wycliffe, and criminalizes possession of a copy of Wycliffe's translation of the Bible.

1402.title: Aq Qoyunlu
1402.body: The Aq Qoyunlu ("White Sheep Turkmen") tribal confederation, in modern-day northern Iraq and Iran, moves its capital from Amida to Diyarbakır.

1402.title: Battle of Ankara
1402.body: Battle of Ankara: An invading Timurid Empire force defeats the Ottoman Sultan Bayezid I, who is captured. A period of interregnum begins in the Ottoman Empire, with the future Mehmed I as one of the leading claimants to the throne. After Serbia is freed from Ottoman rule, Stefan Lazarević is crowned Despot of Serbia.

1402.title: Henry III of Castile
1402.body: King Enrique III of Castile and León (comprising most of Spain) signs a peace treaty with his brother-in-law, King João I of Portugal, ending fighting that began in 1396. The wives of the two kings, Catherine of Lancaster and Philippa of Lancaster respectively, are sisters.

1402.title: Henry IV of England
1402.body: King Henry IV of England is married by proxy to Joan of Navarre in a ceremony at the Eltham Palace near London.

1402.title: Jogaila
1402.body: King Jogaila of the Poland–Lithuania Union answers the rumblings against his rule of Poland, by marrying Anna of Celje, a granddaughter of Casimir III of Poland.

1403.title: Jean II Le Maingre
1403.body: Jean II Le Maingre of France, also known as Boucicaut, the French Governor of Genoa, leads a fleet of 18 ships, 600 horses and 700 infantry to stop an attack by Muslims on the island of Cyprus and the city of Famagusta. Boucicaut besieges the Muslim city of Candelore on June 24.

1403.title: Eleanor of Castile, Queen of Navarre
1403.body: The coronation of Eleanor of Castile, wife of King Carlos III of Navarre, as Queen of Navarre takes place in Pamplona, now in Spain.

1403.title: Myanmar
1403.body: In what is now Myanmar, peace negotiations begin between King Razadarit of Hanthawaddy at his capital Pegu, with the emissaries of Minkhaung I, ruler of the Kingdom of Ava, 10 days after the Ava forces defeated Razadarit's army at the Battle of Nawin.

1403.title: Quarantine
1403.body: The world's first quarantine station, the Lazzaretto Vecchio, is built in Venice, to protect against the Black Death.

1403.title: Treaty of Gallipoli
1403.body: Signing of the Treaty of Gallipoli is completed as Süleyman Çelebi makes wide-ranging concessions to the Byzantine Empire and other Christian powers, in the southern Balkans.

1404.title: Regreg War
1404.body: A civil war, lasting two years, breaks out in the Majapahit Empire in modern-day Indonesia.

1404.title: Kings of Magh Luirg
1404.body: Ruaidri Caech MacDermot succeeds Conchobair Óg MacDermot as King of Magh Luirg, in what later becomes northeast Connacht, Ireland.

1404.title: Centurione II Zaccaria
1404.body: Centurione II Zaccaria buys the Principality of Achaea, located on the Peloponnese peninsula in modern-day Greece, from King Ladislaus of Naples.

1404.title: Bukka Raya
1404.body: In Southern India, Bukka Raya II becomes the new ruler of the Vijayanagara Empire in what are the modern-day Indian states of Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu and Kerala.

1404.title: Pope Boniface
1404.body: Cosimo de' Migliorati, Cardinal of the Basilica Cross in Jerusalem, is elected unanimously by eight cardinals to succeed the late Pope Boniface IX. Migliorati takes the papal name Innocent VII as the 204th pope of the Roman Catholic Church.

1405.title: Sultanate of Sulu
1405.body: The Sultanate of Sulu is established on the Sulu Archipelago, off the coast of Mindanao in the Philippines.

1405.title: Lan
1405.body: The Imperial Chinese Army invades the Lan Na kingdom, covering an area now divided between Myanmar, Thailand and China's Yunnan province.

1405.title: Gur-e-Amir
1405.body: News reaches the Timurid Empire that Timur has died, and a period of mourning begins as he is interred at the Gur-e-Amir mausoleum in Samarkand, now in the Republic of Uzbekistan.

1405.title: Battle of Pwll Melyn
1405.body: At the Battle of Pwll Melyn, fought in Wales, Baron Grey of Codnor routs the Welsh rebels commanded by two sons and the brother of the rebel leader Owain Glyndwr. Owain's eldest son, Gruffud, is taken as a prisoner of war by the English, while the brother, Tudur ap Gruffudd, is killed in action.

1405.title: Khalil Sultan
1405.body: Khalil Sultan becomes the new ruler of the western side of the Timurid Empire upon the death of his grandfather, the Mongol conqueror Timur, while the son of Timur, Shah Rukh, becomes the ruler of the eastern side.

1406.title: James I of Scotland
1406.body: James I becomes King of Scotland on the death of his father. He has been detained by Henry IV of England since March 30 and will remain at the English court for 18 years. With James absent, Robert Stewart, Duke of Albany administers the kingdom as "Governor of Scotland".

1406.title: Vietnam
1406.body: Tran Thien Binh, pretender to the throne of Vietnam (at the time, Dai Ngu) arrives from China, accompanied by an emissary from China, at the invitation of the Emperor Hồ Hán Thương. After crossing into Lang Son, Tran Thien Binh and the Chinese ambassador are killed by King Ho Han Thuong's troops, triggering a war with China.

1406.title: Appenzell
1406.body: At Appenzell in what is now Switzerland, a group of nobles create the "Order of St. George's Shield" at Hegau in the Duchy of Swabia.

1406.title: Al-Musta'in
1406.body: Abū al-Faḍl Al-Musta'in becomes the new Abbasid caliph of Cairo, spiritual leader of the Muslim faith in the Mamluk Sultanate of Egypt, succeeding his father, Al-Mutawakkil I.

1406.title: Piae Postulatio
1406.body: Pope Innocent VII issues the bull Piae Postulatio to protect the charity and hospice of Santa Maria dell'Anima (Saint Mary of the Soul), located in Rome and serving the German-speaking community.

1407.title: Pir Muhammad
1407.body: Pir Muhammad Mirza, co-ruler of the Timurid Empire (Transoxiana) that encompasses what is now Iran and most of the Near East of Asia, is murdered by his vizier, Pir Ali Taz. He is succeeded by his cousin, Khalil Sultan, who becomes the sole ruler of Transoxiana until being overthrown in 1409.

1407.title: Ming–Việt War
1407.body: Ming–Việt War: China conquers Dong Do, the eastern capital of Dai Ngu (now Hanoi, capital of Vietnam, and follows six days later by conquering the western capital, Tay Do (now Thanh Hóa) on January 26.

1407.title: Sino-Tibetan relations during the Ming dynasty
1407.body: After several invitations by the Emperor Cheng Zu of China, the lama Deshin Shekpa, the fifth Karmapa of the Karma Kagyu sect of Tibetan Buddhism, finally visits the Emperor at Nanjing, the Ming dynasty. In his 22-day visit, the Karmapa thrills the Ming court with alleged miracles that are recorded in a gigantic scroll, translated into five different languages. In a show of mystical prowess, Deshin Shekpa adds legitimacy to a questionable succession to the throne by Cheng Zu, who had killed his nephew the Jianwen Emperor in the culmination of a civil war.

1407.title: Nanjing
1407.body: A group of high officials of the former Vietnamese government are put on trial before China's Emperor Cheng Zu at Nanjing, and charged with treason for killing the previous Vietnamese ruler. Most of the prisoners are executed.

1407.title: Bank of Saint George
1407.body: The Banco di San Giorgio is founded by the government of the Republic of Genoa in Italy.

1408.title: Western Schism
1408.body: Western Schism: King Charles VI of France sends a letter to the Antipope Benedict XIII at Avignon, giving an ultimatum to Benedict and the other Avignon Papacy proponents to end the schism in the Roman Catholic Church against the Roman Obedience, headed by Pope Gregory XII. King Charles VI threatens that unless the schism ends by Ascension Day, 40 days after Easter (in 1408, April 24, with Ascension Day on June 3), France will end its support of the Avignon Obedience.

1408.title: Bund ob dem See
1408.body: Appenzell Wars: In the Appenzell region of Switzerland, the alliance of the region with St. Gallen, the Bund ob dem See "alliance over the lake", referring to the nearby Lake Constance fails in its attempt to liberate the city of Bregenz from Austrian rule. The Bund is dissolved less than three months later.

1408.title: Tagu
1408.body: March – (late in the month of Tagu, which ended March 28, 769 ME) Ava–Hanthawaddy War: In Burma (now Myanmar), King Razdarit leads the invasion of the Kingdom of North Arakan and overthrows its ruler, Anawrahta of Launggyet, then has him executed.

1408.title: Prince of Wales
1408.body: September 23 Henry, Prince of Wales (later Henry V of England) retakes Aberystwyth from Owain Glyndŵr. The Battle of Othée is fought between the citizens of Liège and a professional army under command of John the Fearless. The militia of Liège suffers a heavy defeat.

1409.title: Timurid War of Succession
1409.body: The Timurid War of Succession comes to an end as Khalil Sultan surrenders Samarkand to Shah Rukh, ruler of the Timurid Empire since 1405. Shah Rukh installs his son Ulugh Beg as Governor of Transoxiana. Shah Rukh then appoints Khalil as Governor of Shahr-e Rey in modern-day Iran.

1409.title: Galle Trilingual Inscription
1409.body: The Galle Trilingual Inscription, with inscriptions in three languages (Chinese, Tamil and Persian) is installed by the Chinese admiral Zheng He at Galle in Sri Lanka, where he has stopped while on his way home during the second of his treasure voyages.

1409.title: Cheng
1409.body: Cheng Ho (or Zheng He), admiral of the Ming empire fleet, deposes the king of Sri Lanka.

1409.title: Republic of Venice
1409.body: The Republic of Venice, led by the Doge Michele Steno, gains control of both sides of the Adriatic Sea by purchasing the Dalmatian coast (bordered in modern times by Slovenia and Croatia) from King Ladislaus the Magnanimous, ruler of the Kingdom of Naples for 100,000 ducats, or 350 kilograms of gold.

1409.title: Samogitian Uprising
1409.body: The second Samogitian Uprising begins in modern-day Lithuania against the Teutonic Knights who had been granted the rights to the Duchy of Samogita by the Peace of Raciążek in 1402.

1410.title: Antequera
1410.body: After a siege of almost four months, the Muslim city of Antequera, located in what is now Andalusia in Spain, surrenders to the Crown of Castile and its army, commanded by Prince Ferdinand of Aragon.

1410.title: Woerden
1410.body: Construction begins on Castle Woerden in the Netherlands.

1410.title: Prague Astronomical Clock
1410.body: The Prague Astronomical Clock (also known as Prague Orloj) is built by Mikuláš of Kadaň and Jan Šindel in Prague, the capital of the Czech Republic.

1410.title: Siege of Marienburg
1410.body: The Siege of Marienburg (now Malbork in Poland), capital of the State of the Teutonic Order of the Teutonic Knights, begins with an attack by Poland and Lithuania with an army of 26,000 men against less than 5,000 Teutons.

1410.title: Armagnac–Burgundian Civil War
1410.body: A temporary halt to the Armagnac–Burgundian Civil War in France is reached with the signing of a truce at Bicêtre, near Paris.

1411.title: Treaty of Selymbria
1411.body: The Treaty of Selymbria is concluded between the Ottoman Empire and the Republic of Venice.

1411.title: March 4 Friso-Hollandic Wars
1411.body: March 4 Friso-Hollandic Wars: Friesland soldiers capture the Netherlands city of Staveren, the last Netherlands stronghold in Friesland, after a bitter winter prevents ships from both sides from crossing the Zuiderzee and freezes the moat around the city walls. Gujarat Sultan Ahmen Shah I declares the new city of Ahmedabad to be the new Gujarat capital.

1411.title: Friso-Hollandic Wars
1411.body: Friso-Hollandic Wars: Friesland soldiers capture the Netherlands city of Staveren, the last Netherlands stronghold in Friesland, after a bitter winter prevents ships from both sides from crossing the Zuiderzee and freezes the moat around the city walls.

1411.title: 1410–1411 elections
1411.body: Sigismund is formally elected unanimously as King of the Romans by the seven electors of the Holy Roman Empire present.

1411.title: Henry IV of England
1411.body: Henry IV dismisses Prince Henry and his supporters from the government. The next day, the leader of the Armagnacs, the Duke of Orleans, finds that the gates to the walled city of Paris have been locked and are closely guarded.

1412.title: November 8 (5th waxing of Nadaw 774 ME) – Ava–Hanthawaddy War
1412.body: November 8 (5th waxing of Nadaw 774 ME) – Ava–Hanthawaddy War: In modern-day Myanmar, King Razadarit of Hanthawaddy leads the invasion of the city of Prome with 4,000 troops.

1412.title: Black Death
1412.body: The Black Death sweeps England for a final time, in a 60-year period.

1412.title: Henry IV of England
1412.body: King Henry IV of England issues a summons of the English Parliament for his final time, directing the members of Commons and the House of Lords to assemble on February 3.

1412.title: Gian Maria Visconti
1412.body: Gian Maria Visconti, the Duke of Milan since 1402, is assassinated in front of the church of San Gottardo in Milan by a party of Milanese Ghibellines on the orders of his terminally ill rival, Facino Cane. Visconti is stabbed in the leg and then in his head. Filippo Maria Visconti, Gian Maria's son, becomes the new Duke of Milan.

1412.title: Ming dynasty
1412.body: China's Emperor Cheng Zu orders the fourth of the Ming treasure voyages, to be led by Admiral Zheng He.

1413.title: First Scutari War
1413.body: The First Scutari War between the Kingdom of Zeta (now part of Albania and Montenegro) and the Venetian Republic comes to an end.

1413.title: Betrothal
1413.body: The betrothal ceremony of Prince Charles of France (age 10), son of King Charles VI, to Marie of Anjou (age 9), daughter of Louis, Duke of Anjou, takes place at the Palais du Louvre in Paris. The two will marry in 1422.

1413.title: Henry V of England
1413.body: King Henry IV of England dies at Westminster Abbey, where the English Parliament has been meeting, and passes on while in the Jerusalem Chamber of the house of the Abbot, William de Colchester.

1413.title: Ladislaus of Naples
1413.body: King Ladislaus of Naples and his troops conquer and sack the city of Rome and drive out the Antipope John XXIII.

1413.title: Henry IV of England
1413.body: The 10th and final English Parliament of King Henry IV opens its session, but closes abruptly after six weeks because of the death of the King.

1414.title: Oldcastle Revolt
1414.body: The Oldcastle Revolt, led by John Oldcastle as an uprising by the Lollards in England against King Henry V, begins at St. Giles' Fields. King Henry's troops, stationed at Clerkenwell Priory in London, halt the rebellion the next day and capture 80 rebels who are later convicted of and executed for treason.

1414.title: Poland
1414.body: In Poland, Casimir, Duke of Oświęcim takes full power as he reaches the age of 18.

1414.title: Tibet
1414.body: The Tibetan lama Je Tsongkhapa, of the Gelug school of Buddhism, declines the offer of the Emperor Cheng Zu of China to appear in the capital at Nanjing, although he sends his disciple Chosrje Shākya Yeshes, who is given the title "State Teacher". The later Xuande Emperor will grant Yeshes the title of a king, upon a return visit to China (to the new capital at Beijing).

1414.title: Khizr Khan
1414.body: Khizr Khan, Timur's governor of Multan in India, conquers the Delhi Sultanate from Daulat Khan Lodi, founding the Sayyid Dynasty.

1414.title: Yongle Emperor
1414.body: China's Emperor Cheng Zu departs from Beijing to lead a military campaign against the Oirat Mongols.

1415.title: Forty Years' War
1415.body: Forty Years' War: (Full moon of Tabaung 776 ME) In what is now Myanmar, Razadarit, ruler of the Hanthawaddy kingdom, authorizes a plan to fight the forces of Minkhaung I ruler of the Ava Kingdom. King Razadarit leads the invasion on March 2 (8th waning of Tabaung 776 ME). King Charles VI of France and John the Fearless, Duke of Burgundy, sign the Treaty of Arras.

1415.title: Conquest of Ceuta
1415.body: Conquest of Ceuta: Portugal conquers the city of Ceuta from the Moors, initiating the Portuguese Empire, and European expansion and colonialism.

1415.title: Henry V of England
1415.body: Henry V of England is informed of the Southampton Plot against him; he has the leaders arrested and executed, before invading France.

1415.title: Battle of Agincourt
1415.body: Battle of Agincourt: Archers, led by Henry V of England are instrumental in defeating a larger army of French knights. Edward, 2nd Duke of York, the son of King Henry, is killed in the battle, along with the French commander, Charles I d'Albret, Constable of Paris, and the second-in-command, John I, Duke of Alençon.

1415.title: Valencia
1415.body: From Valencia in Spain, the Antipope Benedict XIII issues a papal bull with eleven prohibitions against Jews, including a ban on teaching, reading or possessing the Talmud; prohibition of Jewish possession of Christian artifacts or Christian books; limiting each town to only one synagogue; barring Jews from serving specific jobs or making contracts; segregating Jews from Christians in all public places; and requiring all Jews to wear "a red and yellow sign" on their clothes. Jews who convert to the Roman Catholic faith become exempt from the restrictions

1416.title: London
1416.body: Unable to reach an agreement with France, King Sigismund of Germany travels to London, where he is welcomed by King Henry V of England.

1416.title: The Battle of Sant'Egidio is fought in Italy
1416.body: The Battle of Sant'Egidio is fought in Italy: soldier of fortune Braccio da Montone, Prince of Capua, conquers Umbria and its capital, Perugia.

1416.title: Catholic Church
1416.body: The Catholic Church burns Jerome of Prague for heresy at the Council of Constance.

1416.title: Paris
1416.body: Sigismund arrives in Paris to reach an agreement with the Franch government, but is unable to because of difficulty in reaching a conclusion satisfactory to both its Orleanist and Burgundian factions.

1416.title: Sigismund, Holy Roman Emperor
1416.body: August 15 Sigismund, King of Germany, and King Henry V of England conclude the Treaty of Canterbury, an alliance of the two nations against France. Harfleur relieved, following a naval battle in the estuary of the Seine.

1417.title: Henry V of England
1417.body: King Henry V of England begins using the English language in correspondence back to England from France, where he is leading a campaign, marking the beginning of this king's continuous usage of English in prose, and the beginning of the restoration of English as an official language for the first time since the Norman Conquest, some 350 years earlier.

1417.title: Halloween
1417.body: On Hallowtide, by order of the Lord May Henry Barton, street lighting is first used in London, with lanterns to be hung out on winter evenings, lasting until the night of Candlemas on February 2.

1417.title: Frederick I, Elector of Brandenburg
1417.body: Frederick I, Elector of Brandenburg, is appointed to an additional position as Archchamberlain of the Holy Roman Empire.

1417.title: Tynwald Day
1417.body: The earliest extant description of Tynwald Day; the annual meeting of the Isle of Man's parliament (Tynwald) is written down in law. Its first recorded use for the promulgation of laws dates to 24 June 1417, when Sir John Stanley presided.

1417.title: Trévoux
1417.body: In France, the Baron of Trévoux reverses a ban against the town's Jewish population, and allows them to study the Talmud without interference.

1418.title: Ming treasure voyages
1418.body: The Ming Chinese explorers, commanded by Admiral Zheng He, arrive in Yemen to open trade with the Arab kingdom, and the admiral is brought to the court of the Emir Al Malik al Nasir. The Chinese stay until January 27.

1418.title: Byzantine Empire
1418.body: The Byzantine Empire and the Republic of Venice sign a treaty of commerce.

1418.title: Švitrigaila
1418.body: Švitrigaila of Lithuania, son of the late Grand Duke Algirdas, is freed after nine years imprisonment at Lithuania's Kremenets Castle after a group of 500 soldiers, led by Dashko Ostrogski, storm the castle.

1418.title: Armagnac–Burgundian Civil War
1418.body: Armagnac–Burgundian Civil War: John the Fearless, Duke of Burgundy, captures Paris, forcing King Charles VI to flee from the violence of John's partisans.

1418.title: Castello Visconteo
1418.body: Beatrice Lascaris di Tenda, Duchess of Milan as the wife of the Duke Filippo Maria Visconti, is secretly arrested for adultery and removed from the walled city of Milan while its gates are locked. On her husband's orders, she is transferred to the Castello Visconteo in nearby Binasco, where she, two of her maidens, and her lover, the troubadour Michele Orombelli, are tortured. Beatrice is beheaded on September 13, and her three accomplices are put to death on the same day.

1419.title: Michael I of Wallachia
1419.body: Mihail I defends Wallachia against the Ottomans, with Hungarian help.

1419.title: Portugal
1419.body: Portuguese explorers João Gonçalves Zarco, Tristão Vaz Teixeira and Bartolomeu Perestrello, at the service of Prince Henry the Navigator, discover Madeira Island, a year after Zarco and Tristão had discovered Porto Santo Island.

1419.title: Siege of Ceuta
1419.body: Siege of Ceuta: After four days of battle, the Portuguese defenders of the city of Ceuta are able to force a retreat of invaders from Morocco.

1419.title: Henry V of England
1419.body: King Henry V of England is betrothed to Princess Catherine of Valois, the 17-year-old daughter of King Charles VI of France, in negotiations in the neutral site of Pontoise, north of Paris, during a halt to hostilities in the war between the two kingdoms. Henry and Catherine will marry a little more than a year later, on June 2, 1420.

1419.title: Hundred Years' War
1419.body: Hundred Years' War: Rouen surrenders to Henry V of England, which brings Normandy under the control of England.

1420.title: Great Frisian War
1420.body: Great Frisian War: In a war that began 70 years earlier in 1350 The Skieringers, led by Sikke Sjaarda, capture the city of Bolsward (part of Friesland in the modern Netherlands) from the wealthier Fetkeapers. The allies of the Fetkeapers, led by Focko Ukena, then react and make a counterattack on May 12 at Palesloot near Hindeloopen.

1420.title: 1420 Caldera earthquake
1420.body: A 9.4 MS-strong earthquake shakes Chile's Atacama Region, causing tsunamis in Chile, as well as Hawaii and Japan.

1420.title: Hussite Wars
1420.body: Hussite Wars After their offer of a surrender is refused, the outnumbered Hussites of Bohemia, led by Jan Zizka, defeat the invading Holy Roman Imperial forces at the Battle of Sudoměř fought near modern-day Čejetice in the Czech Republic.

1420.title: Treaty of Troyes
1420.body: Treaty of Troyes: With the Burgundian faction dominant in France, King Charles VI of France acknowledges Henry V of England as his heir, and as virtual ruler of most of France.

1420.title: Catherine of Valois
1420.body: Catherine of Valois (daughter of Charles VI of France) is married to King Henry V of England.

1421.title: Erciş
1421.body: At Erciş in what is now Turkey, Iskandar ibn Yusuf is crowned as the Sultan of Qara Qoyunlu, a kingdom encompassing what are now eastern Turkey, northwestern Iran and northern Iraq, northern Armenia and all of Azerbaijan.

1421.title: Mehmed
1421.body: Mehmed I, Sultan of the Ottoman Empire, dies and is succeeded by his son, Murad II.

1421.title: St. Elizabeth's flood
1421.body: St. Elizabeth's flood: The coastal area near Dordrecht in the Netherlands is flooded, due to the extremely high tide of the North Sea; 72 villages are drowned, killing about 10,000 people, and the course of the Meuse is changed.

1421.title: Battle of Brüx
1421.body: At the Battle of Brüx, Hussite troops led by Jan Želivský, are defeated by the German Saxon baron Frederick of Meissen at what is now the city of Most in what is now the Czech Republic.

1421.title: South Korea
1421.body: In what is now South Korea, Taejong of Goryeo, King of Joseon from 1400 to 1418, then King Emeritus since 1418, is crowned as the first Grand King Emeritus of Joseon.

1422.title: Teutonic Knights
1422.body: The Teutonic Knights sign the Treaty of Melno with the Kingdom of Poland and Grand Duchy of Lithuania, after the brief Gollub War. The Prussian–Lithuanian border established by the treaty remains unchanged, until World War I.

1422.title: Ottoman Empire
1422.body: September – The Ottoman sultan Murad II besieges Constantinople; the siege is broken off as a result of the rebellion of Küçük Mustafa.

1422.title: Romani people
1422.body: In Italy, a group of about 100 Romani people, described as "Gypsies" arrive in Bologna with a stated intention of traveling to Rome to meet the Pope. Their leader, calling himself "Duke Andrea", shows to local officials a decree allegedly signed by the King of Hungary that is said to grant them the right to commit robberies "without penalty or penance" wherever they travel, as a reward for 4,000 gypsies being re-baptized into the Christian faith.

1422.title: Charles VI of France
1422.body: King Charles VI of France passes away at the age of 53 after years of mental illness, and rival groups fight over who his successor will be. In Paris, Henry VI of England, the 10-month old son of the late French regent, England's King Henry V, and grandson of King Charles VI, is proclaimed King Henri II of France. In Bourges, the Dauphin Charles, eldest son of King Charles VI, is proclaimed as King Charles VII.

1422.title: Le Marché
1422.body: Hundred Years' War – The Siege of Meaux ends in France after more than five months as the starving citizens of the walled city of Meaux open the city gates and surrender to the English forces led by King Henry V. The city had been under siege since October 6. The city's defending troops flee across a bridge to their last line of defense, the fortress known as Le Marché.

1423.title: Hussite Wars
1423.body: Hussite Wars: In what is now the Czech Republic, General Sigismund Korybut, commander of the Hussite Army, withdraws his troops from Prague on the orders of Vytautas, Grand Duke of Lithuania and Władysław II Jagiełło, King of Poland.

1423.title: Ataullah Muhammad Shah I of Kedah
1423.body: Ataullah Muhammad Shah I begins a 50-year reign as the Sultan of Kedah in what is now Malaysia, following the death of his father, Sulaiman Shah I, who had reigned for 50 years after becoming Sultan in 1373.

1423.title: Hundred Years' War
1423.body: Hundred Years' War: The Treaty of Amiens is signed as a mutual defense treaty between the Duchy of Burgundy, the Duchy of Brittany and the Kingdom of England.

1423.title: Hundred Years' War – Battle of Cravant
1423.body: Hundred Years' War – Battle of Cravant: The French army is defeated at Cravant, on the banks of the River Yonne near Auxerre, by the English and their Burgundian allies.

1423.title: Henry VI of England
1423.body: The English nobility swear their loyalty to King Henry VI.

1424.title: Joan Beaufort, Queen of Scots
1424.body: While negotiating his release from captivity, King James of Scotland is allowed to be married to Joan Beaufort in London, with a ceremony taking place at Southwark Cathedral.

1424.title: Hussite Wars
1424.body: After the signing of a treaty between the different factions in the Hussite Wars, the Bohemian campaign is completed in the modern-day Czech Republic.

1424.title: Hongxi Emperor
1424.body: Prince Zhu Gaochi of China becomes the Hongxi Emperor (Emperor Renzong) of the Ming dynasty, beginning the Hongxi era, after the August 12 death of his father, the Yongle Emperor.

1424.title: Battle of Verneuil
1424.body: Battle of Verneuil: An English force under John, Duke of Bedford, defeats a larger French army under the Duke of Alençon, John Stewart, and Archibald, Earl of Douglas. Alençon is captured and Douglas killed.

1424.title: Dalmatia
1424.body: Dalmatia: Aliota Capenna, lord of Lesina (modern-day Hvar), offers his realm to the Republic of Venice (also said to have occurred in 1409 and 1421).

1425.title: Tawthalin
1425.body: August – (Tawthalin 787 ME); King Thihathu of Ava, the Burmese monarch in what is now Myanmar, is ambushed at Amarapura after being guided into a trap by one of his wives, Queen Shin Bo-Me. His 8-year-old nephew, Min Hla, becomes the new King while Shin Bo-Me seeks a way to take the throne for herself.

1425.title: Old University of Leuven
1425.body: The Old University of Leuven, Belgium is founded.

1425.title: Henry VI of England
1425.body: King Henry VI of England summons the English Parliament, ordering the members to assemble on April 30 at Westminster.

1425.title: Zafar Nama
1425.body: Sharafuddin Ali Yazdi's critical history of Persia, Zafar Nama, is completed under the auspices of Mirza Ibrahim Sultan, grandson of Timur.

1425.title: Yongle Emperor
1425.body: The interment of Ming dynasty China's Yongle Emperor, who died on August 12, takes place at the Chang Mausoleum in Beijing's Ming tombs.

1426.title: Castello Orsini-Odescalchi
1426.body: Castello Orsini-Odescalchi is built in Bracciano, Italy by the Orsini family.

1426.title: Second Scutari War
1426.body: The Second Scutari War between the Republic of Venice and the Serbian Despotate is formally ended by the signing of a treaty at the Serbian city of Vushtrri in what is now Albania.

1426.title: Jumada al-Thani
1426.body: April 25 (17 Jumada al-Thani 829 AH) – Hasan ibn Ajlan, who has been allowed to leave Cairo after being appointed by the Egyptian Mamluk Sultan as the Emir of Mecca and paying the first installment of a fee of 30,000 dinars, dies on the way back to Mecca. The Sultan, Al-Ashraf Sayf ad-Dīn Barsbāy, summons Hasan's sons Barakat ibn Hasan and Ibrahim ibn Hasan to Cairo to complete payment of the remaining 25,000 dinars owed.

1426.title: Hundred Years' War
1426.body: Hundred Years' War: After being besieged since February 27 in Saint-James (near Avranches in Normandy), a 600-strong force of English knights led by Sir Thomas Rempston routs a 16,000-strong French and Brittanian force under Arthur de Richemont, Constable of France, in the Battle of Saint-James. This forces the Duke of Brittany (Richemont's brother) to offer a truce and on September 8 to acknowledge Henry VI of England's suzerainty over France.

1426.title: Henry VI of England
1426.body: King Henry VI summons the Parliament of England, directing its members to meet at Leicester on February 18.

1427.title: Romani people
1427.body: The first band of Gypsies visits Paris, according to an account of the citizens of Paris.

1427.title: Bhaktapur
1427.body: Bhaktapur Royal Palace in Nepal is built by King Yaksa Malla.

1427.title: Inwa
1427.body: Minrekyansa becomes King of Ava (ancient Myanmar).

1427.title: Sant Jordi
1427.body: The Celebration of Sant Jordi (Saint George) begins in Catalonia (he will later become its patron saint).

1427.title: Diogo de Silves
1427.body: Diogo de Silves, Portuguese navigator, discovers seven islands of the Azores archipelago.

1428.title: Battle of Kratzau
1428.body: The Battle of Kratzau takes place in Bohemia in what is now the city of Chrastava in the Czech Republic, as the Silesian troops of the Bohemian Crown Lands under of the Holy Roman Empire (Upper Lusatia, the duchies of Jawor and Wroclaw, Polenz, Lower Lusatia and the Duchy of Żagań, led by Hans von Polenz, repel an attack by the Sirotci Hussites led by Jan Královec.

1428.title: Lam Sơn uprising
1428.body: Lam Sơn uprising: Lê Lợi, founder of the Lê dynasty in Vietnam, liberates Annam (the territory occupied by Ming dynasty China in 1407) and restores the kingdom as Đại Việt.

1428.title: Joan of Arc
1428.body: Joan of Arc makes her first attempt to reach the Armagnac court at Chinon, arriving at Vaucouleurs speaking to Robert de Baudricourt, captain of the royal garrison, and tells him that God told her in a vision that she had a mission to help restore the Dauphin Charles to the throne. Baudricourt initially declines her request to be given an escort to visit Charles in person.

1428.title: Dano-Hanseatic War (1426–1435)
1428.body: Dano-Hanseatic War (1426–1435): The bombardment of Copenhagen, capital of Denmark, by ships of the Northern German Hanseatic League takes place and is repelled by the Danish Navy.

1428.title: Baynard's Castle
1428.body: A serious fire occurs at Baynard's Castle in the City of London, England.

1429.title: Congress of Lutsk
1429.body: The Congress of Lutsk opens in the Grand Duchy of Lithuania at the castle of Liubartas in Lutsk.
