1756.body: The Marine Society is founded in London, the world's oldest seafarers' charity.

1756.title: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
1756.body: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart is born in Salzburg, Austria, to Anna Maria and Leopold Mozart.

1757.title: Stratford-upon-Avon
1757.body: Inside the house at Stratford-upon-Avon in England known as Shakespeare's Birthplace, a bricklayer, identified only as "Mosely", re-tiling the roof, discovers a supposed pro-Catholic testament of John Shakespeare, father of William Shakespeare, more than 150 years after the elder's death. The find starts "what remains one of the most controversial topics in Shakespeare studies" because of disagreements over its authenticity and date.

1757.title: Osman III
1757.body: Osman III dies, and is succeeded as Ottoman Sultan by Mustafa III.

1757.title: Abdallah of Morocco
1757.body: King Abdallah IV of Morocco dies and is succeeded by his son, who takes the throne as King Mohammed III and reigns until 1790.

1757.title: Sweden
1757.body: Sweden signs an alliance treaty with France and Austria in the multinational effort to remove King Frederick the Great, even though Queen Consort Ulrika of Sweden is Frederick's sister. Sweden agrees to contribute 25,000 troops to the French and Austrian force.

1757.title: Pomeranian War
1757.body: Pomeranian War: a column of troops from Sweden begins the surprise invasion of Prussia, setting up a pontoon bridge across the Peene River that marks the boundary between Swedish Pomerania and northern Prussia. After crossing at Loitz in the early morning hours, the troops march 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) and begin the occupation of the undefended Prussian town of Demmin. Hours later, another Swedish infantry regiment charges across the border into the Prussian town of Anklam, where the city gate had been left open.

1758.title: Novartis
1758.body: J. R. Geigy, predecessor of Novartis, a global pharmaceutical brand, founded in Basel, Switzerland.

1758.title: Oslo
1758.body: A fire destroys parts of Christiania, Norway.

1758.title: Pope Clement XIII
1758.body: July 6 Pope Clement XIII succeeds Pope Benedict XIV, as the 248th pope. Seven Years' War – Battle of Bernetz Brook: British troops defeat the French.

1758.title: Cap-Haïtien
1758.body: At Cap-Haïtien in Haiti, former slave turned rebel François Mackandal is executed by the French colonial government by being burned at the stake.

1758.title: Seven Years' War – Battle of Zorndorf
1758.body: Seven Years' War – Battle of Zorndorf: Frederick halts the Russian army of Count Wilhelm Fermor near the Oder.

1759.title: George Washington
1759.body: George Washington marries Martha Dandridge Custis.

1759.title: Great Stockholm Fire 1759
1759.body: The Great Stockholm Fire 1759 breaks out at Södermalm in Stockholm, Sweden.

1759.title: Egedesminde
1759.body: The town of Egedesminde (modern Aasiaat) is founded in Greenland.

1759.title: Violent
1759.body: Near East earthquakes of 1759: The second and stronger event in an earthquake doublet occurs to the east of Beirut, with a surface wave magnitude of 7.4 and a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (Violent), destroying all the villages in the Beqaa Valley.

1759.title: Alamgir
1759.body: Alamgir II, the Mughal Emperor of India, is assassinated in a conspiracy orchestrated by his Prime Minister, Imad-ul-Mulk. The Shah Alam II, a grandson of the 17th century Emperor Aurangzeb, is made the new Mughal Emperor.

1760.title: Seven Years' War
1760.body: Seven Years' War: Jeffery Amherst and his British troops capture Montreal from the French, effectively bringing Canada completely under British control.

1760.title: Alaungpaya
1760.body: The Burmese Army, under the command of King Alaungpaya, reaches the outskirts of Siam's capital, Ayutthaya, but then retreats rather than laying siege to the city.

1760.title: Daniel Bernoulli
1760.body: Swiss mathematician Daniel Bernoulli presents a paper at the French Academy of Sciences in Paris in which "a mathematical model was used for the first time to study the population dynamics of infectious disease."

1760.title: Great Boston Fire of 1760
1760.body: The Great Fire of Boston, Massachusetts, destroys 349 buildings and marks the biggest conflagration in the American colonies up to this time.

1760.title: Royal Katherine
1760.body: The British Royal Navy ship HMS Royal Katherine runs aground off Bolt Head in England, with the loss of 699 lives.

1761.title: George III
1761.body: George III marries Charlotte of Mecklenburg-Strelitz.

1761.title: Maryland Gazette
1761.body: Two years after his marriage to Martha Custis and his move to Mount Vernon, American military officer and politician George Washington advertises a reward in the Maryland Gazette for the capture of four fugitive slaves who ran away from him: Cupid, Peros, Jack and Neptune, claiming in the gazette that they had escaped "without the least suspicion, provocation, or difference with anybody".

1761.title: Spain
1761.body: A 500-man force from the Army of Spain brings the revolt of Mexico's Maya population to an end, capturing the Yucatan village of Cisteil, killing about 500 of the 2,500 Mayan defenders and losing 40 of their own. The Spaniards arrest 254 people, including Jacinto Canek, who had proclaimed himself as King Canek Montezuma of the Mayas. Canek and eight other rebellion leaders are executed less than three weeks later.

1761.title: Earthquake
1761.body: An earthquake in London breaks chimneys in Limehouse and Poplar.

1761.title: Influenza
1761.body: A severe epidemic of influenza breaks out in London and "practically the entire population of the city" is afflicted; particularly contagious to pregnant women, the disease causes an unusual number of miscarriages and premature births.

1762.title: North Carolina General Assembly
1762.body: The North Carolina General Assembly incorporates Kingston, named for King George III, as the county seat of Dobbs County, North Carolina. The name is later shortened to Kinston in 1784.

1762.title: 1762 Arakan earthquake
1762.body: A powerful earthquake along the border between modern-day Bangladesh and Myanmar causes a tsunami in the Bay of Bengal that kills at least 200 people.

1762.title: Catherine the Great
1762.body: Catherine II becomes empress of Russia after planning the overthrow of her husband, the Tsar Peter III. The incipient Russo-Prussian alliance falls apart, but Russia does not rejoin the war. Peter is strangled eight days later.

1762.title: kromye Zhydov
1762.body: Less than six months after becoming Russia's Empress, Catherine the Great announces that almost all foreigners are welcome to travel to and settle in Russia, and waives previous requirements that new residents must be members of the Russian Orthodox Church; however, the manifesto adds the phrase kromye Zhydov - "except the Jews".

1762.title: Plymouth Synagogue
1762.body: The Plymouth Synagogue is built in Plymouth, England, the oldest built by Ashkenazi Jews in the English-speaking world.

1763.title: Province of North Carolina
1763.body: The colonial authorities in the Province of North Carolina establish Mecklenburg County from the western portion of Anson County. The county is named for Charlotte of Mecklenburg-Strelitz, who married George III in 1761.

1763.title: Royal Proclamation of 1763
1763.body: The Royal Proclamation of 1763 is issued by George III of Great Britain, restricting the westward expansion of British North America, and stabilizing relations with the indigenous peoples of the Americas, by barring white settlement of lands west of the Appalachian Mountains.

1763.title: Smyrna
1763.body: August – Fire in Smyrna, Ottoman Empire, destroys 2,600 houses.

1763.title: Violent
1763.body: A magnitude 6.2 earthquake shakes Hungary and Slovakia, with a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (Violent). Damage is limited, but 83 are killed.

1763.title: Mozart family grand tour
1763.body: The Mozart family grand tour of Europe began, lifting the profile of child prodigy Wolfgang Amadeus.

1764.title: Acadians
1764.body: Conditional repatriation of the Acadians in Canada, French colonists who took up arms against the British during the war, is approved by order of King George III on advice of the Privy Council. The Council offers settlement to any Acadians willing to take an oath of allegiance to the British Crown and that those living in New Brunswick are to "be allowed to settle in Nova Scotia, but that they should be dispersed in small numbers in various localities."

1764.title: Havana
1764.body: The last British troops depart Havana, Cuba, two years after having captured it from Spain during the Seven Years' War. The removal of troops follows the treaty between the two Kingdoms, with Spain ceding West Florida to Great Britain in return for the Havana withdrawal.

1764.title: Zend Avesta
1764.body: The day after his return to Paris from a nine-year mission, French explorer and scholar Anquetil Du Perron presents a complete copy of the Zoroastrian sacred text, the Zend Avesta, to the Bibliothèque Royale in Paris, along with several other traditional texts. In 1771, he publishes the first European translation of the Zend Avesta.

1764.title: Russo-Prussian alliance
1764.body: A mutual defense treaty between the Russian Empire and the Kingdom of Prussia is signed in Saint Petersburg between representatives of Russia's Empress Catherine the Great and Prussia's King Frederick the Great. By agreement, each nation agrees (for an eight-year period) to commit 10,000 soldiers and 2,000 horses to the defense of the other in case of an attack, and secretly agree to maintain security within the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth.

1764.title: Catherine the Great
1764.body: Catherine the Great establishes the first secondary education school for females in Russia – The Smolny Institute, for girls of the nobility in St. Petersburg.

1765.title: Qianlong Emperor
1765.body: Qianlong, the Emperor of China issues a decree that copper engravings be made to depict all of his victories in battle. In the interest of amity with the Chinese, King George III of Great Britain gives priority to the sale of British copper, and King Louis XV of France assents to the use of French artisans.

1765.title: visitador general
1765.body: Spain's five-member "special junta", appointed by Prime Minister Jerónimo Grimaldi, delivers its report regarding "ways to address the backwardness of Spain's commerce with its colonies and with foreign nations". The report provides detailed orders to be delivered to José de Gálvez, the visitador general in charge of New Spain.

1765.title: Russian Empire
1765.body: Russian Empress Catherine II issues a decree authorizing the new way to produce vodka (by freezing).

1765.title: Catherine the Great
1765.body: Catherine the Great establishes the first secondary education school for non-noble females in Russia: the Novodevichii Institute, for the daughters of commoners.

1765.title: Voltaire
1765.body: After a public campaign by the writer Voltaire, judges in Paris posthumously exonerate Jean Calas of murdering his son. Calas had been tortured and executed in 1762 on the charge, though his son may have committed suicide.

1766.title: George III
1766.body: On the occasion of the 28th birthday of King George III, members of the Sons of Liberty in Manhattan erect a liberty pole as a protest for the first time. The historic symbol, a tall "wooden pole with a Phrygian cap" is placed "on the Fields somewhere between Broadway and Park Row". British soldiers cut down the pole in August.

1766.title: List of heads of the Serbian Orthodox Church
1766.body: The position of Patriarch of the Serbs, established on April 9, 1346 as the authority over the Serbian Orthodox Church, is abolished by order of Sultan Mustafa III of the Ottoman Empire; the patriarchate is not re-established until 1920 following the creation of Yugoslavia at the end of World War One.

1766.title: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
1766.body: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart returns to Salzburg, after the Mozart family grand tour of Europe.

1766.title: Law on the Freedom of Printing
1766.body: The Law on the Freedom of Printing abolishes censorship in Sweden and guarantees freedom of the press, making Sweden the first country of the world to introduce constitutional protection of press freedom, and to pass wide-ranging freedom of information legislation.

1766.title: John Mills
1766.body: John Mills is elected a Fellow of the Royal Society, with Benjamin Franklin as one of his sponsors.

1767.title: Oconostota
1767.body: Oconostota and Attakullakulla, Chiefs of the Cherokee people in the Carolinas, depart from Charleston, South Carolina on a ship voyage to New York City, where they are welcomed by British colonial officials as a prelude to negotiations with Britain's Superintendent of Indian Affairs, Sir William Johnson.

1767.title: Timucua
1767.body: The Timucua Indian tribe, native to central Florida, becomes extinct with the death of the last speaker of the Timucuan language, Juan Alonso Cabale. Eight years earlier, the last 95 surviving Timucuan people had been forcibly relocated by the Spanish colonial government to Guanabacoa, a township in western Cuba.

1767.title: les Îles Malouines
1767.body: Spain acquires control of what are now called the Falkland Islands from France, compensating French Admiral Louis Antoine de Bougainville for the money spent on the construction of the settlement at Fort Saint Louis. The islands, named les Îles Malouines by the French, are renamed las Islas Malvinas by the Spanish, and Fort Saint Louis is renamed as Puerto Soledad. In 1816, Argentina declares independence from Spain and takes the Malvinas; and in 1833, Britain's Royal Navy captures the islands from the Argentines and renames them the Falklands, and renames Puerto Soledad as Port Louis.

1767.title: William Petty, 2nd Earl of Shelburne
1767.body: The Earl of Shelburne, British Secretary of State for the Southern Department (which has jurisdiction over Britain's American colonies) fires the unpopular Governor of West Florida, George Johnstone, and summons him back to London.

1767.title: Foundling Hospital
1767.body: At the Foundling Hospital in London, Dr. William Watson becomes the first physician to conduct a controlled clinical trial, selecting 32 boys and girls of similar age who have not yet had smallpox. He divides them into three groups in order to test treatments before inoculation for smallpox, with one group receiving a mixture of mercury and jalap, another senna glycoside, and the third getting no pre-treatment at all.

1768.title: The North Briton
1768.body: Massacre of St George's Fields: John Wilkes is imprisoned for writing an article for The North Briton, severely criticizing King George III. This action provokes protesters to riot; in the Southwark district of London, troops fire on the mob, killing seven.

1768.title: Balta, Ukraine
1768.body: The massacre of Polish people (most likely by the Russians) at the village of Balta, now a part of Ukraine but at the time an Ottoman Empire town on the frontier with Poland, leads to the Russo-Turkish War.

1768.title: Cuba
1768.body: A powerful hurricane sweeps across Cuba during the Festival of Santa Teresa, killing hundreds of people. Spain's King Carlos III begins a precedent of ordering the colonial government to fund disaster relief, a task previously left to the Catholic Church.

1768.title: Pyotr Krechetnikov
1768.body: Catherine the Great of Russia dispatches troops under General Pyotr Krechetnikov to intervene in a civil war in Poland, at the request of Poland's King Stanisław II Augustus, a move that will ultimately lead to the Partitions of Poland.

1768.title: Prithvi Narayan Shah
1768.body: King Prithvi Narayan Shah unifies several small kingdoms to establish modern-day Nepal; this kingdom will collapse in 2008.

1769.title: Charles III of Spain
1769.body: Charles III of Spain sends Spanish missionaries, who found California missions in San Diego, Santa Barbara, San Francisco and Monterey, and begin the settlement of California.

1769.title: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
1769.body: Mozart departs Italy, after the last of his three tours there.

1769.title: James Watt
1769.body: Scottish inventor James Watt is granted a British patent for "A method of lessening the consumption of steam in steam engines" – the separate condenser, a key improvement (first devised by Watt in 1765) and the basis for the Watt steam engine which stimulates the Industrial Revolution.

1769.title: Authorized King James Version
1769.body: The Authorized King James Version of the Bible is published in England in the Oxford standard text, edited by Benjamin Blayney.

1769.title: Endeavour
1769.body: James Cook arrives in Tahiti, on the ship HM Bark Endeavour, preparing for the 1769 Transit of Venus observed from Tahiti on June 3. After the voyage, the data is found to be inaccurate in determining the distance between the Sun and Earth.

1770.title: James Cook
1770.body: April 18 (April 19 by Cook's log) 18:00 – First voyage of James Cook: English explorer Captain James Cook and his crew become the first recorded Europeans to encounter the eastern coastline of the Australian continent. Land is sighted at Point Hicks, and named after Lieutenant Hicks who first observes landform at 6am.

1770.title: Lexell's Comet
1770.body: Lexell's Comet (D/1770 L1) passes the Earth at a distance of 2,184,129 kilometres (1,357,155 mi), the closest approach by a comet in recorded history.

1770.title: Battle of Aspindza
1770.body: Battle of Aspindza: Georgian king Erekle II defeats the Ottoman forces, despite being abandoned by an ally, Russian General Totleben.

1770.title: Battle of Chesma and Battle of Larga
1770.body: Battle of Chesma and Battle of Larga: The Russian Empire defeats the Ottoman Empire in both battles. When the news of the defeat reaches the Ottoman city of Smyrna (July 8), the crowd attacks the Greek community of the city (perceived as favourable to the Russian cause) and kills an estimated 200 Greeks and three Western Europeans (although some reports estimate the number of victims at 3,000 or even 5,000 including "3 or 4 thousands who die due to the fright").

1770.title: Marie Antoinette
1770.body: year-old Marie Antoinette of Austria marries Louis-Auguste (who in 1774 becomes King Louis XVI) at the Palace of Versailles.

1771.title: California
1771.body: In California, Fathers Pedro Cambon and Angel Somera found Mission Vieja, later called, Mission San Gabriel Arcángel, in what becomes San Gabriel, California.

1771.title: Adolf Frederick, King of Sweden
1771.body: Upon the death of Adolf Frederick, he is succeeded as King of Sweden by his son Gustav III. At the time, however, Gustav is unaware of this, since he is abroad in Paris; the news of his father's death reaches him about a month later.

1771.title: Ras
1771.body: May – Three battles of Sarbakusa: An alliance of three of the most powerful aristocrats of Ethiopia (Goshu of Amhara, Wand Bewossen and Fasil of Damot) defeats Ras Mikael Sehul and Emperor Tekle Haymanot II, taking control of Ethiopia.

1771.title: Iceland
1771.body: The trade monopoly with Iceland is transferred to the Danish crown.

1771.title: Kalmyk people
1771.body: The Great Kalmyk (Torghut Migration) is led by Ubashi Khan, from the east bank of the Lower Volga River back to the homeland of Dzungaria, at this time under Qing dynasty rule.

1772.title: Resolution
1772.body: The second voyage of James Cook departs from Plymouth on Captain Cook's new ship, HMS Resolution and the companion ship HMS Adventure in an attempt to prove the existence of an uncharted continent even further south than New Zealand.

1772.title: Warren Hastings
1772.body: Warren Hastings begins his service for the British East India Company as Governor of Bengal, arriving at the company's headquarters at Fort William, outside of Calcutta, and including what are now parts of northeast India and Bangladesh. Hastings serves for two years, then later becomes Governor-General of India.

1772.title: Revolution of 1772
1772.body: A coup d'état by King Gustav III is completed by adopting a new Constitution, ending half a century of parliamentary rule in Sweden, and making him an enlightened despot.

1772.title: American Revolutionary War
1772.body: American Revolutionary War: Samuel Adams and Joseph Warren form the first Committee of Correspondence.

1772.title: Johann Friedrich Struensee
1772.body: Johann Friedrich Struensee and Queen Caroline Matilda are arrested, leading to his execution and her banishment from Denmark.

1773.title: Regulating Act of 1773
1773.body: The Regulating Act is given royal assent by King George III, creating the office of Governor General, with an advising council, to exercise political authority over the territory under British East India Company rule in India.

1773.title: Christ Church
1773.body: The construction of Christ Church (Alexandria, Virginia), known for being the house of worship for George Washington and the visiting site for subsequent U.S. presidents, is completed.

1773.title: Egypt
1773.body: In Egypt, Ottoman rebels revolt, killing Ali Bey, Mamluk Sultan of Egypt.

1773.title: Moscow State Academy of Choreography
1773.body: Moscow State Academy of Choreography was founded under the reign of Catherine II. It is the second ballet school in Russia after Vaganova Academy of Russian Ballet.

1773.title: Thetis and Phelée
1773.body: The first opera performance in the Swedish language, Thetis and Phelée, performed by Carl Stenborg and Elisabeth Olin in Bollhuset in Stockholm, Sweden, marks the establishment of the Royal Swedish Opera.

1774.title: George III
1774.body: November 30 Parliament adjourns in Great Britain, but declines to authorize any action against the rebellious American colonies, despite an address the day before by King George III and Prime Minister North. Thomas Paine, a native of England, arrives in America at the age 37 and soon becomes an influential advocate for the colonies' independence.

1774.title: George Mason
1774.body: George Mason and George Washington found the Fairfax County Militia Association, a military unit independent of British control.

1774.title: Mustafa III
1774.body: Mustafa III, Sultan of the Ottoman Empire, dies and is succeeded by his brother Abdul Hamid I.

1774.title: Old Style and New Style dates
1774.body: June 20 (June 9 O.S.) – Russo-Turkish War (1768–1774): Battle of Kozludzha – The Imperial Russian Army, led by Alexander Suvorov, routs numerically superior Ottoman Empire forces.

1774.title: Russian Empire
1774.body: Russia and the Ottoman Empire sign the Treaty of Küçük Kaynarca with Russian victory, ending six years of war. The treaty gives Russia the right to intervene in Ottoman politics, to protect its Christian subjects.

1775.title: American Revolution
1775.body: American Revolution: The Continental Congress bans trade with Canada.

1775.title: Second Continental Congress
1775.body: The Second Continental Congress meets, elects John Hancock president, raises the Continental Army under George Washington as commander and authorizes the colonies to adopt their own constitutions.

1775.title: Habsburg monarchy
1775.body: January – The Habsburg monarchy forces the Ottoman Empire to cede Bukovina to its rule.

1775.title: Catherine the Great
1775.body: Catherine the Great of Russia issues a manifesto prohibiting freed serfs from being returned to serfdom.

1775.title: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
1775.body: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart finishes a Sonata for Keyboard in C.

1776.title: American Revolution – Battle of Long Island
1776.body: American Revolution – Battle of Long Island: Washington's troops are defeated in Brooklyn by the British, under William Howe.

1776.title: Rhode Island
1776.body: Rhode Island becomes the first American colony to renounce allegiance to King George III of Great Britain.

1776.title: American Revolution
1776.body: American Revolution – An angry mob in New York City topples the equestrian statue of George III of Great Britain in Bowling Green.

1776.title: Illuminati
1776.body: Adam Weishaupt founds the Illuminati in Ingolstadt, Bavaria.

1776.title: Serenade No. 7
1776.body: Mozart's Serenade No. 7 (the "Haffner") is first performed in Salzburg, Austria.

1777.title: American Revolutionary War – Battle of the Assunpink Creek
1777.body: American Revolutionary War – Battle of the Assunpink Creek: American general George Washington's army repulses a British attack by Lieutenant General Charles Cornwallis, in a second battle at Trenton, New Jersey.

1777.title: American Revolutionary War – Battle of Germantown
1777.body: American Revolutionary War – Battle of Germantown: Troops under George Washington are repelled by British troops under Sir William Howe.

1777.title: American Revolutionary War
1777.body: American Revolutionary War – George Washington's Continental Army goes into winter quarters at Valley Forge, Pennsylvania.

1777.title: American Revolutionary War – Battle of Princeton
1777.body: American Revolutionary War – Battle of Princeton: American general George Washington's army defeats British troops.

1777.title: American Revolutionary War – Battle of Bound Brook
1777.body: American Revolutionary War – Battle of Bound Brook: A British and Hessian force led by Charles Cornwallis surprises a Continental Army outpost in New Jersey, commanded by Major General Benjamin Lincoln.

1778.title: In the United States
1778.body: In the United States: South Carolina becomes the first state to ratify the Articles of Confederation. General John Cadwalader shoots and seriously wounds Major General Thomas Conway in a duel after a dispute between the two officers over Conway's continued criticism of General George Washington's leadership of the Continental Army.

1778.title: John Cadwalader
1778.body: General John Cadwalader shoots and seriously wounds Major General Thomas Conway in a duel after a dispute between the two officers over Conway's continued criticism of General George Washington's leadership of the Continental Army.

1778.title: American Revolutionary War
1778.body: American Revolutionary War: George Washington approves the dishonorable discharge of Lieutenant Frederick Gotthold Enslin, for "attempting to commit sodomy, with John Monhort a soldier".

1778.title: Solar eclipse of June 24, 1778
1778.body: A total solar eclipse takes place across parts of North America, from Texas to Virginia.

1778.title: Louis XVI
1778.body: Louis XVI of France declares war on the Kingdom of Great Britain.

1779.title: Treaty of Aynalıkavak
1779.body: The Treaty of Aynalıkavak is signed between Ottoman Turkey and the Russian Empire, regarding the Crimean Khanate.

1779.title: Spain
1779.body: Spain and France secretly sign the Convention of Aranjuez, with Spain joining an alliance against Great Britain in return for France's pledge to recover all Spanish territory lost to the British.

1779.title: Tampere
1779.body: The city of Tampere, Finland (belonging to Sweden at this time) is founded by King Gustav III of Sweden.

1779.title: North Carolina General Assembly
1779.body: After a second petition for partition from its residents, the North Carolina General Assembly abolishes Bute County, North Carolina (established 1764) by dividing it and naming the northern portion Warren County (for Revolutionary War hero Joseph Warren), the southern portion Franklin County (for Benjamin Franklin). The General Assembly also establishes Warrenton (also named for Joseph Warren) to be the seat of Warren County, and Louisburg (named for Louis XVI of France) to be the seat of Franklin County.

1779.title: American Revolutionary War
1779.body: American Revolutionary War – Claudius Smith is hanged at Goshen, Orange County, New York for supposed acts of terrorism upon the people of the surrounding communities.

1780.title: Anti-Catholic
1780.body: An Anti-Catholic mob led by Lord George Gordon marches on the Parliament of Great Britain, leading to the outbreak of the Gordon Riots in London.

1780.title: Alexander Hamilton
1780.body: Alexander Hamilton marries Elizabeth Schuyler Hamilton

1780.title: Hermione
1780.body: March 11 The First League of Armed Neutrality is formed by Russia with Denmark and Sweden to try to prevent the British Royal Navy from searching neutral vessels for contraband (February 28 O.S.). General Lafayette embarks on French frigate Hermione at Rochefort, arriving in Boston on April 28, carrying the news that he has secured French men and ships to reinforce the American side in the American Revolutionary War.

1780.title: First League of Armed Neutrality
1780.body: The First League of Armed Neutrality is formed by Russia with Denmark and Sweden to try to prevent the British Royal Navy from searching neutral vessels for contraband (February 28 O.S.).

1780.title: American Revolutionary War
1780.body: American Revolutionary War – Battle of Cape St. Vincent: British Admiral Sir George Rodney defeats a Spanish fleet.

1781.title: Georgium Sidus
1781.body: Sir William Herschel discovers the planet Uranus. Originally he calls it Georgium Sidus (George's Star), in honour of King George III.

1781.title: American Revolution
1781.body: American Revolution: Following the Siege of Yorktown, General Charles Cornwallis surrenders to General George Washington at Yorktown, Virginia, ending the armed struggle of the American Revolution.

1781.title: El Pueblo de Nuestra Señora la Reina de Los Ángeles de Porciuncula
1781.body: Los Angeles is founded as El Pueblo de Nuestra Señora la Reina de Los Ángeles de Porciuncula ("City of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of Porciuncula"), by a group of 44 Spanish settlers in California.

1781.title: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
1781.body: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart arrives in Vienna after being fired by the Archbishop of Salzburg. He would stay there for the rest of his brief life.

1781.title: Andrew Jackson
1781.body: Future U.S. President Andrew Jackson, age 14, is slashed by a British officer's sword at his home near Waxhaw, North Carolina, after refusing to clean the officer's boots, an event that leaves physical and psychological scars.

1782.title: Bronze Horseman
1782.body: August 7 George Washington orders the creation of the Badge of Military Merit (or the Order of the Purple Heart) to honor soldiers' merit in battle (reinstated later by Franklin D. Roosevelt, and renamed to the more poetic "Purple Heart", to honor soldiers wounded in action). Étienne Maurice Falconet's Bronze Horseman statue of Tsar Peter the Great is unveiled in Saint Petersburg.

1782.title: George Washington
1782.body: George Washington orders the creation of the Badge of Military Merit (or the Order of the Purple Heart) to honor soldiers' merit in battle (reinstated later by Franklin D. Roosevelt, and renamed to the more poetic "Purple Heart", to honor soldiers wounded in action).

1782.title: Freemasonry
1782.body: The Masonic Congress of Wilhelmsbad, Germany, one of the most important secret society congresses in history, takes place. High-degree Freemasons from the whole of Europe spend the time deliberating the fate of the rite of Strict Observance, and hierarchy of the governing bodies of world Freemasonry, at the Hanau-Wilhelmsbad spa.

1782.title: Rama
1782.body: Rama I overthrows King Taksin of Siam (now Thailand) in a coup d'état, and moves the political capital from Thonburi, across the Chao Phraya River to Rattanakosin Island, the historic center of Bangkok.

1782.title: Die Entführung aus dem Serail
1782.body: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's opera Die Entführung aus dem Serail premieres at the Burgtheater in Vienna.

1783.title: Newburgh Conspiracy
1783.body: Newburgh Conspiracy: A potential uprising in the Continental Army stationed at Newburgh, New York, is defused, when George Washington asks the officers to support the supremacy of the United States Congress.

1783.title: George Washington
1783.body: April – General George Washington sends a letter to the 13 governors of the Confederation of the United States, regarding the needs of the nation.

1783.title: Farewell Address to the Army
1783.body: In Rocky Hill, New Jersey, United States General George Washington gives his Farewell Address to the Army.

1783.title: Fraunces Tavern
1783.body: At Fraunces Tavern in New York City, U.S. General George Washington formally bids his officers farewell.

1783.title: John Michell
1783.body: English rector John Michell concludes that some stars might have enough gravity force to prevent light escaping from them, so he calls them "dark stars".

1784.title: George III
1784.body: The North Carolina General Assembly changes the name of Kingston, North Carolina, originally named for King George III of Great Britain, to Kinston.

1784.title: Treaty of Constantinople
1784.body: Treaty of Constantinople: The Ottoman Empire agrees to Russia's annexation of the Crimea.

1784.title: Experiments on Air
1784.body: Henry Cavendish's paper to the Royal Society of London, Experiments on Air, reveals the composition of water.

1784.title: Locust
1784.body: A huge locust swarm hits South Africa.

1784.title: Kettle War
1784.body: "Kettle War", a 1-day action on the Scheldt in which a ship of the Dutch Republic repels forces of the Holy Roman Empire.

1785.title: James Rumsey
1785.body: March 10 American engineer James Rumsey sends a letter to George Washington informing of his plans to create a steamboat. Thomas Jefferson is appointed the new U.S. Minister to France, and Benjamin Franklin's request for permission to return home is accepted.

1785.title: Freemasonry
1785.body: An edict is issued limiting Masonic lodges throughout the Holy Roman Empire by Emperor Joseph II. With the exception of Vienna, Budapest and Prague, no Empire province may have more than one lodge.

1785.title: Battle of Rạch Gầm-Xoài Mút
1785.body: Battle of Rạch Gầm-Xoài Mút: Invading Siamese forces, attempting to exploit the political chaos in Vietnam, are ambushed and annihilated at the Mekong River by the Tây Sơn.

1785.title: Catherine the Great
1785.body: The Empress Catherine the Great of the Russian Empire issues the Charter to the Towns, providing for "a coherent, unified system of administration" for new governments organized in Russia.

1785.title: Thomas Jefferson
1785.body: Thomas Jefferson is appointed the new U.S. Minister to France, and Benjamin Franklin's request for permission to return home is accepted.

1786.title: George III
1786.body: King George III of the United Kingdom appoints Captain Arthur Phillip as the first Governor of New Holland, which comprises the area of modern Australia from the 135th meridian east to the east coast and all adjacent islands in the Pacific Ocean.

1786.title: The Marriage of Figaro
1786.body: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's opera The Marriage of Figaro premieres in Vienna.

1786.title: Islamic calendar
1786.body: October 23 The 13th century AH begins on the Islamic calendar on the 1st of Muharram 1201 AH The settlement of Östersund is established in Sweden.

1786.title: Östersund
1786.body: The settlement of Östersund is established in Sweden.

1786.title: Poems, Chiefly in the Scottish Dialect
1786.body: The 'Kilmarnock volume' of Robert Burns' Poems, Chiefly in the Scottish Dialect is published in Scotland.

1787.title: Caroline Herschel
1787.body: Caroline Herschel is granted an annual salary of £50, by King George III of Great Britain, for acting as assistant to her brother William in astronomy.

1787.title: Philadelphia
1787.body: In Philadelphia, delegates begin to convene the Constitutional Convention, intended to amend the Articles of Confederation (however, a new United States Constitution is eventually produced). George Washington presides over the Convention.

1787.title: Delaware
1787.body: Delaware ratifies the Constitution, and becomes the first U.S. state.

1787.title: Pennsylvania
1787.body: Pennsylvania becomes the second U.S. state.

1787.title: New Jersey
1787.body: New Jersey becomes the third U.S. state.

1788.title: George III
1788.body: October – King George III of the United Kingdom becomes deranged; the Regency Crisis of 1788 starts.

1788.title: The Times
1788.body: The first edition of The Times, previously The Daily Universal Register, is published in London.

1788.title: Georgia
1788.body: Georgia ratifies the United States Constitution, and becomes the fourth U.S. state under the new government.

1788.title: Connecticut
1788.body: Connecticut ratifies the United States Constitution, and becomes the fifth U.S. state.

1788.title: Massachusetts
1788.body: Massachusetts ratifies the United States Constitution, and becomes the sixth U.S. state.

1789.title: George Washington
1789.body: George Washington is unanimously elected the first president of the United States, by the United States Electoral College.

1789.title: Vietnam
1789.body: In Vietnam, Emperor Quang Trung crushes the Chinese Qing forces in Ngọc Hồi-Đống Đa. It is considered one of the greatest victories in Vietnamese military history.

1789.title: Alexander Hamilton
1789.body: Alexander Hamilton is appointed as the first United States Secretary of the Treasury.

1789.title: United States House of Representatives
1789.body: At Federal Hall, the United States House of Representatives attains its first quorum, and elects congressman Frederick Muhlenberg as the first Speaker of the House.

1789.title: United States Bill of Rights
1789.body: A proposal for a Bill of Rights is adopted by the United States House of Representatives.

1790.title: Vermont
1790.body: Commissioners appointed by the New York legislature announce the successful conclusion of negotiations between New York and Vermont, concerning disputed real-estate claims, and the consent of New York's legislature to the admission to the Union of the state of Vermont as the 14th State (which was formed within what New York claimed as its territory, under an Order in Council, that King George III issued on July 20, 1764).

1790.title: George Washington
1790.body: United States President George Washington gives the first State of the Union address, in New York City.

1790.title: Battle of Andros
1790.body: Battle of Andros: An Ottoman–Algerian fleet destroys the fleet of the Greek privateer Lambros Katsonis.

1790.title: Alexander Hamilton
1790.body: Alexander Hamilton's Assumption Bill, giving effect to his First Report on the Public Credit, is passed in the United States Congress, allowing the federal government to assume the consolidated debts of the U.S. states.

1790.title: Residence Act
1790.body: S. President George Washington signs the Residence Act into law, establishing a site along the Potomac River as the District of Columbia and the future site of the capital of the United States. The move comes after the bill is narrowly approved on July 1 by the Senate, 14 to 12, and on July 9 by the House, 32 to 29.

1791.title: Washington, D.C.
1791.body: The capital of the United States, Washington, D.C., is named after the incumbent 1st President George Washington.

1791.title: Vermont
1791.body: Vermont is admitted as the 14th U.S. state.

1791.title: opera seria
1791.body: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's opera seria, La clemenza di Tito, premières at the Estates Theatre in Prague with Mozart conducting to mark the coronation of Leopold II as King of Bohemia.

1791.title: The Magic Flute
1791.body: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's singspiel opera The Magic Flute (Die Zauberflöte) premières at the Freihaus-Theater auf der Wieden in Vienna with Mozart conducting.

1791.title: Holy Roman Empire
1791.body: Holy Roman troops reenter Liège, heralding the end of the Liège Revolution, and the restoration of its Prince-Bishops.

1792.title: Postal Service Act
1792.body: February 20 The Postal Service Act, establishing the United States Post Office Department, is signed by President George Washington. Parliament House, Dublin catches fire during a legislative session. "Although in imminent danger of the roof falling in," it is noted later, "the House did not adjourn until a proper motion had been put and carried in the affirmative."

1792.title: George Washington
1792.body: United States President George Washington vetoes a bill designed to apportion representatives among U.S. states. This is the first time the presidential veto is used in the United States.

1792.title: November 6 War of the First Coalition
1792.body: November 6 War of the First Coalition: Battle of Jemappes – Austrian armies under the command of Duke Albert of Saxe-Teschen are defeated in Belgium (at this time part of the Austrian Netherlands) by the French Army led by General Charles François Dumouriez. The second United States presidential election is held. Incumbent President George Washington receives all 132 electoral votes for president, and incumbent Vice President John Adams is re-elected with 77 of 132 votes, with George Clinton receiving 50.

1792.title: Treaty of Jassy
1792.body: The Treaty of Jassy ends the Russian Empire's war with the Ottoman Empire over Crimea.

1792.title: Washington, D.C.
1792.body: Foundation of Washington, D.C.: The cornerstone of the United States Executive Mansion (known as the White House after 1818) is laid.

1793.title: George Washington
1793.body: George Washington holds the first Cabinet meeting as President of the United States.

1793.title: French Revolution
1793.body: French Revolution: Marie Antoinette, the widowed queen consort of Louis XVI, is guillotined in the Place de la Révolution in Paris at the conclusion of a 2-day trial before the Revolutionary Tribunal.

1793.title: French Revolutionary Wars
1793.body: French Revolutionary Wars: The French First Republic declares war on Great Britain, the Dutch Republic and (on March 7) Spain. During the year, the War of the First Coalition is joined by Portugal, the Holy Roman Empire, Naples and Tuscany in opposition to France.

1793.title: United States House of Representatives
1793.body: The Giles Resolutions are introduced to the United States House of Representatives, asking the House to condemn Alexander Hamilton's handling of loans.

1793.title: Citizen Capet
1793.body: French Revolution: After being found guilty of treason by the French National Convention, Citizen Capet, formerly King Louis XVI, is guillotined in Paris.Second Partition of Poland in 1793

1794.title: Napoleon
1794.body: Napoleon is arrested and put under house arrest at Nice for his association with the Robespierres during their fall in the Thermidorian Reaction. He is later taken to Antibes and detained in a military fort.

1794.title: George III
1794.body: King George III of Great Britain delivers the speech opening Parliament and recommends a continuation of Britain's war with France.

1794.title: Sweden
1794.body: Aristocrats in Sweden gather to mourn the demise of coffee after the beverage is forbidden by royal decree.

1794.title: French Revolution
1794.body: French Revolution: The National Convention of the French First Republic abolishes slavery.

1794.title: July 27 (9 Thermidor) – French Revolution – Thermidorian Reaction
1794.body: July 27 (9 Thermidor) – French Revolution – Thermidorian Reaction: Maximilien, Augustin Robespierre and Saint-Just are arrested on the orders of the French National Convention; they are executed the next day, ending the French Revolution's Reign of Terror.

1795.title: 13 Vendémiaire
1795.body: 13 Vendémiaire: Royalist riots in Paris are crushed by troops under Paul Barras and newly-reinstalled artillery officer Napoleon Bonaparte.

1795.title: George
1795.body: George, Prince of Wales, marries Caroline of Brunswick.

1795.title: Governor-General of India
1795.body: April 23 Former Governor-General of India Warren Hastings is acquitted by the British House of Lords of misconduct. Sweden becomes the first monarchy to recognize the French Republic - Swedish ambassador introduced into the French Convention.

1795.title: Sweden
1795.body: Sweden becomes the first monarchy to recognize the French Republic - Swedish ambassador introduced into the French Convention.

1796.title: Farewell Address
1796.body: U.S. President George Washington issues his Farewell Address, which warns against partisan politics and foreign entanglements. In addition, he sets a precedent by declining to run for a third term.

1796.title: Venice
1796.body: June 1 The French-Republican army divisions of the Army of Italy invade the territories of Venice. Tennessee is admitted as the 16th U.S. state.

1796.title: Tennessee
1796.body: Tennessee is admitted as the 16th U.S. state.

1796.title: Catherine the Great
1796.body: Empress Catherine the Great signs an agreement with Great Britain, formally joining Russia to the coalition.

1797.title: Feldzeugmeister
1797.body: War of the First Coalition – Battle of Rivoli: French forces under General Napoleon Bonaparte defeat an Austrian army of 28,000 men, under Feldzeugmeister József Alvinczi, near Rivoli (modern-day Italy), ending Austria's fourth and final attempt to relieve the fortress city of Mantua.

1797.title: Battle of Faenza
1797.body: Battle of Faenza: A French corps (9,000 men) under General Claude Victor-Perrin defeats the forces from the Papal States, at Castel Bolognese near Faenza, Italy.

1797.title: Armistice of Leoben
1797.body: Armistice of Leoben: On behalf of the French Republic, a delegation under Napoleon Bonaparte signs a peace treaty with the Holy Roman Empire at Leoben.

1797.title: Treaty of Tolentino
1797.body: Treaty of Tolentino: Pope Pius VI signs a peace treaty with Revolutionary France. He is forced to deliver works of art, treasures, territory, the Comtat Venaissin and 30 million francs.

1797.title: United States
1797.body: The first ship of the United States Navy, the frigate USS United States, is commissioned.

1798.title: L'Orient
1798.body: Napoleon sets off for Toulon, sailing aboard Vice-Admiral Brueys's flagship L'Orient; his squadron is part of a larger fleet of over 300 vessels, carrying almost 37,000 troops.

1798.title: Battle of the Pyramids
1798.body: Battle of the Pyramids: Napoleon defeats Ottoman forces near the Pyramids.

1798.title: coup d'état
1798.body: June 12 The French take Malta. A moderate coup d'état in the Netherlands (Batavian Republic) deposes Pieter Vreede.

1798.title: Delaware
1798.body: July 7 Quasi-War: The United States Congress rescinds treaties with France, sparking the war. In the action of USS Delaware vs La Croyable, the newly-formed United States Navy makes its first capture.

1799.title: War of the Second Coalition
1799.body: War of the Second Coalition: Siege of Jaffa – Napoleon captures Jaffa in Palestine from the Ottomans and his troops proceed to kill more than 2,000 Albanian captives.

1799.title: Siege of Acre
1799.body: The Siege of Acre ends after two months; Napoleon's attempt to widen his Middle Eastern campaign into Syria is frustrated by Ottoman forces, and he withdraws to Egypt.

1799.title: Aboukir
1799.body: At Aboukir, Egypt, Napoleon defeats 10,000 Ottoman Mamluk troops under Mustafa Pasha.

1799.title: coup d'état
1799.body: Coup of 18 Brumaire: Napoleon overthrows the French Directory in a coup d'état, which ends the French Revolution.

1799.title: French Revolutionary Wars
1799.body: French Revolutionary Wars: At the Battle of Mount Tabor severely outnumbered French forces repulse an Ottoman attack.

1800.title: 1800 French constitutional referendum
1800.body: A public plebiscite in France confirms Napoleon as First Consul, by a substantial majority.

1800.title: Napoleon
1800.body: Napoleon and his French army (40,000 men)—not including the field artillery and baggage trains—(35,000 light artillery and infantry, 5,000 cavalry) begin crossing the Alps. He selects the shortest route through the Great St Bernard Pass, and invades after five days traversing the northern region of Italy.

1800.title: John Adams
1800.body: November 1 U.S. President John Adams becomes the first President of the United States to live in the Executive Mansion (later renamed the White House). Middlebury College is granted its charter by the Vermont General Assembly.

1800.title: December 3 War of the Second Coalition
1800.body: December 3 War of the Second Coalition: Battle of Hohenlinden – The French army defeats Habsburg and Bavarian troops. The 1800 United States presidential election: The Electoral College casts votes for president and Vice President that results in a tie between Thomas Jefferson and Aaron Burr, requiring a contingent election which will select Jefferson as president.

1800.title: The 1800 United States presidential election
1800.body: The 1800 United States presidential election: The Electoral College casts votes for president and Vice President that results in a tie between Thomas Jefferson and Aaron Burr, requiring a contingent election which will select Jefferson as president.

1801.title: Napoleon
1801.body: Napoleon signs a Concordat with Pope Pius VII.

1801.title: Thomas Bruce, 7th Earl of Elgin
1801.body: Thomas Bruce, 7th Earl of Elgin, British ambassador to the Ottoman Empire, begins removal of the Elgin Marbles from the Parthenon in Athens.

1801.title: Electoral College
1801.body: An electoral tie between Thomas Jefferson and Aaron Burr is resolved, when Jefferson is elected President of the United States and Burr Vice President by the United States House of Representatives.

1801.title: Enterprise
1801.body: First Barbary War: Action of 1 August 1801 – United States Navy schooner USS Enterprise (1799) captures the 14-gun Tripolitan corsair polacca Tripoli off the north African coast, in a single-ship action.

1801.title: War of the Second Coalition
1801.body: War of the Second Coalition: First Battle of Copenhagen – The British Royal Navy, under Admiral Sir Hyde Parker, forces the Royal Dano-Norwegian Navy to accept an armistice. Vice-Admiral Horatio Nelson leads the main attack, deliberately disregarding his commander's signal to withdraw. He is created a Viscount on May 19; Denmark-Norway is forced to withdraw from the Second League of Armed Neutrality.

1802.title: Abdul-Aziz bin Muhammad
1802.body: About 12,000 Wahhabi Sunnis under the command of Abdul-Aziz bin Muhammad, the second ruler of the First Saudi State attack and sack Karbala, kill between 2,000 and 5,000 inhabitants and plunder the tomb of Husayn ibn Ali, grandson of Muhammad and son of Ali ibn Abi Talib.

1802.title: Ancien Régime
1802.body: A general amnesty signed by Napoleon allows all but about 1,000 of the most notorious émigrés of the French Revolution to return to France as part of a conciliatory gesture to make peace with the various factions of the Ancien Régime that ultimately consolidates his own rule.

1802.title: Law of 20 May 1802
1802.body: By the Law of 20 May 1802, Napoleon reinstates slavery in the French colonies, revoking its abolition in the French Revolution.

1802.title: Referendum
1802.body: In a plebiscite, Napoleon Bonaparte is confirmed as the First Consul of France.

1802.title: Mentor
1802.body: Thomas Bruce, 7th Earl of Elgin, British ambassador to the Ottoman Empire, begins removal of the Elgin Marbles from the Parthenon in Athens, claiming they are at risk of destruction during the Ottoman occupation of Greece; the first shipment departs Piraeus on board Elgin's ship, the Mentor, "with many boxes of moulds and sculptures", including three marble torsos from the Parthenon.

1803.title: Napoleon
1803.body: May – First Consul of France, Citizen Bonaparte, begins making preparations to invade England.

1803.title: Haitian Revolution
1803.body: Haitian Revolution: Battle of Vertières – The Haitian army, led by Jean-Jacques Dessalines, defeats the French army sent by Napoleon.

1803.title: Balmis Expedition
1803.body: November 30 The Balmis Expedition starts in Spain, with the aim of vaccinating millions against smallpox in Spanish America and the Philippines. At the Cabildo in New Orleans, Spanish representatives Governor Manuel de Salcedo and the Marqués de Casa Calvo officially transfer Louisiana (New Spain) to French representative Prefect Pierre Clément de Laussat. Barely three weeks later, France transfers the same land to the United States.

1803.title: Reichsdeputationshauptschluss
1803.body: A major redistribution of territorial sovereignty within the Holy Roman Empire is enacted, via an act known as the Reichsdeputationshauptschluss.

1803.title: Ludwig van Beethoven
1803.body: Ludwig van Beethoven puts on a big Akademie concert at the Theater an der Wien, in Vienna consisting of the premieres of his second symphony, third piano concerto, and his oratorio Christ on the Mount of Olives.

1804.title: Napoleon
1804.body: Napoleon Bonaparte is proclaimed Emperor of the French by the French Senate.

1804.title: Coronation of Napoleon
1804.body: Coronation of Napoleon I: At the cathedral of Notre Dame de Paris, Napoleon crowns himself as the first Emperor of the French in a thousand years. Witnessing this, Simón Bolívar dedicates himself to liberating Venezuela from Spanish rule.

1804.title: First Serbian uprising
1804.body: The First Serbian uprising begins the Serbian Revolution. By 1817, the Principality of Serbia will have proclaimed self-rule from the Ottoman Empire, the first nation-state in Europe to do so.

1804.title: Twelfth Amendment to the United States Constitution
1804.body: The Twelfth Amendment to the U.S. Constitution, which reforms the way that candidates for President and Vice President are chosen, is ratified by Tennessee, removing doubt surrounding adoption.

1804.title: Aaron Burr
1804.body: Aaron Burr, Vice President of the United States, shoots former U.S. Secretary of the Treasury Alexander Hamilton during a duel; Hamilton dies the next day.

1805.title: Rutger Jan Schimmelpenninck
1805.body: Rutger Jan Schimmelpenninck is appointed as Grand Pensionary of the Batavian Republic by Napoleon.

1805.title: Napoleon
1805.body: Napoleon is crowned King of Italy.

1805.title: Napoleonic Wars
1805.body: Napoleonic Wars: Battle of Austerlitz – French troops under Napoleon decisively defeat a joint Russo-Austrian force.

1805.title: French Republican Calendar
1805.body: The French Republican Calendar (which featured a 10-day week until 1802) is used for the last time, 8 days after being annulled by Napoleon, with the final official date being "9 Nivôse in Year XIV of the Revolution".

1805.title: Samuel Chase
1805.body: Justice Samuel Chase is acquitted of impeachment charges by the United States Senate.

1806.title: French Republican Calendar
1806.body: January 1 The French Republican Calendar is abolished. The Kingdom of Bavaria is established by Napoleon.

1806.title: Kingdom of Bavaria
1806.body: The Kingdom of Bavaria is established by Napoleon.

1806.title: Louis Bonaparte
1806.body: Louis Bonaparte is appointed as King of Holland by his brother, Emperor Napoleon, replacing the Batavian Republic.

1806.title: Battle of Jena–Auerstedt
1806.body: Battle of Jena–Auerstedt: At Jena, Napoleon defeats the Prussian army of Prince Hohenlohe, while at Auerstedt, Marshal Davout defeats the main Prussian army under Karl Wilhelm Ferdinand, Duke of Brunswick (who is fatally wounded).

1806.title: Napoleon
1806.body: Napoleon's Berlin Decree initiates the Continental System, blocking the import of British manufactured goods to the rest of Europe.

1807.title: Napoleon
1807.body: Napoleon leads the forces of the French Empire in an invasion of the Russian Empire, and begins fighting at the Battle of Eylau against Russian and Prussian forces.

1807.title: Battle of Eylau
1807.body: Battle of Eylau: Napoleon fights a hard but inconclusive battle against the Russians under Bennigsen.

1807.title: Battle of Friedland
1807.body: Battle of Friedland: Napoleon decisively defeats Bennigsen's Russian army.

1807.title: Treaties of Tilsit
1807.body: The Treaties of Tilsit are signed between France, Prussia and Russia. Napoleon and Russian Emperor Alexander I ally together against the British. The Prussians are forced to cede more than half their territory, which is formed into the Duchy of Warsaw in their former Polish lands, and the Kingdom of Westphalia in western Germany.

1807.title: Nicéphore Niépce
1807.body: Nicéphore Niépce is awarded a patent by Emperor Napoleon for the Pyréolophore, the world's first internal combustion engine, after it successfully powers a boat upstream on the river Saône in France.

1808.title: Act Prohibiting Importation of Slaves
1808.body: January 1 The importation of slaves into the United States is formally banned, as the 1807 Act Prohibiting Importation of Slaves takes effect. However Americans still continue the slave trade by transporting Africans to Cuba and Brazil. Sierra Leone becomes a British Crown Colony.

1808.title: Transfer of the Portuguese court to Brazil
1808.body: Transfer of the Portuguese court to Brazil: John (Dom João), Prince Regent, and the Braganza royal family of Portugal arrive in their colony of Brazil in exile from the French occupation of their home kingdom.

1808.title: Finnish War
1808.body: February 21 The Finnish War begins as Russian troops cross the border into Finland without a declaration of war. Russia issues an ultimatum to Sweden, to join Napoleon's Continental System against the United Kingdom.

1808.title: Sweden
1808.body: Russia issues an ultimatum to Sweden, to join Napoleon's Continental System against the United Kingdom.

1808.title: Rome
1808.body: French troops take Rome as part of the Napoleonic Wars.

1809.title: Battle of Abensberg, Bavaria
1809.body: Battle of Abensberg, Bavaria: Napoleon defeats Austria.

1809.title: Battle of Landshut
1809.body: Battle of Landshut: Napoleon defeats Austria, in yet another battle.

1809.title: Battle of Aspern-Essling
1809.body: Battle of Aspern-Essling: Austrian troops under Archduke Karl beat the French under Napoleon, in a hard-fought battle.

1809.title: Treaty of the Dardanelles
1809.body: The Treaty of the Dardanelles, between the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland and the Ottoman Empire, is concluded.

1809.title: Apodaca–Canning treaty
1809.body: The Apodaca–Canning treaty is signed in London between Britain and Spain

1810.title: Napoleon
1810.body: The marriage of Napoleon and Joséphine is annulled.

1810.title: Patria Boba
1810.body: First Republic of New Granada: Patria Boba: A junta of seven patriots, led by José Acevedo y Gómez, assemble in Bogotá in the Viceroyalty of New Granada (modern-day Colombia), to declare its independence from the Spanish Empire, the first South American nation to break away from Spain as the third oldest independent republic state after Haiti.

1810.title: Venezuela achieves home rule
1810.body: Venezuela achieves home rule: Vicente Emparán, Governor of the Captaincy General of Venezuela, is removed by the people of Caracas, and Supreme Junta is installed. Venezuela is the first South American state to proclaim independence from Spain.

1810.title: Primera Junta
1810.body: May Revolution: Armed citizens of Buenos Aires expel the Viceroy and establish a provincial government for Argentina (the Primera Junta). Baltasar Hidalgo de Cisneros is removed.

1810.title: Santa Cruz de Mompox
1810.body: The city of Santa Cruz de Mompox, in modern-day Colombia, declares independence from the Spanish Empire.

1811.title: Juliana
1811.body: Inventor John Stevens' boat, the Juliana, begins operation as the first steam-powered ferry service, between New York City and Hoboken, New Jersey.

1811.title: Carlton House Fête
1811.body: The Carlton House Fête is held at the Prince Regent's London residence Carlton House to celebrate the establishment of the Regency era.

1811.title: Great Podil fire
1811.body: The Great Podil fire breaks out in Kiev, Ukraine.

1811.title: Tyrol
1811.body: Forest fires break out in the Tyrol region of Switzerland, reportedly destroying 64 villages and hamlets.

1811.title: Citadel Massacre in Cairo
1811.body: Citadel Massacre in Cairo: Egyptian ruler Muhammad Ali kills the last Mamluk leaders.

1812.title: Mesures usuelles
1812.body: Napoleon authorizes the usage of Mesures usuelles, the basis of the metric system.

1812.title: Napoleon
1812.body: Napoleon's Grande Armée crosses the Neman River, and invades Russia.

1812.title: French invasion of Russia and Fire of Moscow
1812.body: French invasion of Russia and Fire of Moscow: Napoleon's troops enter Moscow, which is deliberately set on fire by Muscovites, on orders of Fyodor Rostopchin. Later accounts report that France lost 40,000 troops during four days of fire between September 17 and 20, and that 20,000 Russian soldiers were killed in what would be described in 1876 as "the greatest example in history of national self-sacrifice for the destruction of an invader."

1812.title: Napoleonic Wars – Battle of Maloyaroslavets
1812.body: Napoleonic Wars – Battle of Maloyaroslavets: An inconclusive encounter between the French vanguard and a Russian force leads Napoleon to decide to retreat along the same line as his advance, with disastrous results.

1812.title: Louisiana
1812.body: Louisiana is admitted as the 18th U.S. state.

1813.title: Napoleonic Wars
1813.body: Napoleonic Wars: Battle of Lützen – Napoleon wins against the German alliance.

1813.title: Napoleonic Wars – Battle of Großbeeren
1813.body: Napoleonic Wars – Battle of Großbeeren: Napoleon is defeated by Prussia and Sweden.

1813.title: Napoleonic Wars – Battle of Katzbach
1813.body: Napoleonic Wars – Battle of Katzbach: Napoleon's troops are defeated by Prussia and Russia.

1813.title: Napoleonic Wars – Battle of Dresden
1813.body: Napoleonic Wars – Battle of Dresden: Napoleon's troops are victorious.

1813.title: Napoleonic Wars – Battle of Dennewitz
1813.body: Napoleonic Wars – Battle of Dennewitz: The armies of Napoleon are again defeated by Prussia and Russia.

1814.title: War of the Sixth Coalition – Battle of Brienne
1814.body: War of the Sixth Coalition – Battle of Brienne: A French army led by Napoleon is victorious against von Blücher.

1814.title: The Corsair
1814.body: February 1 Mount Mayon in the Philippines erupts for the second time; more than 1,200 people die and the Cagsawa Church is destroyed. Lord Byron's semi-autobiographical tale in verse The Corsair is published by John Murray in London, and sells 10,000 copies on this day. War of the Sixth Coalition – Battle of La Rothière: Blücher's Allied army defeats the French under Napoleon.

1814.title: War of the Sixth Coalition – Battle of La Rothière
1814.body: War of the Sixth Coalition – Battle of La Rothière: Blücher's Allied army defeats the French under Napoleon.

1814.title: War of the Sixth Coalition – Battle of Champaubert
1814.body: War of the Sixth Coalition – Battle of Champaubert: A French army led by Napoleon effectively destroys a small Russian corps led by Zakhar Dmitrievich Olsufiev, opening the Six Days' Campaign.

1814.title: Kalmar Union
1814.body: February 11 Norway's independence is proclaimed, marking the ultimate end of the Kalmar Union. War of the Sixth Coalition – Battle of Montmirail: A French army led by Napoleon is victorious against Fabian Gottlieb von der Osten-Sacken and Ludwig Yorck von Wartenburg in the Six Days' Campaign.

1815.title: Napoleonic Wars
1815.body: Napoleonic Wars: Napoleon Bonaparte escapes from Elba.

1815.title: March 1 Napoleonic Wars
1815.body: March 1 Napoleonic Wars: Napoleon returns to France from his banishment on Elba. Georgetown University's congressional charter is signed into law, by President James Madison.

1815.title: Congress of Vienna
1815.body: Participants at the Congress of Vienna declare Napoleon an outlaw following his escape from Elba

1815.title: Hundred Days
1815.body: Napoleonic Wars: Napoleon enters Paris, after escaping from Elba with a regular army of 140,000 and a volunteer force of around 200,000, beginning his Hundred Days rule.

1815.title: Battle of Ligny
1815.body: Battle of Ligny – Napoleon defeats a Prussian army under Gebhard Leberecht von Blücher.

1816.title: United Provinces of the Río de la Plata
1816.body: The United Provinces of South America (today Argentina, Uruguay, Bolivia and southern Brazil) declares independence from Spain.

1816.title: Indiana
1816.body: Indiana is admitted as the 19th U.S. state.

1816.title: Gorkha War
1816.body: The Gorkha War between the United Kingdom and Nepal is ended after more than a year by the ratification of the Treaty of Sugauli, with Nepal ceding about one-third of its territory to British Indian control.

1816.title: Leopold I of Belgium
1816.body: Leopold of Saxe-Coburg (later King of the Belgians) marries Charlotte Augusta, daughter of the Prince Regent, at Carlton House in London.

1816.title: Cherokee Nation
1816.body: S. in 1814 by the Creek Nation. S.

1817.title: Ferdinand VII of Spain
1817.body: King Ferdinand VII of Spain, by royal decree, makes the production and sale of tobacco a legal endeavor in Cuba, thus sparking the birth of the Cuban cigar industry.

1817.title: Ram Mohan Roy
1817.body: Ram Mohan Roy and David Hare found Hindu College, Calcutta, offering instructions in English on Western subjects, including other European languages.

1817.title: Town
1817.body: The town of Araraquara, Brazil, is founded.

1817.title: Great Rebellion of 1817-18
1817.body: The Great Rebellion of 1817-18 begins in Sri Lanka.

1817.title: Alabama Territory
1817.body: By act of the U.S. Congress (March 3), the Alabama Territory is created by splitting the Mississippi Territory in half, on the day the Mississippi constitution is drafted, four months before Mississippi becomes a U.S. state.

1818.title: William
1818.body: Prince William, Duke of Clarence and St Andrews, third oldest son of King George III and the future King William IV of the United Kingdom, marries Adelaide of Saxe-Meiningen.

1818.title: Stamford Raffles
1818.body: September – Sir Stamford Raffles sets out to visit Lord Hastings, Governor-General of India, to gain his approval to establish a trading station at the southern tip of the Malay Peninsula (modern-day Singapore).

1818.title: Illinois
1818.body: Illinois is admitted as the 21st U.S. state.

1818.title: Switzerland
1818.body: At least 34 people are killed in Switzerland, when the melting of a glacier releases the natural dam of Lac de Mauvoisin, sending the waters of the lake and the Dranse River into the valley of Bagnes.

1818.title: The Old Vic Theatre
1818.body: May 11 Charles XIV of Sweden–Norway is crowned king of Sweden. The Old Vic Theatre is founded (as the Royal Coburg Hall) in London. The Westmorland Gazette is first published at Kendal in the Lake District of England; in July, Thomas De Quincey will begin a 16-month term as editor.

1819.title: Stamford Raffles
1819.body: Sir Stamford Raffles lands on the island of Singapore.

1819.title: 1819 Singapore Treaty
1819.body: The Treaty of Singapore, is signed between Hussein Shah of Johor and Sir Stamford Raffles of Britain, to create a trading settlement in Singapore.

1819.title: Cienfuegos
1819.body: The city of Fernandina de Jagua (later Cienfuegos) is founded in Cuba.

1819.title: Thomas Jefferson
1819.body: Thomas Jefferson founds the University of Virginia.

1819.title: United States House of Representatives
1819.body: The United States House of Representatives agrees to the Tallmadge Amendment, barring slaves from the new state of Missouri (the opening vote in a controversy that leads to the Missouri Compromise).

1820.title: Filiki Eteria
1820.body: Alexander Ypsilantis becomes the leader of Filiki Eteria, a secret organization to overthrow Ottoman rule over Greece.

1820.title: Cato Street Conspiracy
1820.body: The Cato Street Conspiracy, a plot to assassinate Britain's Prime Minister, Lord Liverpool, and his Cabinet is thwarted when police in London arrest 13 plotters after being warned by an informant.

1820.title: Minh Mạng
1820.body: Emperor Minh Mạng starts to rule in Vietnam.

1820.title: Missouri Compromise
1820.body: March 3 and 6 – The Missouri Compromise becomes law, allowing admission of Missouri and Maine, as slave and free states respectively, as U.S. states.

1820.title: Maine
1820.body: Maine is admitted as the 23rd U.S. state.

1821.title: Manchester Guardian
1821.body: May 5 Emperor Napoleon dies in exile on Saint Helena of stomach cancer. The first edition of the Manchester Guardian newspaper (from 1959 simply The Guardian) is published in England.

1821.title: Napoleon
1821.body: Emperor Napoleon dies in exile on Saint Helena of stomach cancer.

1821.title: Julian calendar
1821.body: March 25 (O.S.)/April 6 (N.S.) – Metropolitan bishop Germanos of Patras raises the revolutionary flag of Greece at the Monastery of Agia Lavra (according to oral tradition), symbolically marking the beginning of the Greek War of Independence against the Ottoman Empire; later celebrated as Greece's traditional Independence Day.

1821.title: Egyptian conquest of Sudan (1820–1824)
1821.body: Egyptian conquest of Sudan (1820–1824): King Badi VII of Sennar surrenders his throne and realm without a fight to Ismail Pasha, general of the Ottoman Empire, ending the existence of the Funj Sultanate in Sudan.

1821.title: Mexico
1821.body: In Mexico, the Embrace of Acatempan takes place between Agustín de Iturbide and Vicente Guerrero, which seals the peace between the viceroyalty troops and the insurgents.

1822.title: Holy Alliance
1822.body: March 19 The Holy Alliance sends the Ottoman Empire a final ultimatum after Ottoman repression of Austrian subjects in Bucharest during the Wallachian uprising, otherwise facing war with Austria. Boston, Massachusetts, is incorporated as a city following a vote.

1822.title: Greek War of Independence – Chios massacre
1822.body: Greek War of Independence – Chios massacre: 20,000 Greeks on the island of Chios are slaughtered by Ottoman troops, and 23,000 more are exiled.

1822.title: Greek War of Independence
1822.body: Greek War of Independence: Greek soldiers defeat Ottoman forces at Thermopylae.

1822.title: 1822 Aleppo earthquake
1822.body: A sequence of earthquakes occurs in the northern part of the Ottoman Empire, causing severe damage from Gaziantep to Antakya in modern Turkey and from Aleppo to Khan Sheikhun in modern Syria. At least 20,000 people are killed.

1822.title: Alligator
1822.body: Action of 9 November 1822: USS Alligator (1820) engages three pirate schooners off Cuba, as part of the West Indies anti-piracy operations of the United States.

1823.title: Gold
1823.body: February 15 (approx.) – The first officially recognised gold is found in Australia, by surveyor James McBrien at Fish River, near Bathurst, New South Wales, predating the Australian gold rushes.

1823.title: Raffles Institution
1823.body: Raffles Institution is established (as the Singapore Institution) by the founder of Singapore, Sir Stamford Raffles.

1823.title: Congress of Verona
1823.body: By secret treaty signed at the Congress of Verona, the Quintuple Alliance gives France a mandate to invade Spain for the purpose of restoring Ferdinand VII (who has been captured by armed revolutionary liberals) as absolute monarch of the country.

1823.title: Assembleia Geral
1823.body: Emperor Pedro I of Brazil inaugurates Brazil's first Assembleia Geral, with 50 Senators and 102 Deputies.

1823.title: Mikhail Semyonovich Vorontsov
1823.body: Mikhail Semyonovich Vorontsov is appointed as Governor-General of Novorossiya (New Russia), the portion of Russian Empire bordering the Black Sea (in modern days it constitutes southern regions of Ukraine).

1824.title: Gordon Bremer
1824.body: The earliest reference by the British government to an official renaming of its South Pacific Ocean territory as "Australia" comes in a dispatch titled "Taking Possession of Melville and Bathurst Islands", as the Admiral Sir Gordon Bremer refers to "the North Coast of New Holland or Australia".

1824.title: Chumash Revolt of 1824
1824.body: The Chumash Revolt of 1824 begins against the Spanish presence in Alta California (now the U.S. state of California) as an uprising by the Chumash people at Mission Santa Inés (now Solvang, Mission Santa Barbara, and Mission La Purisima (now Lompoc in what is now Santa Barbara County, California. By June 28, the uprising ends after the Spanish government and the Chumash leaders reach a peace agreement.

1824.title: First Anglo-Burmese War
1824.body: The First Anglo-Burmese War begins in much of what is now Bangladesh, and lasts for almost two years until the surrender of territories by the Kingdom of Burma on February 24, 1826.

1824.title: Kamehameha
1824.body: King Kamehameha II of Hawaii dies of measles, during a visit to the United Kingdom, before he can meet with King George IV. Because of the slow communications of the era, news of the King's death does not reach Hawaii until the following March; his funeral would then take place on May 11, 1825, and subsequently he is succeeded by his brother Kamehameha III.

1824.title: 1824 United States presidential election
1824.body: S. president— Andrew Jackson, John Quincy Adams, Henry Clay or William H. 5% of the popular vote.

1825.title: Minh Mạng
1825.body: Minh Mạng outlaws the teaching of Christianity in Vietnam.

1825.title: United States Electoral College
1825.body: After no presidential candidate receives a majority of United States Electoral College votes following the 1824 United States presidential election, the United States House of Representatives elects John Quincy Adams President of the United States in a contingent election.

1825.title: Charles X of France
1825.body: Charles X of France recognizes Haiti, 21 years after it expelled the French following the successful Haitian Revolution, and demands the payment of 150 million gold francs, 30 million of which Haiti must finance through France itself, as down payment.

1825.title: Mexico City
1825.body: The National Mexican Rite is created in Mexico City.

1825.title: Uruguay
1825.body: Uruguay is declared independent of the Empire of Brazil by the Thirty-Three Orientals, a militant revolutionary group led by Juan Antonio Lavalleja.

1826.title: The Auspicious Incident
1826.body: The Auspicious Incident: Mahmud II, sultan of the Ottoman Empire, crushes the last mutiny of janissaries in Istanbul.

1826.title: Thomas Jefferson
1826.body: Former U.S. Presidents Thomas Jefferson and John Adams both die on the 50th anniversary of the signing of the United States Declaration of Independence.

1826.title: Bernardino Rivadavia
1826.body: Unitarian Bernardino Rivadavia becomes the first President of the United Provinces of the Río de la Plata, now Argentina.

1826.title: Grosse Fuge
1826.body: March – Ludwig van Beethoven's String Quartet No. 13 in B♭ major, Op. 130 is first performed and is premiered by the Schuppanzigh Quartet.

1826.title: Ludwig van Beethoven
1826.body: July – Ludwig van Beethoven puts the finishing touches on the String Quartet No. 14 in C♯ minor, Op. 131, the jewel in the crown of his late string quartets.

1827.title: Zarafa
1827.body: Zarafa, the first giraffe to be seen in Europe for over three centuries, arrives in Marseille as a gift from the Ottoman Viceroy of Egypt, Mehmet Ali Pasha, to King Charles X of France. The giraffe then begins a 50-day journey to Paris, walking with its handlers toward Paris where it will arrive on July 9.

1827.title: Greek War of Independence
1827.body: Greek War of Independence: The Treaty of London between France, Britain and Russia, demands that the Turks agree to an armistice in Greece.

1827.title: Coahuila y Tejas
1827.body: March 11 The new state constitution for the Mexican state of Coahuila y Tejas is ratified, including a phasing-out of slavery in its Article 13, which declares that "From and after the promulgation of the constitution in the capital of each district, no one shall be born a slave in the state, and after six months the introduction of slaves under any pretext shall not be permitted." The prohibition of importing slaves from the United States is lifted when Texas declares independence in 1836, and the Republic of Texas Constitution will provide specifically that Africans and "the descendants of Africans" will not be considered "citizens of the republic". The predecessor of Qasr El Eyni Hospital and Cairo University School of Medicine is established in Egypt under the direction of Antoine Clot as the first medical school in the region.

1827.title: Qasr El Eyni Hospital
1827.body: The predecessor of Qasr El Eyni Hospital and Cairo University School of Medicine is established in Egypt under the direction of Antoine Clot as the first medical school in the region.

1827.title: Russo-Persian War, 1826–1828
1827.body: Russo-Persian War, 1826–1828: The Russians under Ivan Paskevich storm Yerevan, ending a millennium of Muslim domination in Armenia.

1828.title: Russo-Turkish War
1828.body: Russia declares war on the Ottoman Empire, beginning the Russo-Turkish War. The Treaty of Commerce and Navigation is signed between Brazil and Denmark, establishing diplomatic relations between the two countries.

1828.title: Brazil
1828.body: The Treaty of Commerce and Navigation is signed between Brazil and Denmark, establishing diplomatic relations between the two countries.

1828.title: Treaty of Turkmenchay
1828.body: Treaty of Turkmenchay: By this Russian-Persian peace treaty signed on February 10 at Torkamanchay, Persia (Iran), the latter country is forced irrevocably to cede the territories of the Erivan Khanate (most of modern-day central Armenia and the northern Iğdır Province of Turkey), the Nakhichevan Khanate (most of the modern-day Nakhchivan Autonomous Republic of Azerbaijan), the remainder of the Talysh Khanate (southeastern Azerbaijan), and the Ordubad and Mughan regions (also part of modern-day Azerbaijan) to Imperial Russia. By this and the Treaty of Gulistan (1813) it has now lost all its territories north of the Aras River, comprising modern-day Georgia, Dagestan, Azerbaijan and Armenia to Russia. Armenians from Persian Azerbaijan are to be resettled in the Caucasus.

1828.title: Simón Bolívar
1828.body: Simón Bolívar, President of Colombia (and former President of Venezuela, Peru and Bolivia), departs from the capital at Bogotá in order to help his ally, General José Antonio Páez, suppress an uprising near the Venezuelan border, but is sidetracked by another rebellion in Cartagena.

1828.title: Bahía Blanca
1828.body: The city of Bahía Blanca in modern-day Argentina is founded.

1829.title: Ottoman Empire
1829.body: Greece receives autonomy from the Ottoman Empire in the London Protocol, signed by Russia, France and Britain, effectively ending the Greek War of Independence. Greece continues to seek full independence through diplomatic negotiations with the three Great Powers.

1829.title: Pickle
1829.body: HMS Pickle captures the armed slave ship Voladora off the coast of Cuba.

1829.title: Wellington–Winchilsea duel
1829.body: The bloodless Wellington–Winchilsea duel takes place at Battersea near London

1829.title: Russo-Turkish War (1828–29)
1829.body: Russo-Turkish War (1828–29): Russian Field-Marshal Hans Karl von Diebitsch launches the Trans-Balkan Offensive, which brings the Russian army within 68 kilometres (42 mi) of Istanbul.

1829.title: Joseph Smith
1829.body: Joseph Smith claims to have received the Aaronic Priesthood from John the Baptist.

1830.title: London Protocol
1830.body: The London Protocol establishes the full independence and sovereignty of Greece from the Ottoman Empire, as the result of the Greek War of Independence.

1830.title: Argyll Rooms
