1830.body: A fire destroys the Argyll Rooms in London, where the Philharmonic Society of London presents concerts, but firefighters are able to prevent its further spread by use of their new equipment, steam-powered fire engines.

1830.title: William
1830.body: William IV succeeds his brother George IV, as King of the United Kingdom.

1830.title: Belgian Revolution
1830.body: Belgian Revolution: The Provisional Government in Brussels declares the creation of the independent state of Belgium.

1831.title: Bosnian uprising
1831.body: The Bosnian uprising (1831–32) against the Ottoman Empire begins.

1831.title: Brazil
1831.body: Slave trading is forbidden in Brazil.

1831.title: Ecuador
1831.body: Ecuador and Venezuela are separated from Gran Colombia.

1831.title: Egyptian–Ottoman War (1831–1833)
1831.body: Egyptian–Ottoman War (1831–1833): Egypt's forces occupy Ottoman Syria.

1831.title: Aimé Bonpland
1831.body: French-born botanical explorer Aimé Bonpland leaves Paraguay for Argentina.

1832.title: Egyptians
1832.body: The Egyptians, aided by Maronites, seize Acre from the Ottoman Empire after a 7-month siege.

1832.title: Battle of Konya
1832.body: Battle of Konya: The Egyptians defeat the main Ottoman army in central Anatolia.

1832.title: Siege of Antwerp
1832.body: Siege of Antwerp: The last remaining Dutch stronghold, Antwerp Citadel, comes under French attack in the aftermath of the Belgian Revolution.

1832.title: mutasallim
1832.body: Qasim al-Ahmad is appointed as the new Ottoman Governor (mutasallim) of Jerusalem (Kudüs), after Sultan Mahmud II dismisses Muhammad Said Agha.

1832.title: Watch
1832.body: Global watch brand Longines is founded in Switzerland.

1833.title: Amphitrite
1833.body: The chartered ship Amphitrite sinks off Boulogne-sur-Mer while transporting 108 British female convicts and 12 children from Woolwich in England to New South Wales in what is now Australia. All of the passengers die, along with 13 of the 16 crew, leaving 3 survivors.

1833.title: Carl Friedrich Gauss
1833.body: May 6 In Germany, Carl Friedrich Gauss and Wilhelm Weber obtain permission to build an electromagnetic telegraph in Göttingen. The Ottoman Empire promulgates a firman ratifying the Peace Agreement of Kütahya, bringing an end to the Egyptian-Ottoman War. S.

1833.title: Ottoman Empire
1833.body: The Ottoman Empire promulgates a firman ratifying the Peace Agreement of Kütahya, bringing an end to the Egyptian-Ottoman War.

1833.title: Treaty of Hünkâr İskelesi
1833.body: The Treaty of Hünkâr İskelesi creates an alliance between the Ottoman Empire and the Russian Empire.

1834.title: Anti-abolitionist riots
1834.body: Anti-abolitionist riots break out in New York City.

1834.title: William Lamb, 2nd Viscount Melbourne
1834.body: William Lamb, 2nd Viscount Melbourne becomes the last Prime Minister of the United Kingdom to be dismissed by the British monarch. King William IV temporarily appoints Arthur Wellesley, 1st Duke of Wellington, to form a caretaker government.

1834.title: Andrew Jackson
1834.body: Andrew Jackson is censured by the United States Congress (expunged in 1837).

1834.title: Slavery
1834.body: August 1 Slavery is abolished in the British Empire, by the Slavery Abolition Act 1833. Construction work begins on the Wilberforce Monument in Kingston Upon Hull.

1834.title: York, Upper Canada
1834.body: York, Upper Canada, is incorporated as Toronto.

1835.title: Texas Declaration of Independence
1835.body: The Texas Declaration of Independence is signed by American residents rebelling against Mexico at Goliad, Texas.

1835.title: Slavery
1835.body: Slavery is finally abolished in British-administered Mauritius, with compensation to slaveowners.

1835.title: Attempted assassination of Andrew Jackson
1835.body: Attempted assassination of Andrew Jackson: The first assassination attempt against a President of the United States is carried out against President Andrew Jackson at the United States Capitol in Washington, D.C., by English-born delusional unemployed house painter Richard Lawrence; the attempt fails when his pistols misfire due to humidity.

1835.title: Rail transport in Belgium
1835.body: May 5 The first railway in continental Europe is opened between Brussels and Mechelen in Belgium. Braulio Carrillo is sworn in as Head of State of Costa Rica.

1835.title: Maria II of Portugal
1835.body: January 26 Queen Maria II of Portugal marries Auguste de Beauharnais, 2nd Duke of Leuchtenberg, in Lisbon; he dies only two months later. Saint Paul's in Macau is largely destroyed by fire after a typhoon hits.

1836.title: Ancient Order of Hibernians
1836.body: The Ancient Order of Hibernians, an Irish Catholic fraternal organization, is founded in New York City.

1836.title: Hurva Synagogue
1836.body: Rebuilding begins at the Hurva Synagogue in Jerusalem.

1836.title: Davy Crockett
1836.body: Former U.S. Representative Davy Crockett of Tennessee arrives in Texas to join the Texan fight for independence from Mexico.

1836.title: Texas Revolution – Convention of 1836
1836.body: Texas Revolution – Convention of 1836: Delegates from many Texas communities gather in Washington-on-the-Brazos, Texas, to deliberate independence from Mexico.

1836.title: Texas Revolution – Battle of San Jacinto
1836.body: Texas Revolution – Battle of San Jacinto: Mexican forces under General Antonio López de Santa Anna are defeated at San Jacinto, Texas.

1837.title: Queen Victoria
1837.body: Queen Victoria, 18, accedes to the throne of the United Kingdom, on the death of her uncle King William IV without legitimate heirs (she will reign for more than 63 years). Under Salic law, the Kingdom of Hanover passes to William's brother, Ernest Augustus, Duke of Cumberland, ending the personal union of Britain and Hanover which has persisted since 1714.

1837.title: Panic of 1837
1837.body: The Panic of 1837 begins in New York City.

1837.title: Netherlands
1837.body: The Dutch colonial forces sack the fortress of Bonjol, Indonesia, ending the Padri War.

1837.title: Electric locomotive
1837.body: The first electric locomotive built is a miniature battery locomotive constructed by chemist Robert Davidson of Aberdeen in Scotland, and powered by galvanic cells (batteries).

1837.title: Seminole
1837.body: Seminoles attack Fort Foster in Florida.

1838.title: Danube River
1838.body: A combination of rain and melting snow causes the Danube River to overflow its banks, washing away villages in western Hungary and inundating the twin cities that become Budapest. More than 150 people are drowned and Europe's nations come to Hungary's aid to prevent the spread of famine and disease.

1838.title: December – First Anglo-Afghan War
1838.body: December – First Anglo-Afghan War: British and Presidency armies set out from Punjab in support of Shah Shujah Durrani's claim to the throne of Afghanistan.

1838.title: Sylvain Charles Valée
1838.body: Sylvain Charles Valée founds Skikda, Algeria.

1838.title: British Empire
1838.body: Most former slaves in the British Empire are released from apprenticeships, giving them full emancipation under terms of the Slavery Abolition Act 1833.

1838.title: Nicholas Nickleby
1838.body: The first installment of Nicholas Nickleby, the new novel by Charles Dickens, is released as the opener of a 20-part serialization in London.

1839.title: The Bedchamber Crisis in the United Kingdom
1839.body: The Bedchamber Crisis in the United Kingdom: Following the announcement by Prime Minister Lord Melbourne that he intends to resign, Robert Peel asks (for political reasons) that Queen Victoria dismiss some of her personal attendants, Ladies of the Bedchamber, as a condition for his forming a government. Victoria refuses to accept the condition and Melbourne is persuaded to stay on as Prime Minister.

1839.title: Faisal bin Turki bin Abdullah Al Saud
1839.body: Khalid bin Saud Al Suad usurps the throne from Faisal bin Turki bin Abdullah Al Saud, who assumed power of Nejd in 1834, and is sent to Cairo as prisoner. Omar bin Ofaysan, the Amir Faisal's governor in the Eastern Province seeks asylum in Bahrain, but Khalid the pretender demands his surrender and the surrender of the fort at Dammam; then under the control of the Al Khalifa of Bahrain.

1839.title: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland
1839.body: The United Kingdom, backed by the Russian Empire and the Austrian Empire, compels July Monarchy France to abandon Muhammad Ali of Egypt, and forces him to return Syria and Arabia to the Ottoman Empire.

1839.title: First Anglo-Afghan War
1839.body: First Anglo-Afghan War: Battle of Ghazni – British forces capture the fortress city of Ghazni in Afghanistan.

1839.title: John Lambton, 1st Earl of Durham
1839.body: Former British statesman Lord Durham, as President of the New Zealand Company, formally asks the British government for permission to colonize New Zealand, and to establish a colonial government under the sovereignty of the United Kingdom.

1840.title: Queen Victoria
1840.body: Queen Victoria of the United Kingdom marries her cousin Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha.

1840.title: Johnny Appleseed
1840.body: Johnny Appleseed meets Abraham Lincoln, and plants apple trees in New York City.

1840.title: Paweł Strzelecki
1840.body: Paweł Strzelecki reaches Australia's highest summit, Mount Kosciuszko.

1840.title: Belle Poule
1840.body: The French frigate Belle Poule arrives in Cherbourg, bringing back the remains of Napoleon from Saint Helena to France.

1840.title: Napoleon
1840.body: The body of Napoleon is laid to rest in Les Invalides in Paris.

1841.title: Queen's University at Kingston
1841.body: Queen's University is founded in Kingston, Ontario, by Rev. Thomas Liddell, who carries a Royal Charter from Queen Victoria, and becomes the school's first principal.

1841.title: First Anglo-Afghan War
1841.body: First Anglo-Afghan War: At a meeting with the Afghan general Akbar Khan, British diplomat Sir William Hay Macnaghten is shot dead at close quarters.

1841.title: Hong Kong
1841.body: Britain occupies Hong Kong. Later in the year, the first census of the island records a population of about 7,500.

1841.title: Filibuster
1841.body: The first ongoing filibuster in the United States Senate begins, and lasts until March 11.

1841.title: John Augustus
1841.body: John Augustus develops the concept of probation in Boston, Massachusetts.

1842.title: Queen Victoria
1842.body: Queen Victoria becomes the first reigning British monarch to travel by train, on the Great Western Railway between Slough and London Paddington station.

1842.title: First Anglo-Afghan War
1842.body: First Anglo-Afghan War – Massacre of Elphinstone's army (Battle of Gandamak): British East India Company troops are destroyed by Afghan forces on the road from Kabul to Jalalabad, Afghanistan, by Akbar Khan, son of Dost Mohammad Khan.

1842.title: New Zealand
1842.body: The New Zealand seat of government moves from Russell to Auckland.

1842.title: Debtors' prison
1842.body: November 10 & 19 – London debtor's prisons the Fleet Prison and Marshalsea are closed and inmates transferred to Queen's Bench Prison. Pentonville Prison for criminals is completed in north London this year.

1842.title: Treaty of Nanking
1842.body: The Treaty of Nanking, an unequal treaty between the United Kingdom and Qing dynasty China, ends the First Opium War, and establishes Hong Kong as a British colony (later a British dependent territory until 1997).

1843.title: New Zealand
1843.body: In New Zealand, a posse of British settlers sent to arrest Māori chief Te Rauparaha clash with members of his Ngāti Toa tribe, resulting in 26 deaths.

1843.title: Martin Chuzzlewit
1843.body: January Serial publication of Charles Dickens's novel Martin Chuzzlewit begins in London; in the July chapters, he lands his hero in the United States. Edgar Allan Poe's short story "The Tell-Tale Heart" is published in The Pioneer, a Boston magazine. The Quaker magazine The Friend is first published in London.

1843.title: Mount Etna
1843.body: Mount Etna erupts in Italy, and kills 69 people in the village of Bronte.

1843.title: Uruguayan Civil War
1843.body: Uruguayan Civil War: Argentina supports Oribe of Uruguay, and begins a siege of Montevideo.

1843.title: Althing
1843.body: The Danish government re-establishes the Althing in Iceland as an advisory body, by royal decree.

1844.title: Millerites
1844.body: This second date predicted by the Millerites for the Second Coming of Jesus (and said to be 6,000 years from creation, relating them to the 6 days of creation, using a day-for-a-year bible principle, with which they proved that the 1,000 years of rest in heaven with God would total to 7,000 years, indicating the completion of creation in the beginning, which make 7 days, but the 7th day is for rest, same as the 7,000th year is for rest in heaven), leads to the Great Disappointment. The Seventh-day Adventist Church believes this date to be the starting point of the Investigative judgment, just prior to the Second Coming of Jesus, as declared in the 26th of 28 fundamental doctrines of Seventh-day Adventists.

1844.title: Báb
1844.body: The Báb is publicly proclaimed to be the promised one of Islam (the Qá'im, or Mahdi). He is also considered to be simultaneously the return of Elijah, John the Baptist and the "Ushídar-Máh" referred to in the Zoroastrian scriptures. He announces to the world the coming of "He whom God shall make manifest".

1844.title: Edict of Toleration
1844.body: The Edict of Toleration is passed in the Ottoman Empire.

1844.title: Hungarian language
1844.body: Hungarian becomes the official language of Hungary.

1844.title: Philippines
1844.body: This date is omitted from the calendar in the Philippines in order to align it with the calendar elsewhere in East Asia, essentially moving the International Date Line to the east of the archipelago.

1845.title: Philippines
1845.body: The Philippines began reckoning Asian dates by hopping the International Date Line through skipping Tuesday, December 31, 1844. That time zone shift was a reform made by Governor–General Narciso Claveria on August 16, 1844, in order to align the local calendars in the country with the rest of Asia as trade interests with Imperial China, Dutch East Indies and neighboring countries increased, after Mexico became independent in 1821. The reform also applied to Caroline Islands, Guam, Marianas Islands, Marshall Islands, and Palau as part of the Captaincy General of the Philippines.

1845.title: Florida
1845.body: March 3 Florida is admitted as the 27th U.S. state. For the first time, both houses of the United States Congress vote, by a two-thirds majority, to override a presidential veto in order to pass legislation.

1845.title: Republic of Texas
1845.body: A majority of voters in the Republic of Texas approve a proposed constitution that, if accepted by the United States Congress, will make Texas a U.S. state.

1845.title: Texas
1845.body: Texas is admitted as the 28th U.S. state.

1845.title: Terror
1845.body: HMS Terror and HMS Erebus of the Franklin Expedition go missing in the Davis Strait west of Greenland, while searching for the Northwest Passage.

1846.title: First Anglo-Sikh war
1846.body: The First Anglo-Sikh war ends with the signing of the Treaty of Lahore. Kashmir is ceded to the British East India Company, and the Koh-i-Noor diamond is surrendered to Queen Victoria.

1846.title: Mexican–American War
1846.body: Mexican–American War: Battle of Monterey – Acting on instructions from Washington, D.C., Commodore John Drake Sloat orders his troops to occupy Monterey and Yerba Buena, thus beginning the United States annexation of California.

1846.title: United States House of Representatives
1846.body: The United States House of Representatives votes to stop sharing the Oregon Country with the United Kingdom.

1846.title: Texas annexation
1846.body: Texas annexation: United States president James K. Polk's annexation of the Republic of Texas is finalized by Texas president Anson Jones in a formal ceremony of transfer of sovereignty. The newly formed Texas state government is officially installed in Austin.

1846.title: Pope Pius
1846.body: Pope Pius IX succeeds Pope Gregory XVI as the 255th pope. He will reign for 31½ years (the longest definitely confirmed).

1847.title: Wuthering Heights
1847.body: Emily Brontë and Anne Brontë publish Wuthering Heights and Agnes Grey, respectively, in a 3-volume set under the pen names of Ellis Bell and Acton Bell in England.

1847.title: Second Treaty of Erzurum
1847.body: Second Treaty of Erzurum: the Ottoman Empire cedes Abadan Island to the Persian Empire.

1847.title: New Zealand Wars
1847.body: New Zealand Wars: A minor Māori chief is accidentally shot by a junior British Army officer in Whanganui on New Zealand's North Island, triggering the Wanganui Campaign (which continues until July 23).

1847.title: Treaty of Cahuenga
1847.body: The Treaty of Cahuenga ends fighting in the Mexican–American War in California.

1847.title: John C. Frémont
1847.body: John C. Frémont is appointed Governor of the new California Territory.

1848.title: Lajos Batthyány
1848.body: The first Hungarian national government is formed, under the leadership of Lajos Batthyány. The April Laws, the first democratic revolutionary laws in Hungary, are promulgated, putting an end to the feudal privileges of the nobility and serfdom; proclaiming the freedom of religion, freedom of the press and foundation of the Hungarian National Bank; and organising the first democratic election in Hungary based in popular representation, a national guard and reunion of Transylvania with Hungary. The Habsburg emperor, and Hungarian king Ferdinand I of Austria, ratify these laws, which form the basis of modern Hungary.

1848.title: John Wickliffe
1848.body: The settlement of Dunedin, New Zealand is founded, with the arrival of settlers from Scotland on board the John Wickliffe.

1848.title: Statuto Albertino
1848.body: Carlo Alberto di Savoia signs the Statuto Albertino that will later represent the first constitution of the Regno d'Italia.

1848.title: Wisconsin
1848.body: Wisconsin is admitted as the 30th U.S. state.

1848.title: The Communist Manifesto
1848.body: Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels publish The Communist Manifesto (Manifest der Kommunistischen Partei) in London.

1849.title: Hungarian Revolution of 1848
1849.body: Hungarian Revolution of 1848: Romanian armed groups massacre 600 unarmed Hungarian civilians, at Nagyenyed.

1849.title: Antananarivo
1849.body: March 28 Four Christians are ordered to be burnt alive in Antananarivo, Madagascar, by Queen Ranavalona I; 14 others are executed. The Frankfurt National Assembly adopts the Frankfurt Constitution, providing for a constitutional monarchy for Germany under a hereditary emperor and with a two-chamber parliament.

1849.title: California
1849.body: Regular steamboat service from the west to the east coast of the United States begins, with the arrival of the SS California in San Francisco Bay. The California leaves New York Harbor on October 6, 1848, rounds Cape Horn at the tip of South America, and arrives at San Francisco after the 4-month, 21-day journey.

1849.title: Second Anglo-Sikh War
1849.body: Second Anglo-Sikh War: Battle of Gujrat – Forces of the British East India Company defeat those of the Sikh Empire in Punjab.

1849.title: United States Department of the Interior
1849.body: March 3 The United States Department of the Interior is established, incorporating the Census Office, United States General Land Office, Office of Indian Affairs and Patent and Trademark Office. Minnesota becomes a United States territory. The United States Congress passes the Gold Coinage Act allowing the minting of two additional denominations of gold coins, the gold dollar and the double eagle.

1850.title: Elizabeth
1850.body: The ship Elizabeth, an American merchant freighter carrying cargo that included mostly marble from Carrara, slammed into a sandbar less than 100 yards from Fire Island, New York, drowning Margaret Fuller, her husband Ossoli, and their young son Angelino.

1850.title: Abraham Lincoln
1850.body: Abraham Lincoln’s second son, Edward Baker Lincoln, died at the age of three after 52 days for struggling with tuberculosis (consumption) before his fourth birthday in Springfield, Illinois.

1850.title: California
1850.body: September 9 California is admitted as the 31st U.S. state. The New Mexico Territory is organized by order of the United States Congress.

1850.title: Los Angeles
1850.body: Los Angeles is incorporated as a city in California.

1850.title: Hippopotamus
1850.body: The hippopotamus Obaysch arrives at London Zoo from Egypt, the first seen in Europe since Roman times.

1851.title: The Great Exhibition of the Works of Industry of All Nations
1851.body: The Great Exhibition of the Works of Industry of All Nations in the Crystal Palace, Hyde Park, London is opened by Queen Victoria.

1851.title: Solar eclipse
1851.body: Total solar eclipse visible in Canada, Greenland, Iceland and Northern Europe, the first solar eclipse to be photographed.

1851.title: Maine
1851.body: June 2 Maine passes the first state-wide prohibition law in the United States. Castle & Cooke, the predecessor of Dole Food Company, is founded in Hawaii.

1851.title: Castle & Cooke
1851.body: Castle & Cooke, the predecessor of Dole Food Company, is founded in Hawaii.

1851.title: Reuters
1851.body: October – The Reuters news service is founded in London

1852.title: Hawaii
1852.body: In Hawaii, sugar planters bring over the first Chinese laborers on 3- or 5-year contracts, giving them 3 dollars per month plus room and board for working a 12-hour day, 6 days a week.

1852.title: Battle of Caseros, Argentina
1852.body: Battle of Caseros, Argentina: The Argentine provinces of Entre Rios and Corrientes, allied with Brazil and members of Colorado Party of Uruguay, defeat Buenos Aires troops under Juan Manuel de Rosas.

1852.title: Harvard–Yale Regatta
1852.body: The first American intercollegiate athletic event, the Boat Race between Yale and Harvard, is held.

1852.title: Uncle Tom's Cabin
1852.body: Uncle Tom's Cabin, by Harriet Beecher Stowe, is published in book form in Boston, Massachusetts.

1852.title: Napoleon III
1852.body: President Louis-Napoléon Bonaparte proclaims a new constitution for the French Second Republic.

1853.title: Prince Leopold, Duke of Albany
1853.body: Prince Leopold, the youngest son and the eighth child of Queen Victoria and Prince Albert, is born in Buckingham Palace; he has inherited haemophilia. During the labour, Victoria chooses to use chloroform, thereby encouraging the use of anesthesia in childbirth.

1853.title: Old Style and New Style dates
1853.body: November 30 (November 18 O.S.) – Crimean War: Battle of Sinop – The Russian fleet destroys the Turkish fleet.

1853.title: Crimean War
1853.body: Crimean War: The Ottoman Empire begins war with Russia.

1853.title: Joaquin Murrieta
1853.body: Outlaw and bandit Joaquin Murrieta is killed in California.

1854.title: Sa'id of Egypt
1854.body: Mohamed Sa'id Pasha succeeds his nephew Abbas as the Wāli of Egypt and Sudan, then a province of the Ottoman Empire.

1854.title: Ostend Manifesto
1854.body: United States diplomats in Europe meet and draft the Ostend Manifesto, setting out a rationale for the U.S. to acquire Cuba from Spain.

1854.title: In the United States
1854.body: In the United States: The Boston Public Library opens to the public. The Republican Party is formed by anti-slavery opponents of the Kansas–Nebraska Act in Ripon, Wisconsin.

1854.title: Republican Party
1854.body: The Republican Party is formed by anti-slavery opponents of the Kansas–Nebraska Act in Ripon, Wisconsin.

1854.title: Jackson, Michigan
1854.body: In Jackson, Michigan, the first convention of the U.S. Republican Party is held.

1855.title: Queen Victoria
1855.body: Queen Victoria of the United Kingdom, with Prince Albert, begins a 10-day state visit to Paris, the first visit of a reigning British monarch to France since 1413. While there, she visits the Exposition Universelle.

1855.title: Mount Sinai Hospital
1855.body: Mount Sinai Hospital, New York, is dedicated (originally named as the Jews' Hospital) in New York City; it opens to patients on June 5.

1855.title: Leaves of Grass
1855.body: July 4 Thomas Cook escorts his first party of excursionists from England to tour the European continent, travelling via Belgium and enabling the tourists to visit the Paris Exposition. Walt Whitman's poetry collection Leaves of Grass is published in Brooklyn.

1855.title: Van Diemen's Land
1855.body: Van Diemen's Land is separated administratively from the Australian province of New South Wales and granted self-government.

1855.title: Port of Iloilo
1855.body: The Port of Iloilo in the Philippines is opened to international trade, by Queen Isabel II of Spain. This year also the ports of Sual (modern-day Pangasinan) and Zamboanga City are opened to international trade.

1856.title: North Carolina Railroad
1856.body: January 29 The 223-mile North Carolina Railroad is completed from Goldsboro through Raleigh and Salisbury to Charlotte. Queen Victoria institutes the Victoria Cross as a British military decoration.

1856.title: Queen Victoria
1856.body: Queen Victoria institutes the Victoria Cross as a British military decoration.

1856.title: Bounty
1856.body: Queen Victoria of the United Kingdom gives Norfolk Island to the population of the colony at Pitcairn Island, most being descendants of the Mutiny on the Bounty. They first settle on Norfolk Island on June 8. Women's suffrage, as practiced on Pitcairn, is extended to Norfolk Island.

1856.title: Know Nothing
1856.body: The American Know Nothing Party convenes in Philadelphia to nominate their first Presidential candidate, former President Millard Fillmore.

1856.title: Isabela
1856.body: The province of Isabela is created in the Philippines, in honor of Queen Isabella II of Spain.

1857.title: Princess Beatrice of the United Kingdom
1857.body: Princess Beatrice, the fifth daughter and the youngest child of Queen Victoria and Prince Albert, is born.

1857.title: Victoria and Albert Museum
1857.body: The Victoria and Albert Museum in London is officially opened by Queen Victoria.

1857.title: Queen Victoria
1857.body: At a ceremony in London, Queen Victoria awards the first 66 Victoria Crosses to British troops, for actions during the Crimean War.

1857.title: Indus Valley Region
1857.body: The Indus Valley Region (i.e., Pakistan Region) is incorporated as part of British India, for the next 90 years until 1947.

1857.title: Spain
1857.body: Spanish financial group, Banco Santander founded in Cantabria, Spain.

1858.title: Wedding March
1858.body: The Wedding March by Felix Mendelssohn becomes a popular wedding recessional, after it is played on this day at the marriage of Queen Victoria's daughter Victoria, Princess Royal, to Prince Friedrich of Prussia in St James's Palace, London.

1858.title: Queen Victoria
1858.body: Queen Victoria announces her choice of Ottawa as capital of the Province of Canada.

1858.title: James Buchanan
1858.body: U.S. President James Buchanan inaugurates the new trans-Atlantic telegraph cable, by exchanging greetings with Queen Victoria. However, a weak signal forces a shutdown of the service in a few weeks.

1858.title: House Divided
1858.body: Abraham Lincoln accepts the Republican Party nomination for a seat in the United States Senate, delivering his House Divided speech in Springfield, Illinois.

1858.title: Battle of Grahovac
1858.body: Battle of Grahovac: The Ottomans are decisively defeated by Montenegrin forces. The Bawani Imli massacre, (April 28) where 52 Indian freedom fighters were hanged to death on a tamarind tree by British colonial forces.

1859.title: Old Style and New Style dates
1859.body: January 24 (O. S.) – Under the rule of Alexandru Ioan Cuza, the provinces of Wallachia and Moldavia are united under the jurisdiction of the Ottoman Empire. It would be a principal step in forming the modern state of Romania.

1859.title: Mekteb-i Mülkiye
1859.body: The Mekteb-i Mülkiye School is founded in the Ottoman Empire.

1859.title: Border Treaty between Brazil and Venezuela
1859.body: Border Treaty between Brazil and Venezuela: The two countries agree their borders should be traced at the water divide, between the Amazon and the Orinoco basins.

1859.title: Charles Rigault de Genouilly
1859.body: French naval forces under Charles Rigault de Genouilly capture the city and Citadel of Saigon in Vietnam, beginning the Siege of Saigon.

1859.title: Oregon
1859.body: Oregon is admitted as the 33rd U.S. state.

1860.title: Prussia
1860.body: Austria, Britain, France, Prussia and the Ottoman Empire form a commission to investigate the causes of the massacres of Maronite Christians, committed by Druzes in Lebanon earlier in the year.

1860.title: Abraham Lincoln
1860.body: Abraham Lincoln makes his Cooper Union speech in New York that is largely responsible for his election to the presidency.

1860.title: 1860 United States presidential election
1860.body: 1860 United States presidential election: Abraham Lincoln elected as the 16th President of the United States, the first Republican to hold that office, defeating John C. Breckinridge, Stephen A. Douglas, and John Bell.

1860.title: American Civil War
1860.body: American Civil War: South Carolina, led by Governor Francis Pickens, becomes the first state to secede from the United States.

1860.title: Battle of Tétouan, Morocco
1860.body: Battle of Tétouan, Morocco: Spanish troops under General Leopoldo O'Donnell, 1st Duke of Tetuan defeat the Moroccan Army.

1861.title: Abraham Lincoln
1861.body: President-elect Abraham Lincoln arrives secretly in Washington, D.C. in light of suspicions of a conspiracy in Baltimore to kill him.

1861.title: February 11 American Civil War
1861.body: February 11 American Civil War: The U.S. House unanimously passes a resolution, guaranteeing non-interference with slavery in any state. About 850 convicts at Chatham Dockyard in England take over their prison in a riot.

1861.title: American Civil War
1861.body: American Civil War: The U.S. House unanimously passes a resolution, guaranteeing non-interference with slavery in any state.

1861.title: Nevada Territory
1861.body: March 2 Nevada is organized as a United States territory. American Civil War: Texas is admitted to the Confederate States of America.

1861.title: Kansas
1861.body: Kansas is admitted as the 34th U.S. state, being admitted as a free state.

1862.title: Internal Revenue Service
1862.body: July 1 The Bureau of Internal Revenue, the forerunner of the Internal Revenue Service, is established in the United States. Princess Alice, the second daughter of Queen Victoria, marries Prince Ludwig of Hesse and by Rhine. S.

1862.title: Monitor
1862.body: American Civil War: The first U.S. ironclad warship, USS Monitor, is launched in Brooklyn.

1862.title: Ciudad Serdán
1862.body: An ammunition warehouse explosion in San Andres Chalchicomula, Mexico, kills between 1,400 and 2,280 during the Second French intervention in Mexico.

1862.title: Dun Mountain Railway
1862.body: The first railway is opened in New Zealand, by the Dun Mountain Copper Mining Company.

1863.title: Queen Victoria
1863.body: Queen Victoria issues Letters Patent granting Goulburn city status, making it Australia's first inland city.

1863.title: Abraham Lincoln
1863.body: Abraham Lincoln signs the Emancipation Proclamation during the third year of the American Civil War, making the abolition of slavery in the Confederate States of America an official war goal. The signing proclaimed the freedom of 3.1 million of the nation's four million slaves and immediately frees 50,000 of them, with the rest freed as the Union Army advances. This event marks the start of America's Reconstruction Era.

1863.title: Lieber Code
1863.body: The Lieber Code signed and issued by President Abraham Lincoln to the Union forces of the United States in the American Civil War constitutes the world's first official comprehensive code of the modern laws of war.

1863.title: Edward VII
1863.body: Albert Edward, Prince of Wales (later Edward VII of the United Kingdom) marries Princess Alexandra of Denmark (later Queen Alexandra).

1863.title: American Civil War
1863.body: American Civil War: Grierson's Raid – Union cavalrymen are ambushed while crossing the Tickfaw River in Mississippi.

1864.title: American Civil War
1864.body: American Civil War: March 9 – Abraham Lincoln appoints Ulysses S. Grant commander in chief of all Union armies. March 10 – The Red River Campaign begins, as Union troops reach Alexandria, Louisiana.

1864.title: Abraham Lincoln
1864.body: Abraham Lincoln appoints Ulysses S. Grant commander in chief of all Union armies.

1864.title: New York World
1864.body: Civil War gold hoax: The New York World and the New York Journal of Commerce publish a fake proclamation that President Abraham Lincoln has issued a draft of 400,000 more soldiers.

1864.title: Circassian genocide
1864.body: The Russian Empire begins the Circassian genocide. More than 1.5 million Circassians are driven from their homeland to the Ottoman Empire, ending the Russo-Circassian War.

1864.title: H. L. Hunley
1864.body: American Civil War: February 17 – The tiny Confederate hand-propelled submarine H. L. Hunley sinks the USS Housatonic (1861), using a spar torpedo in Charleston Harbor, becoming the first submarine to sink an enemy ship, although the submarine and her crew of eight are also lost.

1865.title: Our American Cousin
1865.body: April 14 Assassination of Abraham Lincoln: President of the United States Abraham Lincoln is shot while attending an evening performance of the farce Our American Cousin at Ford's Theatre in Washington, D.C., by actor and Confederate sympathizer John Wilkes Booth. United States Secretary of State William H. Seward and his family are attacked in his home, by Lewis Powell.

1865.title: American Civil War
1865.body: American Civil War: Second Battle of Fort Fisher – Union forces launch a major amphibious assault against the last seaport held by the Confederates, Fort Fisher, North Carolina.

1865.title: American Civil War – Battle of Five Forks
1865.body: American Civil War – Battle of Five Forks: In Petersburg, Virginia, Confederate General Robert E. Lee begins his final offensive.

1865.title: Treaty of the Triple Alliance
1865.body: The Treaty of the Triple Alliance of Argentina, Brazil, and Uruguay against Paraguay is formally signed, following the outbreak of the Paraguayan War.

1865.title: American Civil War – Battle of Palmito Ranch
1865.body: American Civil War – Battle of Palmito Ranch: In far south Texas, more than a month after Confederate General Lee's surrender, the last land battle of the civil war with casualties, ends with a Confederate victory.

1866.title: Princess Helena of the United Kingdom
1866.body: Princess Helena, third daughter of Queen Victoria, marries Prince Christian of Schleswig-Holstein.

1866.title: Ottoman Empire
1866.body: Ottoman troops clash with supporters of Maronite leader Youssef Bey Karam, at St. Doumit in Lebanon; the Ottomans are defeated.

1866.title: Pluto
1866.body: Calculations indicate Pluto (not known at this time) reaches its only aphelion (furthest point from the Sun) between 1618 and August 2113.

1866.title: Reconstruction
1866.body: Reconstruction: Tennessee becomes the first U.S. state to be readmitted to the Union following the American Civil War.

1866.title: Royal Aeronautical Society
1866.body: January 12 The Royal Aeronautical Society is formed as The Aeronautical Society of Great Britain in London, the world's oldest such society. British auxiliary steamer SS London (1864) sinks in a storm in the Bay of Biscay, on passage from the Thames to Australia, with the loss of 244 people, and only 19 survivors.

1867.title: Ausgleich
1867.body: May 29 The Austro-Hungarian Compromise (called Ausgleich in German or kiegyezés in Hungarian (The Compromise)) is born through Act 12, which establishes the Austro-Hungarian Empire; on June 8 Emperor Francis Joseph of Austria is crowned King of Hungary. Canadian Confederation: Queen Victoria signs the British North America Act, creating the Dominion of Canada, effective July 1.

1867.title: Canadian Confederation
1867.body: Canadian Confederation: Queen Victoria signs the British North America Act, creating the Dominion of Canada, effective July 1.

1867.title: Maronite
1867.body: Maronite nationalist leader Youssef Bey Karam leaves Lebanon aboard a French ship for Algeria.

1867.title: British North America Act
1867.body: July 1 Canadian Confederation: The British North America Act of 29 March comes into force, creating the Dominion of Canada, the first independent dominion in the British Empire. The Constitution of the North German Confederation comes into effect, creating a confederation of states, under the leadership of Prussia and Otto von Bismarck.

1867.title: Singapore
1867.body: The Strait Settlement of Singapore, formerly ruled from Calcutta, becomes a Crown colony, under the jurisdiction of the Colonial Office in London.

1868.title: Metropolitan Life Insurance Company
1868.body: The Metropolitan Life Insurance Company is formed, in New York City.

1868.title: February 24 Impeachment of Andrew Johnson
1868.body: February 24 Impeachment of Andrew Johnson: Three days after his action to dismiss United States Secretary of War Edwin M. Stanton, the United States House of Representatives votes 126–47 in favor of a resolution to impeach Andrew Johnson, President of the United States, the first of three Presidents to be impeached by the full House. Johnson is later acquitted by the United States Senate.

1868.title: Impeachment of Andrew Johnson
1868.body: Impeachment of Andrew Johnson: Three days after his action to dismiss United States Secretary of War Edwin M. Stanton, the United States House of Representatives votes 126–47 in favor of a resolution to impeach Andrew Johnson, President of the United States, the first of three Presidents to be impeached by the full House. Johnson is later acquitted by the United States Senate.

1868.title: President of the United States
1868.body: May 16, May 26 – President Andrew Johnson is twice acquitted during his impeachment trial, by one vote in the United States Senate.

1868.title: University of California
1868.body: The University of California is founded in Oakland, California, when the Organic Act is signed into California law.

1869.title: Irish Church Act 1869
1869.body: The Irish Church Act 1869 is given royal assent by Queen Victoria, disestablishing the Church of Ireland effective January 1, 1871.

1869.title: Abdur Rahman Khan
1869.body: Abdur Rahman Khan is defeated at Tinah Khan, and exiled from Afghanistan.

1869.title: North German Confederation
1869.body: March 1 The North German Confederation issues 10gr and 30gr value stamps, printed on goldbeater's skin. (O. S.

1869.title: Dmitri Mendeleev
1869.body: (O. S. February 17) – Dmitri Mendeleev finishes his design of the first periodic table and sends it for publishing.

1869.title: Ranavalona
1869.body: Ranavalona II, the Merina Queen of Madagascar, is baptized.

1870.title: Bulgarian Exarchate
1870.body: The Bulgarian Exarchate is established, by decree of Sultan Abdülaziz of the Ottoman Empire.

1870.title: Hiram Rhodes Revels
1870.body: Hiram Rhodes Revels, a Republican from Mississippi, is sworn into the United States Senate, becoming the first African American to sit in the U.S. Congress.

1870.title: Fifteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution
1870.body: March 30 The Fifteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution, giving African American men the right to vote, is ratified. Reconstruction: Texas is readmitted to the Union.

1870.title: Reconstruction
1870.body: Reconstruction: Texas is readmitted to the Union.

1870.title: Association football
1870.body: The first ever international Association football match, England v Scotland, takes place under the auspices of the Football Association at The Oval, London.

1871.title: Surgeon General of the United States
1871.body: March 29 The first Surgeon General of the United States (John Maynard Woodworth) is appointed. The Royal Albert Hall in London is opened by Queen Victoria; it incorporates a grand organ by Henry Willis & Sons, the world's largest at this time.

1871.title: Royal Albert Hall
1871.body: The Royal Albert Hall in London is opened by Queen Victoria; it incorporates a grand organ by Henry Willis & Sons, the world's largest at this time.

1871.title: British Columbia
1871.body: July 20 British Columbia joins the confederation of Canada. C. W.

1871.title: The Crystal Palace
1871.body: The first cat show is held at the Crystal Palace of London.

1871.title: C. W. Alcock
1871.body: W. Alcock proposes that "a Challenge Cup should be established in connection with the Association", giving birth to the FA Cup for Association football in England.

1872.title: William Gosse
1872.body: Explorer William Gosse reaches Uluru in central Australia and names it Ayers Rock.

1872.title: Victoria Woodhull
1872.body: Victoria Woodhull becomes the first woman nominated for President of the United States, although she is a year too young to qualify and does not appear on the ballot.

1872.title: Filipino people
1872.body: Filipino priests José Burgos, Mariano Gomez and Jacinto Zamora, collectively known as Gomburza, are executed in Bagumbayan Fields, Manila, Philippines by the authorities of New Spain, on charges of subversion arising from the 1872 Cavite mutiny.

1872.title: Charles XV of Sweden
1872.body: Upon the death of King Charles XV of Sweden and Norway, he is succeeded as King of both crowns by his brother Oscar II.

1872.title: 1872 FA Cup Final
1872.body: 1872 FA Cup Final: In the first ever final of the FA Cup, the world's oldest football competition, Wanderers F.C. defeat Royal Engineers A.F.C. 1–0 at The Oval in Kennington, London.

1873.title: Griqualand West
1873.body: New Rush in Griqualand West, South Africa, is renamed Kimberley.

1873.title: Colfax massacre
1873.body: Colfax massacre: More than 60 to 150 black men are murdered in Colfax, Louisiana, while surrendering to a mob of former Confederate soldiers and members of the Ku Klux Klan.

1873.title: Emancipation Day for Puerto Rico
1873.body: Emancipation Day for Puerto Rico: Most slaves are freed.

1873.title: Emilio Castelar
1873.body: February 12 Emilio Castelar, the former foreign minister, becomes prime minister of the new Spanish Republic. The Coinage Act of 1873 in the United States is signed into law by President Ulysses S. Grant.

1873.title: Coinage Act of 1873
1873.body: The Coinage Act of 1873 in the United States is signed into law by President Ulysses S. Grant. Coming into effect on April 1, it ends bimetallism in the U.S., and places the country on the gold standard.

1874.title: Alfred, Duke of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha
1874.body: Prince Alfred, Duke of Edinburgh, second son of Queen Victoria, marries Grand Duchess Maria Alexandrovna of Russia, only daughter of Tsar Alexander III of Russia, in the Grand Church of the Winter Palace in Saint Petersburg.

1874.title: Harper's Weekly
1874.body: Harper's Weekly publishes a cartoon by Thomas Nast which is the first use of an elephant as a symbol for the Republican Party in the United States.

1874.title: Democratic Party
1874.body: Democrats gain control of the United States House of Representatives for the first time since 1860.

1874.title: Ignacio María González
1874.body: Ignacio María González becomes head of state of the Dominican Republic for the first time.

1874.title: Yomiuri Shimbun
1874.body: The first issue of Japanese-language newspaper Yomiuri Shimbun is published in Tokyo, Japan.

1875.title: Ottoman Empire
1875.body: October – The Ottoman state declares partial bankruptcy, and places its finances in the hands of European creditors. It is also suffering widespread nationalist rebellions resulting in Russian intervention and Great Power tensions.

1875.title: Battle of Agurdat
1875.body: Battle of Agurdat: An Egyptian invasion of Ethiopia fails, when Emperor Yohannes IV defeats an army led by Werner Munzinger.

1875.title: Association football
1875.body: September – English Association football team Birmingham City F.C. is founded as Small Heath Alliance in Birmingham by a group of cricketers from Holy Trinity Church, Bordesley, playing its first match in November.

1875.title: Mason County War
1875.body: The Mason County War begins, as a German-American mob breaks into a prison, and lynches cattle rustlers in central Texas.

1875.title: Carmen
1875.body: March 3 Bizet's Carmen is first performed at the Opéra-Comique, Paris, France, three months before the composer's death. The Page Act of 1875 is enacted in the United States, effectively prohibiting the immigration of Chinese women. The first indoor ice hockey game is played at the Victoria Skating Rink in Montreal, Quebec, Canada.

1876.title: Royal Titles Act 1876
1876.body: May 1 The Royal Titles Act 1876 in the UK Parliament confers the title 'Empress of India' upon Queen Victoria from 1877. The Settle–Carlisle Railway in England is opened to passenger traffic (it opened to goods traffic in 1875).

1876.title: Transcontinental Express
1876.body: The Transcontinental Express arrives in San Francisco via the first transcontinental railroad, 83 hours and 39 minutes after having left New York City.

1876.title: May 11/12 – Berlin Memorandum
1876.body: May 11/12 – Berlin Memorandum: Germany, Russia and Austria-Hungary propose an armistice between Turkey and its insurgents.

1876.title: Abdülaziz
1876.body: May 30 Abdülaziz is deposed by his nephew Murad V as Sultan of the Ottoman Empire on the grounds of mismanaging the economy; 6 days later, Abdülaziz is found dead at the Çırağan Palace in Istanbul and 93 days later Murad is deposed by Abdul Hamid II on the grounds of mental illness. For this reason, in Turkey 1876 is known as the 'Year of the Three Sultans'. The Ems Ukaz, a secret decree, is issued by Tsar Alexander II of Russia in the German city of Bad Ems, aimed at stopping the printing and distribution of Ukrainian-language publications in the Russian Empire.

1876.title: Old Style and New Style dates
1876.body: May 2 (April 20 O.S.) – April Uprising of 1876 in Ottoman Bulgaria begins: members of the Bulgarian Revolutionary Central Committee rise up against their Ottoman Empire overlords. Over the course of one month and 6 days, the rebellion is suppressed by irregular Ottoman bashi-bazouk units that engage in indiscriminate slaughter of both rebels and non-combatants, killing 15,000–30,000, including the principal rebel leaders and around 5,000 in the Batak massacre. Although the rising does not immediately succeed in its aims, reports of the "Bulgarian Horrors" cause a public outcry in the West and the liberation of Bulgaria is achieved two years later.

1877.title: Queen Victoria
1877.body: Queen Victoria is proclaimed Empress of India by the Royal Titles Act 1876, introduced by Benjamin Disraeli, the prime minister of the United Kingdom .

1877.title: Great Sioux War of 1876
1877.body: Great Sioux War of 1876: Sitting Bull leads his band of Lakota into Canada, to avoid harassment by the U.S. Army under Colonel Nelson Miles.

1877.title: Constantinople Conference
1877.body: The Constantinople Conference ends, with Ottoman Turkey rejecting proposals of internal reform and Balkan provisions.

1877.title: Russo-Turkish War (1877–1878)
1877.body: Russo-Turkish War (1877–1878): Russia declares war on the Ottoman Empire.

1877.title: Old Style
1877.body: May 21 (May 9 O.S.) – By a speech in the Parliament of Romania by Mihail Kogălniceanu, the country declares itself independent from the Ottoman Empire (recognized in 1878 after the end of the Romanian independence war).

1878.title: Cyprus Convention
1878.body: Cyprus Convention: The Ottoman Empire cedes Cyprus to the United Kingdom, but retains the nominal title.

1878.title: Treaty of Berlin
1878.body: The Treaty of Berlin makes Serbia, Montenegro and Romania completely independent, confirms the autonomy of Bulgaria, makes Cyprus a British possession, and allows Austria-Hungary to garrison the Bosnia Vilayet.

1878.title: Russo-Turkish War
1878.body: Russo-Turkish War: Battle of Shipka Pass IV – Russian and Bulgarian forces defeat the Ottoman Empire.

1878.title: Ottoman Empire
1878.body: Greece declares war on the Ottoman Empire.

1878.title: Old Style and New Style dates
1878.body: March 3 (February 19 O.S.) – Treaty of San Stefano signed between the Russian and Ottoman Empires following Russian victory in the Russo-Turkish War (1877–1878) provides for establishment of an autonomous Principality of Bulgaria (although not as large as originally envisaged). Russia however regards the treaty as a preliminary one only, refusing on March 25 a British proposal to lay it before a European congress, and matters are finally settled in July's Treaty of Berlin.

1879.title: United States Note
1879.body: January 1 The Specie Resumption Act takes effect. The United States Note is valued the same as gold, for the first time since the American Civil War. Brahms' Violin Concerto is premiered in Leipzig with Joseph Joachim as soloist and the composer conducting.

1879.title: Kotka
1879.body: The city of Kotka is founded in Kymenlaakso, Finland, by separating its two islands from the old Kymi parish.

1879.title: Mary Baker Eddy
1879.body: Mary Baker Eddy founds the Church of Christ, Scientist in Boston, Massachusetts.

1879.title: Qing dynasty
1879.body: Qing dynasty China signs the Treaty of Livadia with the Russian Empire.

1879.title: Dual Alliance
1879.body: The Dual Alliance is formed by Germany and Austria-Hungary.

1880.title: Australia
1880.body: Australian police capture bank robber Ned Kelly, after a gun battle at Glenrowan, Victoria.

1880.title: Columbia
1880.body: The SS Columbia completes her maiden voyage, arriving without incident in Portland, Oregon, after a stopover in San Francisco.

1880.title: Munich
1880.body: German company Munich Re is founded in Munich.

1880.title: 1880 United Kingdom general election
1880.body: 1880 United Kingdom general election: William Ewart Gladstone defeats Benjamin Disraeli, to become Prime Minister for the second time.

1880.title: Thomas Edison
1880.body: Thomas Edison is granted a patent for the incandescent light bulb. Edison filed for a US patent for an electric lamp using "a carbon filament or strip coiled and connected ... to platina contact wires." Although the patent described several ways of creating the carbon filament, including using "cotton and linen thread, wood splints, papers coiled in various ways," Edison and his team later discovered that a carbonized bamboo filament could last more than 1200 hours.

1881.title: Tararua
1881.body: SS Tararua hits a reef and sinks off the coast of New Zealand's South Island with only 20 survivors of the 151 on board.

1881.title: Assassination of James A. Garfield
1881.body: Assassination of James A. Garfield: United States President James A. Garfield is shot by lawyer Charles J.

1881.title: Aedes aegypti
1881.body: Carlos Finlay introduces his discovery of the transmission of Yellow Fever by mosquitoes Aedes aegypti, in the Fifth International Sanitary Conference held in Washington, D.C.

1881.title: Gross-Lichterfelde Tramway
1881.body: The world's first regular electric tram service is started near Berlin, by Siemens & Halske.

1881.title: Temuco
1881.body: April 15 Temuco, Chile, is founded. Anti-Semitic pogroms in Southern Russia begin.

1882.title: Roderick Maclean
1882.body: Roderick Maclean fails in an attempt to assassinate Queen Victoria, at Windsor.

1882.title: Labor Day
1882.body: September 5 The first United States Labor Day parade is held in New York City. Tottenham Hotspur F.C. is founded (as Hotspur F.C.) in London.

1882.title: Tottenham Hotspur F.C.
1882.body: Tottenham Hotspur F.C. is founded (as Hotspur F.C.) in London.

1882.title: 'Urabi revolt
1882.body: The 'Urabi revolt breaks out in Egypt against Khedive Tewfik Pasha and European influence in that country.

1882.title: Anglo-Egyptian War
1882.body: Anglo-Egyptian War: The British Mediterranean Fleet carries out the Bombardment of Alexandria, its forces capturing the city of Alexandria, Egypt, and securing the Suez Canal.

1883.title: Battle of Gia Cuc
1883.body: Battle of Gia Cuc: A French force defeats the Vietnamese in northern Vietnam in the run-up to the Sino-French War.

1883.title: Boys' Brigade
1883.body: October 4 The Boys' Brigade (the first uniformed youth organization in existence) is founded in Glasgow, Scotland. The Orient Express train begins to run through from Paris Gare de l'Est to Giurgiu in Romania, with onward ferry and train connections to Istanbul (the train has been running since June 5 as far as Vienna).

1883.title: Orient Express
1883.body: The Orient Express train begins to run through from Paris Gare de l'Est to Giurgiu in Romania, with onward ferry and train connections to Istanbul (the train has been running since June 5 as far as Vienna).

1883.title: Vaudeville
1883.body: The first vaudeville theater is opened, in Boston, Massachusetts.

1884.title: Prince Leopold, Duke of Albany
1884.body: Prince Leopold, the youngest son and eighth child of Queen Victoria and Prince Albert, dies, aged 30 in Cannes (France) following a fall complicated by his haemophilia. His son, Charles Edward, Duke of Saxe-Coburg and Gotha, is born nearly 4 months later.

1884.title: 1884 United States presidential election
1884.body: 1884 United States presidential election: Democratic governor of New York Grover Cleveland defeats Republican James G. Blaine in a very close contest, to win the first of his non-consecutive terms.

1884.title: Humanum genus
1884.body: Pope Leo XIII publishes the encyclical Humanum genus, denouncing Freemasonry and certain liberal beliefs which he considers to be associated with it.

1884.title: Angelo Moriondo
1884.body: May 16 Angelo Moriondo of Turin is granted a patent for an espresso machine. Sweden's Finance Minister Robert Themptander becomes his country's Prime Minister (1884–88).

1884.title: Sweden
1884.body: Sweden's Finance Minister Robert Themptander becomes his country's Prime Minister (1884–88).

1885.title: BHP
1885.body: BHP (Broken Hill Proprietary), a worldwide mining and natural gas producer is founded in New South Wales, Australia.

1885.title: Hawaii
1885.body: The first Japanese arrive in Hawaii.

1885.title: Sino-French War
1885.body: Sino-French War: Battle of Núi Bop – French troops under General Oscar de Négrier defeat a numerically superior Qing Chinese force, in northern Vietnam.

1885.title: Battle for Kushka
1885.body: The Battle for Kushka triggers the Panjdeh Incident, which nearly gives rise to war between the British Empire and Russian Empire.

1885.title: S Andromedae
1885.body: S Andromedae, the only supernova seen in the Andromeda Galaxy so far by astronomers, and the first ever noted outside the Milky Way, is discovered.

1886.title: Royal Holloway, University of London
1886.body: The Royal Holloway College for women is opened by Queen Victoria, near London, England.

1886.title: Avon Products
1886.body: September – Avon Products, a worldwide cosmetics and household brand is founded by David H. McConnell in New York City, United States.

1886.title: Anglo-Chinese School
1886.body: The Anglo-Chinese School is founded by Bishop William Fitzjames Oldham at 70 Amoy Street, Singapore.

1886.title: Grover Cleveland
1886.body: U.S. President Grover Cleveland marries Frances Folsom in the White House (Washington, D.C.), becoming the only President of the United States to wed in the executive mansion. She is 27 years his junior.

1886.title: 1886 eruption of Mount Tarawera
1886.body: The Mount Tarawera volcano erupts in New Zealand, resulting in the deaths of over 150 people and the destruction of the famous Pink and White Terraces.

1887.title: British Empire
1887.body: The British Empire celebrates Queen Victoria's Golden Jubilee, marking the 50th year of her reign.

1887.title: Stanford University
1887.body: The cornerstone of the new Stanford University, in northern California, is laid (the college opens in 1891).

1887.title: Anne Sullivan
1887.body: Anne Sullivan begins teaching Helen Keller. March 3: Helen Keller and Sullivan.

1887.title: Kapunda
1887.body: January 20 The United States Senate allows the United States Navy to lease Pearl Harbor as a naval base. British emigrant ship Kapunda sinks after a collision off the coast of Brazil, killing 303 with only 16 survivors.

1887.title: United States Senate
1887.body: The United States Senate allows the United States Navy to lease Pearl Harbor as a naval base.

1888.title: 1888 Republican National Convention
1888.body: In Chicago, the Republican Convention opens at the Auditorium Building. Benjamin Harrison and Levi P. Morton win the nominations for President and Vice President of the United States, respectively.

1888.title: The San Francisco Examiner
1888.body: June 3 The Kingdom of Sedang is formed, in modern-day Vietnam. American writer Ernest Thayer's baseball poem "Casey at the Bat" is first published (under the pen name "Phin") as the last of his humorous contributions to The San Francisco Examiner.

1888.title: Kingdom of Sedang
1888.body: The Kingdom of Sedang is formed, in modern-day Vietnam.

1888.title: National Geographic Society
1888.body: The National Geographic Society is founded in Washington, D.C.

1888.title: Schoolhouse Blizzard
1888.body: The Schoolhouse Blizzard hits Dakota Territory and the states of Montana, Minnesota, Nebraska, Kansas and Texas, leaving 235 dead, many of them children on their way home from school.

1889.title: Adolf Hitler
1889.body: Birth of Adolf Hitler at Braunau am Inn in Austria-Hungary, the son of customs official Alois Hitler and his third wife, Klara.

1889.title: President of the United States
1889.body: President Grover Cleveland signs the Enabling Act of 1889, admitting North Dakota, South Dakota, Montana and Washington as U.S. states.

1889.title: Solar eclipse of January 1, 1889
1889.body: January 1 The total solar eclipse of January 1, 1889 is seen over parts of California and Nevada. Paiute spiritual leader Wovoka experiences a vision, leading to the start of the Ghost Dance movement in the Dakotas.

1889.title: Hammarby
1889.body: The Hammarby Roddförening (later Hammarby IF) sports club is founded in Sweden.

1889.title: The Starry Night
1889.body: June – Vincent van Gogh paints The Starry Night at Saint-Rémy-de-Provence.

1890.title: Royal Dutch Shell
1890.body: Royal Dutch Petroleum, predecessor of Royal Dutch Shell, the major worldwide energy production and sales company, is founded in the Netherlands to develop an oilfield in Pangkalan Brandan, North Sumatra.

1890.title: Idaho
1890.body: Idaho is admitted as the 43rd U.S. state.

1890.title: Wyoming
1890.body: Wyoming is admitted as the 44th U.S. state.

1890.title: official
1890.body: The first ever official English County Championship cricket match begins in Bristol; Yorkshire beats Gloucestershire, by eight wickets.

1890.title: Vincent van Gogh
1890.body: Dutch artist Vincent van Gogh moves to Auvers-sur-Oise on the edge of Paris, in the care of Paul Gachet, where he will produce around seventy paintings in as many days.

1891.title: Tobacco Protest
1891.body: February – The Tobacco Protest begins in Iran.

1891.title: Canada
1891.body: January 12 Canada brings suit before the United States Supreme Court in re seizures of vessels in the Bering Sea. St. Mary's Cathedral is dedicated in San Francisco.

1891.title: Liliuokalani
1891.body: Liliuokalani is proclaimed Queen of Hawaii.

1891.title: Nelson A. Miles
1891.body: January 7 General Nelson A. Miles' forces surround the Lakota in the Pine Ridge Reservation. The Inter-American Monetary Commission meets in Washington, D.C.

1891.title: Washington, D.C.
1891.body: The Inter-American Monetary Commission meets in Washington, D.C.

1892.title: Ernst Ludwig, Grand Duke of Hesse and by Rhine
1892.body: Ernest Louis, a grandson of Queen Victoria, becomes Grand Duke of Hesse and the Rhine on the death of his father, Grand Duke Louis IV.

1892.title: Queen Victoria
1892.body: Queen Victoria meets with Martha Ann Ricks.

1892.title: de facto
1892.body: Exclusive Agreement: Rulers of six Trucial States (Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Sharjah, Ajman, Ras al-Khaimah and Umm al-Quwain) and Bahrain sign an agreement, by which they become de facto British protectorates.

1892.title: Liverpool F.C.
1892.body: March 15 The Liverpool Football Club is founded in England by John Houlding, the owner of Anfield; Houlding decides to form his own team after Everton leaves Anfield, in an argument over rent. Jesse W. Reno patents the first escalator, installed at Coney Island.

1892.title: Battle of Yemoja River
1892.body: Battle of Yemoja River: British troops defeat Ijebu infantry in modern-day Nigeria, using a maxim gun.

1893.title: June 6 Wedding of Prince George, Duke of York, and Princess Mary of Teck: the fu…
1893.body: June 6 Wedding of Prince George, Duke of York, and Princess Mary of Teck: the future King George V of the United Kingdom marries at St James's Palace in London. The small town of Pomeroy, Iowa, is nearly destroyed by a tornado; 71 people are killed and 200 injured.

1893.title: Wedding of Prince George, Duke of York, and Princess Mary of Teck
1893.body: Wedding of Prince George, Duke of York, and Princess Mary of Teck: the future King George V of the United Kingdom marries at St James's Palace in London.

1893.title: Overthrow of the Kingdom of Hawaii
1893.body: Overthrow of the Kingdom of Hawaii: Lorrin A. Thurston and the Citizen's Committee of Public Safety in Hawaii, with the intervention of the United States Marine Corps, overthrow the government of Queen Liliuokalani.

1893.title: Indiana
1893.body: USS Indiana, the first battleship in the United States Navy comparable to other nation's battleships of the time, is launched.

1893.title: Chief Petty Officer
1893.body: The rank of Chief Petty Officer is established in the United States Navy.

1894.title: Manchester Ship Canal
1894.body: The Manchester Ship Canal and Docks are officially opened by Queen Victoria, linking the previously landlocked industrial city of Manchester, in north-west England, to the Irish Sea.

1894.title: Canada
1894.body: Canada's largest known landslide occurs in Saint-Alban, Quebec, displacing 185 million cubic metres (6.5×10^9 cu ft) of rock and dirt, and leaving a 40-metre (130 ft) scar that covers 4.6 million square metres (50×10^6 sq ft).

1894.title: Coxey's Army
1894.body: Coxey's Army (of the unemployed), the first significant protest march in the United States, departs from Massillon, Ohio, for Washington, D.C.

1894.title: Republic of Hawaii
1894.body: July 4 The short-lived Republic of Hawaii is proclaimed by Sanford B. Dole. The football club FC La Chaux-de-Fonds is founded in Switzerland.

1894.title: Association football
1894.body: The football club FC La Chaux-de-Fonds is founded in Switzerland.

1895.title: 1895 Quchan earthquake
1895.body: A 6.8 magnitude earthquake strikes northeastern Iran near the town of Quchan and causes at least 1,000 deaths.

1895.title: Liaoyang
1895.body: Japanese troops capture Liaoyang and land in Taiwan.

1895.title: Tang Jingsong
1895.body: Anti-Japanese officials, led by Tang Jingsong in Taiwan, declare independence from the Qing dynasty, forming the short-lived Republic of Formosa.

1895.title: Archie MacLaren
1895.body: Archie MacLaren scores an English County Championship cricket record innings of 424 for Lancashire, against Somerset, at Taunton. This record lasts until 1994.

1895.title: Oscar Wilde
1895.body: Oscar Wilde is arrested in London for "gross indecency", after losing a criminal libel case against the Marquess of Queensberry.

1896.title: South Korea
1896.body: The Park Seung-jik shop, predecessor of South Korean conglomerate Doosan Group, is founded in Joseon (the kingdom of Korea).

1896.title: Utah
1896.body: Utah is admitted as the 45th U.S. state.

1896.title: Cricket
1896.body: Cricket: Against Warwickshire, Yorkshire sets a still-standing County Championship record in England, when they accumulate an innings total of 887.

1896.title: Battle of Adwa
1896.body: Battle of Adwa: Ethiopia defends its independence from Italy, ending the First Italo-Ethiopian War.

1896.title: Salomé
1896.body: Oscar Wilde's play Salomé (1891) has its stage premiere (while Wilde is in prison) in its original French in Paris.

1897.title: British Empire
1897.body: The British Empire celebrates Queen Victoria's Diamond Jubilee, marking the 60th year of her reign. No other British monarch will celebrate such a jubilee until Victoria's great-great-granddaughter Elizabeth II in 2012.

1897.title: Siege of Malakand
1897.body: Siege of Malakand: British troops are besieged by Pashtun tribesmen in Malakand, on the Northwest frontier of British India (modern-day Khyber Pakhtunkhwa in Pakistan).

1897.title: Battle of Saragarhi
1897.body: Battle of Saragarhi: Twenty-one Sikhs of the 36th Sikhs regiment of the British Indian Army defend an army post to the death, against 10,000 Afghan and Orakzai tribesmen, in the Tirah Campaign on the Northwest frontier of the British Raj (modern-day Khyber Pakhtunkhwa in Pakistan).

1897.title: Alpha Omicron
1897.body: The International Alpha Omicron Pi sorority is founded, in New York City.

1898.title: New York City
1898.body: New York City annexes land from surrounding counties, creating the City of Greater New York as the world's second largest. The city is geographically divided into five boroughs: Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, The Bronx and Staten Island.

1898.title: Spanish–American War
1898.body: Spanish–American War: Battle of San Juan Hill – United States troops (including Buffalo Soldiers and Theodore Roosevelt's Rough Riders) take a strategic position close to Santiago de Cuba from the Spanish.

1898.title: Maine
1898.body: Spanish–American War: The USS Maine explodes and sinks in Havana Harbor, Cuba, for reasons never fully established, killing 266 men. The event precipitates the United States' declaration of war on Spain, two months later.

1898.title: Nashville
1898.body: Spanish–American War: The United States Navy begins a blockade of Cuban ports and the USS Nashville captures a Spanish merchant ship.

1898.title: Philippine Declaration of Independence
1898.body: Philippine Declaration of Independence: After 333 years of Spanish dominance, General Emilio Aguinaldo declares the Philippines' independence from Spain.

1899.title: League of Peja
1899.body: The League of Peja, organized by Haxhi Zeka to lobby for a Kosovar Albanian state within the Ottoman Empire, attracts 450 delegates to its first convention, held at the city of Peja. Konstantin Stoilov, Prime Minister of Bulgaria and his cabinet ministers resign in a disagreement over self-government for Macedonia. The premiers of the various states of Australia, along with the premier of Tasmania, meet in a conference at Melbourne to discuss the question of a federation of the states.

1899.title: Australia
1899.body: The premiers of the various states of Australia, along with the premier of Tasmania, meet in a conference at Melbourne to discuss the question of a federation of the states.

1899.title: Cretan Revolt
1899.body: After a successful revolt against the Ottoman Empire by the inhabitants of the island of Crete, the area, which joins Greece, gets its first constitution. A crash between two trains on the Lehigh Valley Railroad kills 16 people and injures 20 in the U.S. state of New Jersey.

1899.title: Porpoise
1899.body: January 1 Spanish rule formally ends in Cuba with the cession of Spanish sovereignty to the U.S., concluding 400 years of the Spanish Empire in the Americas. In Samoa, followers of Mataafa, claimant to the rule of the island's subjects, burn the town of Upolu in an ambush of followers of other claimants, Malietoa Tanus and Tamasese, who are evacuated by the British warship HMS Porpoise. Theodore Roosevelt is inaugurated as Governor of New York at the age of 39.

1899.title: Theodore Roosevelt
1899.body: Theodore Roosevelt is inaugurated as Governor of New York at the age of 39.

1900.title: Labour Party
1900.body: February 27 The British Labour Party is officially established, at a meeting in the Congregational Memorial Hall in London, and Ramsay MacDonald is appointed as its first secretary. Second Boer War: British military leaders accept the unconditional notice of surrender from Boer General Piet Cronjé. FC Bayern, Germany's most successful football club, is founded in Munich.

1900.title: FC Bayern Munich
1900.body: FC Bayern, Germany's most successful football club, is founded in Munich.

1900.title: Olympic Games
1900.body: The second Olympic Games, Paris 1900, open (as part of the Paris World Exhibition).

1900.title: Boxer Rebellion
1900.body: Boxer Rebellion: Foreign diplomats in Peking, Qing dynasty China, demand that the Boxer rebels (who oppose foreign interests in the country) be disciplined.

1900.title: Tosca
1900.body: January 14 Puccini's opera Tosca premieres in Rome. The U.S. Senate accepts the British-German Treaty of 1899, in which the United Kingdom renounces its claims to the American Samoa portion of the Samoan Islands.

1901.title: Queen Victoria
1901.body: Queen Victoria of the United Kingdom dies at Osborne House on the Isle of Wight. She is 81 years old and, having ruled for nearly 64 years, will be the second longest-reigning monarch in British history. Her eldest son, Prince Albert Edward, "Bertie", the longest-serving Prince of Wales to this time, succeeds his mother at the age of 59, reigning as King Edward VII, of the United Kingdom and in innovation the British Dominions and also becoming Emperor of India.

1901.title: Crown colony
1901.body: January 1 The British colonies of New South Wales, Queensland, South Australia, Tasmania, Victoria and Western Australia federate as the Commonwealth of Australia; Edmund Barton becomes the first Prime Minister of Australia. Nigeria becomes a British protectorate.

1901.title: Ottoman Empire
1901.body: The Sultan of Turkey orders 50,000 troops to the Bulgarian frontier because of unrest in Macedonia.

1901.title: Edward VII
1901.body: Edward VII opens his first parliament of the United Kingdom.

1901.title: United States Congress
1901.body: The United States Congress passes the Platt Amendment, limiting the autonomy of Cuba as a condition for the withdrawal of American troops.

1902.title: New York Central Railroad
1902.body: A train collision in the New York Central Railroad's Park Avenue Tunnel kills 17 people, injures 38, and leads to increased demand for electric trains and the banning of steam locomotives in New York City.

1902.title: Georgia
1902.body: March 10 Clashes between police and Georgian workers led by Joseph Stalin leave 15 dead, 54 wounded, and 500 in prison. A Circuit Court decision in the United States ends Thomas Edison's monopoly on 35 mm movie film technology.

1902.title: Edward VII
1902.body: Edward VII institutes the Order of Merit, an order bestowed personally by the British monarch on up to 24 distinguished Empire recipients.

1902.title: Theodore Roosevelt
1902.body: U.S. President Theodore Roosevelt prosecutes the Northern Securities Company for violation of the antitrust Sherman Act.

1902.title: Cuba
1902.body: Cuba gains independence from the United States.

1903.title: Românul de la Pind
1903.body: Românul de la Pind, the longest-running newspaper by and about Aromanians until World War II, is founded.

1903.title: New York City
1903.body: In New York City, the Martha Washington Hotel, the first hotel exclusively for women, opens.

1903.title: Ottoman Empire
1903.body: The Ottoman Empire and the German Empire sign an agreement to build the Constantinople–Baghdad Railway.

1903.title: Macedonian Bulgarians
1903.body: Macedonian Bulgarian revolutionary Gotse Delchev is killed in a skirmish with the Ottoman army.

1903.title: Ilinden–Preobrazhenie Uprising
1903.body: The Ilinden–Preobrazhenie Uprising, organized by the Secret Macedonian-Adrianople Revolutionary Organization, breaks out in the Ottoman provinces of Macedonia and Adrianople.

1904.title: The New York Times
1904.body: April 8 The Entente Cordiale is signed between the UK and France. Longacre Square in Midtown Manhattan is renamed Times Square, after The New York Times.

1904.title: British expedition to Tibet
1904.body: British expedition to Tibet: The British expedition under Colonel Francis Younghusband takes Lhasa, Tibet.

1904.title: Joseph F. Smith
1904.body: Joseph F. Smith announces the Second Manifesto in General Conference of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints in Utah Territory, prohibiting the practice of polygamy, which has continued to be sanctioned by some of its leaders in violation of the 1890 Manifesto officially banning the practice.

1904.title: Amur
1904.body: Russo-Japanese War: Russian minelayer Amur lays a minefield about 15 miles (24 km) off Port Arthur, and sinks Japan's battleships Hatsuse, 15,000 tons with 496 crew, and Yashima. On the same day, the Japanese protected cruiser Yoshino sinks after being accidentally rammed by the armored cruiser Kasuga, killing over 270 crew, including Captain Sayegi and his second-in-command, Commander Hirowateri. Japan will keep the loss of Yashima secret for over a year.

1904.title: Chinese South Africans
1904.body: The first 2,000 of 62,000 contracted Chinese coolies arrive at Durban in South Africa from Qinhuangdao to relieve the shortage of unskilled labourers in the Transvaal Colony gold mines, recruited and shipped by the Chinese Engineering and Mining Corporation (CEMC), of which Herbert Hoover is a director.

1905.title: Albert Einstein
1905.body: Albert Einstein submits his paper "On a heuristic viewpoint concerning the production and transformation of light", in which he explains the photoelectric effect using the notion of light quanta, for publication.

1905.title: Grover Shoe Factory disaster
1905.body: March 20 The Grover Shoe Factory disaster kills 58 employees in Brockton, Massachusetts, when a boiler explodes and the factory building collapses. The title Prime Minister of the United Kingdom is officially recognised by King Edward VII by a royal warrant.

1905.title: Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
1905.body: The title Prime Minister of the United Kingdom is officially recognised by King Edward VII by a royal warrant.

1905.title: Anjou
1905.body: The French ship Anjou is wrecked off of the coast of the uninhabited Auckland Island, located 290 miles (470 km) from the nearest inhabited land in New Zealand. The castaways live on the isle for more than three months until being rescued on May 7.

1905.title: A Shropshire Lad
1905.body: The first performance of A Shropshire Lad, the setting to music of the 1896 set of 63 poems of A. E. Housman by Arthur Somervell as a song-cycle, takes place at Aeolian Hall in London.

1906.title: Cuba
1906.body: Unable to control a rebellion, Cuban President Tomás Estrada Palma requests United States intervention. This leads to the Second Occupation of Cuba, which lasts until 1909.

1906.title: Satyagraha
1906.body: Mahatma Gandhi coins the term Satyagraha, to characterize the nonviolence movement in South Africa.

1906.title: Italy
1906.body: The United Kingdom, France and Italy sign an agreement to preserve, in Ethiopia, the integrity of the ancient empire of Abyssinia.

1906.title: Azusa Street Revival
1906.body: The Azusa Street Revival, the primary catalyst for the revival of Pentecostalism this century, opens in Los Angeles.

1906.title: August 16 1906 Aleutian Islands earthquake
1906.body: August 16 1906 Aleutian Islands earthquake: An earthquake with an estimated magnitude of 8.35 occurs off the Rat Islands in Alaska. 1906 Valparaíso earthquake: A magnitude 8.2 earthquake in Valparaíso, Chile leaves nearly 4,000 dead and approximately 20,000 injured.

1907.title: Mauretania
1907.body: November 16 British Cunard Line passenger liner RMS Mauretania, the world's largest and fastest at this date, sets out on her maiden voyage from Liverpool to New York. President Theodore Roosevelt proclaims that Oklahoma has become the 46th U.S. state.

1907.title: Theodore Roosevelt
1907.body: President Theodore Roosevelt proclaims that Oklahoma has become the 46th U.S. state.

1907.title: International Anarchist Congress of Amsterdam
1907.body: The International Anarchist Congress of Amsterdam meets in the Netherlands.

1907.title: Minas Geraes
1907.body: The first Minas Geraes-class battleship is laid down for Brazil, by Armstrong Whitworth on the River Tyne, in England, triggering the South American dreadnought race.

1907.title: Mud March
1907.body: The "Mud March", the first large procession organised by The National Union of Women's Suffrage Societies (NUWSS), takes place in London.

1908.title: Masjed Soleyman
1908.body: At Masjed Soleyman in southwest Persia, the first major commercial oil discovery in the Middle East is made. The rights to the resource are quickly acquired by the United Kingdom.

1908.title: Feyenoord
1908.body: Feyenoord, the first Dutch football club to win the UEFA Champions League, is founded at Rotterdam, Netherlands

1908.title: Old Style and New Style dates
1908.body: June 30 (June 17 OS) – The Tunguska event or "Russian explosion" near the Podkamennaya Tunguska River in Krasnoyarsk Krai, Siberia, Russian Empire, is believed to have been caused by the air burst of a large meteoroid or comet fragment, at an altitude of 5–10 kilometres (3–6 mi) above the Earth's surface.

1908.title: Young Turk Revolution in the Ottoman Empire
1908.body: Young Turk Revolution in the Ottoman Empire: Major Ahmed Niyazi, with 200 followers (Ottoman troops and civilians), begins an open revolution by defecting from the 3rd Army Corps in Macedonia, decamping into the hill country.

1908.title: Young Turk Revolution
1908.body: Young Turk Revolution: The Committee of Union and Progress (CUP) issues a formal ultimatum to Sultan Abdul Hamid II, to restore the constitution of 1876 within the Ottoman Empire; it is restored the following day.

1909.title: Sergei Rachmaninoff
1909.body: Sergei Rachmaninoff's Piano Concerto No. 3 is premièred in New York City with the composer as soloist.

1909.title: Báb
1909.body: The remains of the Báb are placed in the Baháʼí Shrine of the Báb on Mount Carmel in Haifa, at this time within the Ottoman Empire.

1909.title: Ottoman dynasty
1909.body: Sultan of the Ottoman Empire Abdul Hamid II is overthrown and succeeded by his brother, Mehmed V.

1909.title: Cuba
1909.body: The last United States troops leave Cuba, after being there since the Spanish–American War of 1898.

1909.title: Titanic
1909.body: Construction begins on the RMS Titanic, at the Harland and Wolff Shipyard in Belfast.

1910.title: George
1910.body: George V becomes King of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland upon the death of his father, Edward VII.

1910.title: Ottoman Empire
1910.body: Ottoman forces capture the city of Shkodër to put down the Albanian Revolt of 1910.

1910.title: Sport Club Corinthians Paulista
