1910.body: Sport Club Corinthians Paulista is founded in Brazil by railwaymen; its Association football team will be the first FIFA Club World Cup champions in 2000.

1910.title: 'Fowler's match'
1910.body: 'Fowler's match': the Eton v Harrow cricket match at Lord's ground in London, known after the captain of Eton College, Robert St Leger Fowler, and described as "what might just be the greatest cricket match of all time".

1910.title: United States House of Representatives
1910.body: Progressive Republicans in the United States House of Representatives rebel against Speaker Joseph Gurney Cannon, removing him from the Rules Committee and stripping him of his power to appoint committee chairmen.

1911.title: January 3 1911 Kebin earthquake
1911.body: January 3 1911 Kebin earthquake: An earthquake of 8.0 moment magnitude strikes near Almaty in Russian Turkestan, killing 450 or more people. Siege of Sidney Street in London: Two Latvian anarchists die, after a seven-hour siege against a combined police and military force. Home Secretary Winston Churchill arrives to oversee events.

1911.title: Siege of Sidney Street in London
1911.body: Siege of Sidney Street in London: Two Latvian anarchists die, after a seven-hour siege against a combined police and military force. Home Secretary Winston Churchill arrives to oversee events.

1911.title: George
1911.body: George V and Mary are crowned King and Queen of the United Kingdom and the British Dominions, at Westminster Abbey in London.

1911.title: International Women's Day
1911.body: International Women's Day is celebrated for the first time across Europe.

1911.title: Titanic
1911.body: The hull of the RMS Titanic is launched in Belfast, on the same day RMS Olympic starts her sea trials.

1912.title: Kingdom of Serbia
1912.body: Serbia and Bulgaria secretly sign a treaty of alliance for a term of eight years, with each pledging to come to the defence of the other during war. The treaty would eventually be dishonored in World War I.

1912.title: Giuseppe Garibaldi
1912.body: Battle of Beirut: Italy makes a surprise attack on the Ottoman port of Beirut, when the cruiser Giuseppe Garibaldi and the gunboat Volturno bombard the harbour, killing 97 sailors and civilians.

1912.title: Titanic
1912.body: White Star liner RMS Titanic departs from Southampton, England, with more than 2,200 passengers and crew on her maiden voyage, bound for New York.

1912.title: Carpathia
1912.body: Cunard Line vessel RMS Carpathia arrives in New York with the 705 RMS Titanic survivors.

1912.title: Santos
1912.body: Santos FC, a Brazilian association football club, is founded in State of Sao Paulo.

1913.title: Adolf Hitler
1913.body: Adolf Hitler moves from Vienna to Munich.

1913.title: Sung Chiao-jen
1913.body: March 20 Sung Chiao-jen, a founder of the Chinese nationalist party (Kuomintang), is wounded in an assassination attempt, and dies two days later. The city of Canberra, the center of the Australian Capital Territory, becomes the official capital of the Commonwealth of Australia.

1913.title: Canberra
1913.body: The city of Canberra, the center of the Australian Capital Territory, becomes the official capital of the Commonwealth of Australia.

1913.title: Marxism and the National Question
1913.body: January – Joseph Stalin travels to Vienna to research his Marxism and the National Question. This means that, during this month, Stalin, Hitler, Trotsky and Tito are all living in the city, as also are Freud and Jung.

1913.title: Joseph Stalin
1913.body: Joseph Stalin is arrested by the Russian secret police, the Okhrana, in Petrograd, and exiled to Siberia.

1914.title: Assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria
1914.body: Assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria: Serbian nationalist Gavrilo Princip, 19, assassinates Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria and his wife, Duchess Sophie, in Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina, triggering the July Crisis overnight and eventually World War I. Anti-Serb riots in Sarajevo and Zagreb break out.

1914.title: Saint Patrick's Day
1914.body: March 17 (Saint Patrick's Day) – Green beer is invented by Thomas H. Curtin, and displayed at the Schnorrer Club of Morrisania in the Bronx, New York.

1914.title: Tampico Affair
1914.body: Tampico Affair: A misunderstanding involving United States Navy sailors in Mexico and army troops loyal to Mexican dictator Victoriano Huerta leads to a breakdown in diplomatic relations between the United States and Mexico.

1914.title: Britannic
1914.body: The ocean liner that will become HMHS Britannic, sister to the RMS Titanic, is launched at the Harland and Wolff shipyards in Belfast.

1914.title: Belgrade
1914.body: June 29 The Secretary of the Austro-Hungarian Legation at Belgrade sends a dispatch to Vienna, suggesting Serbian complicity in the crime of Sarajevo. Anti-Serb riots continue throughout Bosnia. Khioniya Guseva attempts and fails to assassinate Grigori Rasputin at his hometown in Siberia.

1915.title: The Birth of a Nation
1915.body: The controversial film The Birth of a Nation, directed by D. W. Griffith, premieres in Los Angeles.

1915.title: Formidable
1915.body: January 1 WWI: British Royal Navy battleship HMS Formidable is sunk off Lyme Regis, Dorset, England, by an Imperial German Navy U-boat, with the loss of 547 crew. WWI: Battle of Broken Hill: A train ambush near Broken Hill, Australia, is carried out by two men (claiming to be in support of the Ottoman Empire) who are killed, together with four civilians.

1915.title: WWI
1915.body: WWI: Battle of Broken Hill: A train ambush near Broken Hill, Australia, is carried out by two men (claiming to be in support of the Ottoman Empire) who are killed, together with four civilians.

1915.title: The Tramp
1915.body: Charlie Chaplin's film The Tramp is released in the United States.

1915.title: A Fool There
1915.body: January 12 The United States House of Representatives rejects a proposal to give women the right to vote. A Fool There Was premières in the United States, starring Theda Bara as a femme fatale; she quickly becomes one of early cinema's most sensational stars.

1916.title: WWI
1916.body: WWI: Battle of Wadi – Ottoman Empire forces defeat the British, during the Mesopotamian campaign in modern-day Iraq.

1916.title: McMahon–Hussein Correspondence
1916.body: The McMahon–Hussein Correspondence concludes with an understanding that the United Kingdom would recognise Arab independence in return for Hussein bin Ali, Sharif of Mecca, launching the Arab Revolt against the Ottoman Empire.

1916.title: May 16 United States Marines invade the Dominican Republic
1916.body: May 16 United States Marines invade the Dominican Republic. Britain and France conclude the secret Sykes–Picot Agreement, which is to divide Arab areas of the Ottoman Empire, following the conclusion of WWI and the partitioning of the Ottoman Empire, into French and British spheres of influence.

1916.title: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland
1916.body: Britain and France conclude the secret Sykes–Picot Agreement, which is to divide Arab areas of the Ottoman Empire, following the conclusion of WWI and the partitioning of the Ottoman Empire, into French and British spheres of influence.

1916.title: Arab Revolt
1916.body: The Arab Revolt against the Ottoman Empire, to create a single unified Arab state spanning from Aleppo to Aden, is formally declared by Hussein bin Ali, Sharif of Mecca.

1917.title: The Times
1917.body: ) – Vladimir Lenin's April Theses are published. They become very influential in the following July Days and Bolshevik Revolution. WWI: The Egyptian Expeditionary Force begins the Second Battle of Gaza.

1917.title: John J. Pershing
1917.body: Pershing's troops in Mexico begin withdrawing back to the United States. They reach Columbus, New Mexico February 5.

1917.title: Batangas
1917.body: The Province of Batangas is formally founded, as one of the Philippines' first encomiendas.

1917.title: Vladimir Lenin
1917.body: April 16 (N.S.) (April 3, O.S.) – Vladimir Lenin arrives at the Finland Station in Petrograd after a German-sponsored voyage in a sealed train from his exile in Switzerland through Germany and Scandinavia. WWI: The Nivelle Offensive commences.

1917.title: WWI
1917.body: WWI: Mata Hari is arrested in Paris for spying. Raid on Nekhl: Units of the Egyptian Expeditionary Force completely reoccupy the Egyptian Sinai Peninsula.

1918.title: Hermes
1918.body: January 15 The keel of HMS Hermes is laid in Britain, the first purpose-designed aircraft carrier to be laid down. The Red Army (The Workers and Peasants Red Army) is formed in the Russian SFSR and Soviet Union.

1918.title: Red Army
1918.body: The Red Army (The Workers and Peasants Red Army) is formed in the Russian SFSR and Soviet Union.

1918.title: United States Congress
1918.body: The United States Congress establishes time zones, and approves daylight saving time (DST goes into effect on March 31).

1918.title: March Days
1918.body: March Days: Bolshevik and Armenian Revolutionary Federation forces suppress a Muslim revolt in Baku, Azerbaijan, resulting in up to 30,000 deaths.

1918.title: Finnish Declaration of Independence
1918.body: The Finnish Declaration of Independence is recognized by Soviet Russia, Sweden, Germany and France.

1919.title: Theodore Roosevelt
1919.body: The funeral of Theodore Roosevelt, 26th President of the United States, is held at Christ Church Oyster Bay, Long Island; Roosevelt had died in his sleep at the age of 60, two days earlier.

1919.title: United Artists
1919.body: February 5 United Artists (UA) is incorporated in the United States by D. W. Griffith, Charlie Chaplin, Mary Pickford and Douglas Fairbanks as a motion picture producer.

1919.title: Faisal–Weizmann Agreement
1919.body: The Faisal–Weizmann Agreement is signed by Emir Faisal (representing the Arab Kingdom of Hejaz) and Zionist leader Chaim Weizmann, for Arab–Jewish cooperation in the development of a Jewish homeland in Palestine, and an Arab nation in a large part of the Middle East.

1919.title: League of Nations
1919.body: The League of Nations is founded in Paris, France.

1919.title: Friedrich Ebert
1919.body: January 9 Friedrich Ebert orders the Freikorps into action in Berlin. Estonian War of Independence: Battle of Tapa – Estonian forces liberate Tapa, Estonia, from the Red Army. A group of socialist and liberal deputies table a motion to make Luxembourg a republic.

1920.title: Deutsche Arbeiterpartei
1920.body: Adolf Hitler presents his National Socialist Program in Munich to the German Workers' Party (Deutsche Arbeiterpartei), which renames itself as the Nazi Party (Nationalsozialistische Deutsche Arbeiterpartei).

1920.title: The New York Times
1920.body: The New York Times ridicules American rocket scientist Robert H. Goddard, which it will rescind following the launch of Apollo 11 in 1969.

1920.title: Occupation of Constantinople
1920.body: Constantinople is occupied by British Empire forces, acting for the Allied Powers against the Turkish National Movement. Retrospectively, the Grand National Assembly of Turkey regards this as the dissolution of the Ottoman regime in Istanbul.

1920.title: Marinduque
1920.body: The island province of Marinduque in the Philippines archipelago is founded.

1920.title: Social democracy
1920.body: The world's first peaceful establishment of a social democratic government takes place in Sweden as Hjalmar Branting takes over as prime minister when Nils Edén leaves office.

1921.title: February 21 1921 Persian coup d'état
1921.body: February 21 1921 Persian coup d'état: Rezā Khan and Zia'eddin Tabatabaee stage a coup d'état in Qajar dynasty Iran. Conference of London of 1921–1922 convenes in an attempt to resolve problems arising from the dissolution of the Ottoman Empire.

1921.title: 1921 Persian coup d'état
1921.body: 1921 Persian coup d'état: Rezā Khan and Zia'eddin Tabatabaee stage a coup d'état in Qajar dynasty Iran.

1921.title: Armenia
1921.body: Armenian Soghomon Tehlirian assassinates Mehmed Talaat, former Interior Minister of the Ottoman Empire, in Charlottenburg, Berlin.

1921.title: The Kid
1921.body: The full-length silent comedy drama film The Kid, written, produced, directed by and starring Charlie Chaplin (in his Tramp character), with Jackie Coogan, is released in the United States.

1921.title: Santa Isabel
1921.body: January 2 The Association football club Cruzeiro Esporte Clube, from Belo Horizonte, is founded as the multi-sports club Palestra Italia by Italian expatriates in Brazil. The Spanish liner Santa Isabel breaks in two and sinks off Villa Garcia, Mexico, with the loss of 244 of the 300 people on board.

1922.title: Joseph Stalin
1922.body: Joseph Stalin is appointed General Secretary of the Central Committee of the Soviet Communist Party.

1922.title: Edward VIII
1922.body: Edward, Prince of Wales, inaugurates the Prince of Wales Royal Indian Military College in Dehradun, India, marking a capitulation of the Governor General and Secretary of State for India to growing pressure for Indianization of the officer cadre of the Indian Army.

1922.title: Renown
1922.body: The United Kingdom's Prince of Wales arrives in Yokohama aboard HMS Renown and rides by train to Tokyo, starting a one-month visit to Japan.

1922.title: President of the United States
1922.body: February 8 President of the United States Warren G. Harding introduces the first radio in the White House. In the Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic, the Cheka becomes the Gosudarstvennoye Politicheskoye Upravlenie (GPU), a section of the NKVD.

1922.title: Mahatma Gandhi
1922.body: Mahatma Gandhi is arrested in Bombay for sedition.

1923.title: Wedding of Prince Albert and Lady Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon
1923.body: Wedding of Prince Albert and Lady Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon: The future King George VI of the United Kingdom marries the future Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother in Westminster Abbey.

1923.title: Vladimir Lenin
1923.body: Vladimir Lenin suffers his third stroke, which renders him bedridden and unable to speak; consequently he retires from his position as Chairman of the Soviet government.

1923.title: Dobrolyot
1923.body: Dobrolyot is formed as the first Soviet civil aviation service; it will become part of flag carrier Aeroflot.

1923.title: Rostov, Yaroslavl Oblast
1923.body: Large hailstones kill 23 people in Rostov, Soviet Union.

1923.title: Treaty of Lausanne
1923.body: The Treaty of Lausanne (1923), settling the boundaries of the modern Republic of Turkey, is signed in Switzerland by Greece, Bulgaria and other countries that fought in the First World War, bringing an end to the Ottoman Empire after 624 years.

1924.title: Adolf Hitler
1924.body: April 1 Adolf Hitler is sentenced to 5 years in Landsberg Prison in Germany for his participation in the 1923 Beer Hall Putsch (he serves less than 9 months). The first revenue flight for Belgium's Sabena Airlines takes place.

1924.title: Ottoman Caliphate
1924.body: The Ottoman Caliphate, a remnant of the Ottoman monarchy abolished on November 1, 1922, and one of the historical claims of succession to the Islamic State of Muhammad, comes to an end with the deposition of the Caliph of the Ottoman dynasty. This marks the end of an era, giving way to the drastic political transformations of Turkey introduced by reforms because of the President Mustafa Kemal Atatürk.

1924.title: Kuomintang
1924.body: Kuomintang in China holds its first National Congress, initiating a policy of alliance with the Soviet Union and the Chinese Communist Party.

1924.title: Soviet Union
1924.body: The United Kingdom recognizes the Soviet Union.

1924.title: August Uprising
1924.body: August Uprising: Georgia rises against rule by the Soviet Union in an abortive rebellion, in which several thousands die.

1925.title: Mein Kampf
1925.body: Adolf Hitler publishes Volume 1 of his personal manifesto Mein Kampf in Germany.

1925.title: New York City Fire Department
1925.body: New York City Fire Department Rescue 2 is put in service in Brooklyn.

1925.title: İsmet İnönü
1925.body: March 4 İsmet İnönü is appointed prime minister in Turkey (Turkey's 4th and İnönü's 3rd government).

1925.title: Stanley Baldwin
1925.body: Presenting the Stanley Baldwin government's budget, Chancellor of the Exchequer Winston Churchill announces Britain's return to the gold standard.

1925.title: Pionerskaya Pravda
1925.body: Pionerskaya Pravda, one of the oldest children's newspapers in Europe, is founded in the Soviet Union.

1926.title: Lebanon
1926.body: The first Lebanese constitution is established.

1926.title: Assassination attempts on Benito Mussolini
1926.body: An assassination attempt against Italian Fascist leader Benito Mussolini fails.

1926.title: Shakespeare Memorial Theatre
1926.body: March 6 The Shakespeare Memorial Theatre in Stratford-upon-Avon (England) is destroyed by fire. The first commercial air route from the United Kingdom to South Africa is established by Alan Cobham.

1926.title: South Africa
1926.body: The first commercial air route from the United Kingdom to South Africa is established by Alan Cobham.

1926.title: Rif War
1926.body: The Rif War ends, when Rif rebels surrender in Morocco.

1927.title: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences
1927.body: The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences, which will create the Academy Awards, is founded in the United States.

1927.title: Treaty of Jeddah
1927.body: By the Treaty of Jeddah, the United Kingdom recognizes the sovereignty of Ibn Saud over the Kingdom of Hejaz and Nejd, the future Saudi Arabia.

1927.title: Diplomatic relations
1927.body: The United Kingdom cuts its diplomatic relations with the Soviet Union due to revelations of espionage and underground agitation.

1927.title: Cardiff City F.C.
1927.body: Cardiff City wins the FA Cup, beating Arsenal 1–0; it is the only time a team from outside England has won the competition.

1927.title: Company
1927.body: The British Broadcasting Company becomes the British Broadcasting Corporation, when its royal charter of incorporation takes effect. John Reith becomes the first Director-General.

1928.title: Eastern Bloc emigration and defection
1928.body: Eastern Bloc emigration and defection: Boris Bazhanov, Joseph Stalin's personal secretary, crosses the border to Iran to defect from the Soviet Union.

1928.title: Oceanic
1928.body: June 28 The keel of the first 1,000 ft (300 m)-long ocean liner, Oceanic, for the British White Star Line, is laid by Harland and Wolff in Belfast; construction is delayed, and cancelled on 23 July 1929. The International Railway (New York–Ontario) switches to one-man crews for its trolleys in Canada.

1928.title: International Railway
1928.body: The International Railway (New York–Ontario) switches to one-man crews for its trolleys in Canada.

1928.title: Pineapple Primary
1928.body: Pineapple Primary: The United States Republican Party primary elections in Chicago are preceded by violence, bombings and assassination attempts (two politicians are killed, Octavius C. Granady and Giuseppe Esposito).

1928.title: Benito Mussolini
1928.body: A bomb attack against Italian Fascist leader Benito Mussolini in Milan kills 17 bystanders.

1929.title: Wings
1929.body: The 1st Academy Awards are presented in a 15-minute ceremony at the Hollywood Roosevelt Hotel, honoring the best movies of 1927 and 1928, Wings (1927) winning Best Picture. Gerald Duffy (died 1928) receives the only Academy Award for Best Title Writing ever awarded (for his intertitles to the silent film The Private Life of Helen of Troy (1927)).

1929.title: Litvinov's Pact
1929.body: "Litvinov's Pact" is signed in Moscow by the Soviet Union, Poland, Estonia, Romania and Latvia, who agree not to use force to settle disputes between themselves.

1929.title: San Juan
1929.body: August 29 The 1929 Palestine riots: 18–20 Jews are killed in Safed by Palestinian Arabs. The SS San Juan collides with the oil tanker S.C.T. Dodd off the California coast, causing the San Juan to sink in 3 minutes, killing 77 people.

1929.title: Graf Zeppelin
1929.body: German rigid airship LZ 127 Graf Zeppelin makes a circumnavigation of the Northern Hemisphere eastabout out of Lakehurst, New Jersey, including the first nonstop flight of any kind across the Pacific Ocean (Tokyo–Los Angeles).

1929.title: January 6 6 January Dictatorship
1929.body: January 6 6 January Dictatorship: King Alexander of the Serbs, Croats, and Slovenes suspends his country's constitution. Albanian missionary sister Agnes Gonxha Bojaxhiu, later known as Mother Teresa, arrives in Calcutta from Ireland to begin her work in India.

1930.title: Turkey
1930.body: The government of Turkey requests the international community to adopt Istanbul and Ankara, as the official names for Constantinople and Angora. The U.S. State Department adopts the "Istanbul" form in May.

1930.title: Pavel Molchanov
1930.body: Pavel Molchanov launches a radiosonde from Slutsk in the Soviet Union.

1930.title: George Washington
1930.body: The dedication of George Washington's sculpted head is held at Mount Rushmore, South Dakota.

1930.title: President of the United States
1930.body: President Herbert Hoover signs the Smoot–Hawley Tariff Act into law, implementing protectionist trade policies in the United States. It has the incidental effect of defining an antique as older than 1830.

1930.title: Moon
1930.body: The Moon moves into its nearest point to Earth, called perigee, at the same time as its fullest phase of the Lunar Cycle. This is the closest moon distance at 356,397 km (221,455 mi) in recent history, and the next one will be on January 1, 2257, at 356,371 km (221,439 mi).

1931.title: City Lights
1931.body: Charlie Chaplin comedy drama film City Lights receives its public premiere at the Los Angeles Theater with Albert Einstein as guest of honor. Contrary to the current trend in cinema, it is a silent film, but with a score by Chaplin. Critically and commercially successful from the start, it will place consistently in lists of films considered the best of all time.

1931.title: Kemal Atatürk
1931.body: Kemal Atatürk is re-elected president of Turkey.

1931.title: Joseph Stalin
1931.body: Soviet leader Joseph Stalin gives a speech calling for rapid industrialization, arguing that only strong industrialized countries will win wars, while "weak" nations are "beaten". Stalin states: "We are fifty or a hundred years behind the advanced countries. We must make good this distance in ten years.

1931.title: Ready for Labour and Defence of the USSR
1931.body: The Ready for Labour and Defence of the USSR programme, abbreviated as GTO, is introduced in the Soviet Union.

1931.title: Lublin
1931.body: A violent tornado strikes the city of Lublin, Poland.

1932.title: Adolf Hitler
1932.body: Adolf Hitler obtains German citizenship by naturalization, opening the opportunity for him to run in the 1932 German presidential election.

1932.title: Paul Gorguloff
1932.body: May 6 Paul Gorguloff shoots French president Paul Doumer in Paris; Doumer dies the next day. The politically powerful General Kurt von Schleicher meets secretly with Adolf Hitler. Schleicher tells Hitler that he is scheming to bring down the Brüning government in Germany and asks for Nazi support of the new "presidential government" Schleicher is planning to form.

1932.title: Gladys Elinor Watkins
1932.body: April 25 Gladys Elinor Watkins consecrates the carillon of the National War Memorial in New Zealand. The bodies of Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman and Jabir ibn Abd Allah, two of the companions of Islamic prophet Muhammad, are moved from their graves in Salmaan Paak following a dream of King Faisal I of Iraq that they are affected by water.

1932.title: Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman
1932.body: The bodies of Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman and Jabir ibn Abd Allah, two of the companions of Islamic prophet Muhammad, are moved from their graves in Salmaan Paak following a dream of King Faisal I of Iraq that they are affected by water.

1932.title: Mahatma Gandhi
1932.body: The British authorities in India arrest and intern Mahatma Gandhi and Vallabhbhai Patel.

1933.title: Nazi Party
1933.body: January 30 Nazi Party leader Adolf Hitler is appointed Chancellor of Germany by President of Germany Paul von Hindenburg. Édouard Daladier forms a government in France in succession to Joseph Paul-Boncour. He is succeeded on October 26 by Albert Sarraut and on November 26 by Camille Chautemps.

1933.title: Adolf Hitler
1933.body: Adolf Hitler gives his "Proclamation to the German People" in Berlin.

1933.title: Gleichschaltung
1933.body: Gleichschaltung: The Reichstag passes the Enabling Act, making Adolf Hitler effectively the dictator of Germany.

1933.title: Now or Never; Are We to Live or Perish Forever?
1933.body: "Pakistan Declaration": Choudhry Rahmat Ali publishes (in Cambridge, UK) a pamphlet entitled Now or Never; Are We to Live or Perish Forever?, in which he calls for the creation of a Muslim state in northwest India that he calls "Pakstan"; this influences the Pakistan Movement.

1933.title: King Kong
1933.body: King Kong: The original King Kong film, starring Fay Wray and directed by Merian C. Cooper, debuts in New York City.

1934.title: Adolf Hitler
1934.body: Adolf Hitler and Benito Mussolini meet for the first time, at the Venice Biennale.

1934.title: Führer
1934.body: Adolf Hitler becomes Führer of Germany, or head of state combined with that of Chancellor, following the death of President Paul von Hindenburg.

1934.title: Wehrmacht
1934.body: The Wehrmacht swears a personal oath of loyalty to Adolf Hitler.

1934.title: Gaston Doumergue
1934.body: February 9 Gaston Doumergue forms a new government in France. Greece, Romania, Turkey and Yugoslavia form the Balkan Pact.

1934.title: Second Hellenic Republic
1934.body: Greece, Romania, Turkey and Yugoslavia form the Balkan Pact.

1935.title: Reza Shah
1935.body: Reza Shah of Iran asks the international community to formally adopt the name "Iran" to refer to the country, in place of the name "Persia".

1935.title: Nazi Germany
1935.body: February 26 In Nazi Germany, Adolf Hitler orders reinstatement of the air force, the Luftwaffe, in violation of the 1919 Treaty of Versailles. Robert Watson-Watt first demonstrates the use of radar to detect aircraft, at Daventry in the UK.

1935.title: Adolf Hitler
1935.body: Adolf Hitler announces German re-armament in violation of the 1919 Treaty of Versailles.

1935.title: Sun Myung Moon
1935.body: Sun Myung Moon, a teenage Presbyterian convert in Korea under Japanese rule, claims to have a revelation from Jesus, telling him to complete his mission from almost 2,000 years ago.

1935.title: Franklin D. Roosevelt
1935.body: United States President Franklin D. Roosevelt signs the Social Security Act into law.

1936.title: Treaty of Versailles
1936.body: In violation of the Treaty of Versailles and Locarno Treaties, Nazi Germany reoccupies the Rhineland. Hitler and other Nazis later admit that the French army alone could have destroyed the Wehrmacht.

1936.title: Modern Times
1936.body: Modern Times, widely considered one of the greatest films ever, premieres in New York City.

1936.title: 1936 United States presidential election
1936.body: 1936 United States presidential election: Franklin D. Roosevelt is reelected to a second term, in a landslide victory over Kansas Governor Alf Landon; farmers support Roosevelt.

1936.title: Montreux Convention Regarding the Regime of the Straits
1936.body: The Montreux Convention Regarding the Regime of the Straits is signed in Montreux, allowing Turkey to fortify the Dardanelles and the Bosphorus, but guaranteeing free passage to ships of all nations in peacetime.

1936.title: Joseph Stalin
1936.body: October Joseph Stalin's Great Purge begins in the Soviet Union. The Viking Age craftsman's Mästermyr chest is discovered in the Mästermyr mire (after which it is later named), west of Hemse, on the island of Gotland, Sweden.

1937.title: Moscow Trials
1937.body: Moscow Trials: Trial of the Anti-Soviet Trotskyist Center – In the Soviet Union 17 leading Communists go on trial, accused of participating in a plot led by Leon Trotsky to overthrow Joseph Stalin's regime, and assassinate its leaders.

1937.title: Wallis Simpson
1937.body: Wallis Simpson marries the Duke of Windsor, the former Edward VIII, in France.

1937.title: Colony of Aden
1937.body: April 1 Aden becomes a British crown colony. The Bombing of Jaén is carried out in Spain, by the Condor Legion of the Nazi German Luftwaffe.

1937.title: Bombing of Jaén
1937.body: The Bombing of Jaén is carried out in Spain, by the Condor Legion of the Nazi German Luftwaffe.

1937.title: Spanish Civil War
1937.body: Spanish Civil War: The Bombing of Guernica is carried out in Spain, by the Condor Legion of the Nazi German Luftwaffe, in support of the Francoists. Three-quarters of the town is destroyed and hundreds killed.

1938.title: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
1938.body: February 4 Adolf Hitler abolishes the War Ministry and creates the Oberkommando der Wehrmacht (High Command of the Armed Forces), giving him direct control of the German military. In addition, he dismisses political and military leaders considered unsympathetic to his philosophy or policies. General Werner von Fritsch is forced to resign as Commander of Chief of the German Army following accusations of homosexuality, and replaced by General Walther von Brauchitsch.

1938.title: Adolf Hitler
1938.body: Adolf Hitler abolishes the War Ministry and creates the Oberkommando der Wehrmacht (High Command of the Armed Forces), giving him direct control of the German military. In addition, he dismisses political and military leaders considered unsympathetic to his philosophy or policies. General Werner von Fritsch is forced to resign as Commander of Chief of the German Army following accusations of homosexuality, and replaced by General Walther von Brauchitsch.

1938.title: May 25 Spanish Civil War
1938.body: May 25 Spanish Civil War: Alicante is bombed by fascist rebels, resulting in 313 deaths. The Soviet ambassador to the United States, A. A.

1938.title: Soviet Union
1938.body: The Soviet ambassador to the United States, A. A. Troyanovsky, declares Moscow ready to defend Czechoslovakia.

1938.title: State-owned enterprise
1938.body: State-owned railway networks are created by merger, in France (SNCF) and the Netherlands (Nederlandse Spoorwegen – NS).

1939.title: January 1 Coming into effect in Nazi Germany
1939.body: January 1 Coming into effect in Nazi Germany of: The Protection of Young Persons Act, passed on April 30, 1938, the Working Hours Regulations. The small businesses obligation to maintain adequate accounting. The Jews name change decree.

1939.title: Hewlett-Packard
1939.body: The Hewlett-Packard technology and scientific instruments manufacturing company is founded by Bill Hewlett and David Packard, in a garage in Palo Alto, California, considered the birthplace of Silicon Valley.

1939.title: New Year
1939.body: With his traditional call to the New Year in Nazi Germany, Führer and Reich Chancellor Adolf Hitler addresses the members of the National Socialist German Workers' Party (NSDAP).

1939.title: Kriegsmarine
1939.body: Adolf Hitler orders Plan Z, a 5-year naval expansion programme intended to provide for a huge German fleet capable of crushing the British Royal Navy by 1944. The Kriegsmarine is given the first priority on the allotment of German economic resources.

1939.title: Adolf Hitler
1939.body: Adolf Hitler advises Jozef Tiso to declare Slovakia's independence, in order to prevent its partition by Hungary and Poland.

1940.title: WWII
1940.body: WWII: Luftwaffe Chief and Generalfeldmarschall Hermann Göring assumes control of most war industries in Germany, in his capacity as Plenipotentiary for the Four Year Plan.

1940.title: Pakistan Movement
1940.body: Pakistan Movement: The Lahore Resolution, calling for greater autonomy for what will become Pakistan in British India, is drawn up by the All-India Muslim League during a three-day general session at Iqbal Park, Lahore.

1940.title: Katyn massacre
1940.body: Katyn massacre: Members of the Soviet Politburo (Joseph Stalin, Vyacheslav Molotov, Lazar Kaganovich, Mikhail Kalinin, Kliment Voroshilov and Lavrentiy Beria) sign an order, prepared by Beria, for the execution of 25,700 Polish intelligentsia, including 14,700 Polish POWs.

1940.title: Moscow Peace Treaty
1940.body: Moscow Peace Treaty: The Soviet Union and Finland sign a peace treaty in Moscow, ending the Winter War; Finns, along with the world at large, are shocked by the harsh terms.

1940.title: Udham Singh
1940.body: Indian nationalist Udham Singh assassinates Sir Michael O'Dwyer (in revenge for the 1919 Jallianwala Bagh massacre) at Caxton Hall in London, for which he is hanged on 31 July at HM Prison Pentonville.

1941.title: Normalschrifterlass
1941.body: A decree (Normalschrifterlass) promulgated in Germany by Martin Bormann, on behalf of Adolf Hitler, requires replacement of blackletter typefaces by Antiqua.

1941.title: Charles Lindbergh
1941.body: Aviator Charles Lindbergh testifies before the U.S. Congress, and recommends that the United States negotiate a neutrality pact with Adolf Hitler.

1941.title: Missouri
1941.body: January 6 During his State of the Union address, President of the United States Franklin D. Roosevelt presents his Four Freedoms, as fundamental global human rights. The keel of battleship USS Missouri is laid at the New York Navy Yard in Brooklyn.

1941.title: State of the Union
1941.body: During his State of the Union address, President of the United States Franklin D. Roosevelt presents his Four Freedoms, as fundamental global human rights.

1941.title: Franklin D. Roosevelt
1941.body: Franklin D. Roosevelt, having been President of the United States for 8 years, 1 day, becomes the longest-serving president in American history.

1942.title: Endlösung
1942.body: The Holocaust: Nazis at the Wannsee Conference convened by Reinhard Heydrich in Berlin decide that the "Final Solution (Endlösung) to the Jewish problem" is deportations to extermination camps.

1942.title: WWII
1942.body: WWII: New Zealand Squadron No. 488 loses two aircraft and five damaged against a force of 27 Japanese fighters, in their first combat mission in Singapore.

1942.title: No. 488 Squadron RNZAF
1942.body: New Zealand Squadron No. 488 loses two aircraft and five damaged against a force of 27 Japanese fighters, in their first combat mission in Singapore.

1942.title: February 8 WWII
1942.body: February 8 WWII: Battle of Singapore: Japanese forces of the 5th Division and 18th Division (some 23,000 men) begin to cross the Johor Strait and attack the Australian 22nd Brigade (some 3,000 men) at Singapore. WWII: Daylight saving time goes into effect in the United States. WWII: Top United States military leaders hold their first formal meeting to discuss American military strategy in the war.

1942.title: Dutch East Indies campaign
1942.body: Dutch East Indies campaign: Japan declares war on the Netherlands and the Dutch East Indies. Japanese forces invade Borneo and Celebes.

1943.title: WWII
1943.body: WWII: Casablanca Conference: Franklin D. Roosevelt, President of the United States; Winston Churchill, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom; and Generals Charles de Gaulle and Henri Giraud of the Free French forces meet secretly at the Anfa Hotel in Casablanca, Morocco, to plan the Allied European strategy for the next stage of the war.

1943.title: January 22 WWII
1943.body: January 22 WWII: Battle of Buna–Gona: American and Australian forces secure control of the territory of Papua. The Holocaust: Round up of Marseille begins – Over 4,000 Jews are arrested in Nazi-occupied Marseille as part of "Action Tiger", before being transported to extermination camps in Poland.

1943.title: The Holocaust
1943.body: The Holocaust: Round up of Marseille begins – Over 4,000 Jews are arrested in Nazi-occupied Marseille as part of "Action Tiger", before being transported to extermination camps in Poland.

1943.title: February 9 WWII
1943.body: February 9 WWII: The Guadalcanal campaign in the Solomon Islands ends with United States forces in command of Guadalcanal, the evacuation of Japanese forces in Operation Ke having been completed two days earlier. The Holocaust: Rue Sainte-Catherine Roundup – The Gestapo arrest 86 Jews in Lyon, 83 of whom are then sent to extermination camps.

1943.title: Iraq
1943.body: Iraq declares war on the Axis powers.

1944.title: Konzentrationslager Plaszow bei Krakau
1944.body: January 11 United States President Franklin D. Roosevelt proposes a Second Bill of Rights for social and economic security, in his State of the Union address. The Nazi German administration expands Kraków-Płaszów concentration camp into the larger standalone Konzentrationslager Plaszow bei Krakau in occupied Poland.

1944.title: Franklin D. Roosevelt
1944.body: United States President Franklin D. Roosevelt proposes a Second Bill of Rights for social and economic security, in his State of the Union address.

1944.title: WWII
1944.body: WWII: Winston Churchill and Charles de Gaulle begin a 2-day conference in Marrakesh.

1944.title: January 15 WWII
1944.body: January 15 WWII: The 27th Polish Home Army Infantry Division is re-created, marking the start of Operation Tempest by the Polish Home Army, a resistance force. 1944 San Juan earthquake: An earthquake hits San Juan, Argentina, killing an estimated 10,000 people, in the worst natural disaster in Argentina's history. The Battle of Monte Cassino begins in Italy.

1944.title: Soviet Union
1944.body: The Soviet Union ceases production of the Mosin–Nagant 1891/30 sniper rifle.

1945.title: Führerbunker
1945.body: WWII: Adolf Hitler takes residence in the Führerbunker in Berlin.

1945.title: Wilhelm Gustloff
1945.body: WWII: MV Wilhelm Gustloff, with over 10,000 mainly civilian Germans from Gotenhafen (Gdynia) is sunk in Gdańsk Bay by three torpedoes from Soviet submarine S-13 in the Baltic Sea; up to 9,400, 5,000 of whom are children, are thought to have died – the greatest loss of life in a single ship sinking in history. Raid at Cabanatuan: 121 American soldiers and 800 Filipino guerrillas free 813 American prisoners of war from the Japanese-held camp in the city of Cabanatuan, in the Philippines. Adolf Hitler makes his last public speech, on broadcast radio, expressing the belief that Germany will triumph.

1945.title: Adolf Hitler
1945.body: Adolf Hitler makes his last public speech, on broadcast radio, expressing the belief that Germany will triumph.

1945.title: WWII
1945.body: WWII: President Franklin D. Roosevelt, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom Winston Churchill and Soviet leader Joseph Stalin hold the Yalta Conference.

1945.title: January 17 WWII
1945.body: January 17 WWII: The Soviet Union occupies Warsaw, Poland. The Holocaust: Swedish diplomat Raoul Wallenberg, who has saved thousands of Jews, is taken into custody by a Soviet patrol during the Siege of Budapest and is never again seen publicly.

1946.title: The Lost Weekend
1946.body: The 18th Academy Awards Ceremony is held. Best Picture goes to The Lost Weekend.

1946.title: Iron Curtain
1946.body: In a speech at Westminster College, in Fulton, Missouri, Winston Churchill talks about the Iron Curtain.

1946.title: Strong
1946.body: 6 Mw Aleutian Islands earthquake shakes the Aleutian Islands, with a maximum Mercalli intensity of VI (Strong). A destructive tsunami reaches the Hawaiian Islands, resulting in many deaths, mostly in Hilo. Between 165 and 173 are killed.

1946.title: Malayan Union
1946.body: The Malayan Union is formed on dissolution of the British Military Administration and Singapore becomes a Crown colony.

1946.title: Permanent Court of International Justice
1946.body: January 31 The last session of the Permanent Court of International Justice occurs. Yugoslavia's new constitution, modeled on the Soviet Union, establishes 6 constituent republics (Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatia, Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia and Slovenia).

1947.title: Republic of Mahabad
1947.body: The leaders of the Kurdish People's Republic of Mahabad, the second Kurdish state in the history of Iran, are hanged at Chuwarchira Square in Mahabad, after the state has been overrun by the Iranian army.

1947.title: Edwin Land
1947.body: Edwin Land demonstrates the first "instant camera", his Polaroid Land Camera, to a meeting of the Optical Society of America in New York City.

1947.title: The Best Years of Our Lives
1947.body: The 19th Academy Awards Ceremony is held. The movie The Best Years of Our Lives wins the Academy Award for Best Picture, along with several other Academy Awards.

1947.title: Sikhote-Alin meteorite
1947.body: February 12 The fragments of a meteorite create a number of impact craters up to 36 metres wide in Sikhote-Alin, in the Soviet Union. In Burma, the Panglong Agreement is reached between the Burmese government under its leader, General Aung San, and the Shan, Kachin, and Chin ethnic peoples at the Panglong Conference. U Aung Zan Wai, Pe Khin, Major Aung, Sir Maung Gyi, Sein Mya Maung and Myoma U Than Kywe are among the negotiators.

1947.title: Cold War
1947.body: Cold War: The Voice of America begins to transmit radio broadcasts into Eastern Europe and the Soviet Union

1948.title: Gentleman's Agreement
1948.body: March 20 Singapore holds its first elections. Renowned Italian conductor Arturo Toscanini makes his television debut, conducting the NBC Symphony Orchestra in an all-Wagner program in the United States. The 20th Academy Awards Ceremony is held in Los Angeles.

1948.title: British Ceylon
1948.body: Ceylon (later known as Sri Lanka) becomes an independent country, within the British Commonwealth adopting King George VI as the King of Ceylon.

1948.title: Soviet Union
1948.body: February 1 The Soviet Union begins to jam Voice of America broadcasts. The Federation of Malaya is proclaimed.

1948.title: Ernest Bevin
1948.body: British foreign secretary Ernest Bevin proposes the formation of a Western Union between Britain, France and the Benelux countries to stand up against the Soviet Union. The Treaty of Brussels is signed March 17 as a consequence, a predecessor to NATO.

1948.title: Mahatma Gandhi
1948.body: Mahatma Gandhi begins his fast-unto-death in Delhi, to stop communal violence during the Partition of India.

1949.title: Israel
1949.body: May 11 Israel is admitted to the United Nations, as its 59th member. Siam officially changes its French name to "Thaïlande" (English name to "Thailand"), having officially changed its Thai name to "Prated Thai" since 1939.

1949.title: Volkswagen Beetle
1949.body: The first VW Type 1 to arrive in the United States, a 1948 model, is brought to New York by Dutch businessman Ben Pon. Unable to interest dealers or importers in the Volkswagen, Pon sells the sample car to pay his travel expenses. Only two 1949 models are sold in America that year, convincing Volkswagen chairman Heinrich Nordhoff the car has no future in the U.S. (The Type 1 goes on to become an automotive phenomenon.)

1949.title: Hamlet
1949.body: The 21st Academy Awards Ceremony is held. The movie Hamlet wins the Academy Award for Best Picture.

1949.title: British Empire
1949.body: The former British colony of Newfoundland joins Canada, as its 10th province.

1949.title: Şemsettin Günaltay
1949.body: Şemsettin Günaltay forms the new government of Turkey. It is the 18th government, last single party government of the Republican People's Party.

1950.title: Israel
1950.body: The Israeli government recognizes the People's Republic of China.

1950.title: Knesset
1950.body: The Knesset passes a resolution that states Jerusalem is the capital of Israel.

1950.title: European Broadcasting Union
1950.body: February 12 The European Broadcasting Union is founded. Albert Einstein warns that nuclear war could lead to mutual destruction.

1950.title: Albert Einstein
1950.body: Albert Einstein warns that nuclear war could lead to mutual destruction.

1950.title: Cold War
1950.body: Cold War: The Soviet Union and the People's Republic of China sign a mutual defense treaty (terminated in 1979). In an election speech at Edinburgh, Winston Churchill proposes "a parley at the summit" with Soviet leaders, the first use of the term "summit" for such a meeting.

1951.title: The King and
1951.body: March 29 Second Red Scare: In the United States, Julius and Ethel Rosenberg are convicted of conspiracy to commit espionage. On April 5 they are sentenced to death. Rodgers and Hammerstein's The King and I opens on Broadway, and runs for three years.

1951.title: All About Eve
1951.body: The 23rd Academy Awards Ceremony is held; All About Eve wins the Best Picture award and five others.

1951.title: July 10 Korean War
1951.body: July 10 Korean War: Armistice negotiations begin at Kaesong. A formal peace agreement between Canada and Germany is signed.

1951.title: George
1951.body: May 3 King George VI opens the Festival of Britain in London, including the Royal Festival Hall. S. S.

1951.title: Operation Ezra and Nehemiah
1951.body: March – The Israeli government organizes Operation Ezra and Nehemiah, a mass airlift of Jews out of Iraq.

1952.title: The Hague
1952.body: April 4 At a tribunal in The Hague, Israel demands reparations worth $3 billion from West Germany. West Ice accidents: During a severe storm in the West Ice, east of Greenland, 78 seal hunters on 5 Norwegian seal hunting vessels vanish without a trace.

1952.title: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
1952.body: February 6 Princess Elizabeth, Duchess of Edinburgh, becomes monarch of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the British Dominions: Canada, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa, Pakistan and Ceylon. The princess, who is on a visit to Kenya when she hears of the death of her father, King George VI, aged 56, takes the regnal name Elizabeth II. In the United States, a mechanical heart is used for the first time in a human patient.

1952.title: New York City
1952.body: New York City announces its first crosswalk devices to be installed.

1952.title: Farouk of Egypt
1952.body: Farouk of Egypt has himself announced as a descendant of the Islamic prophet, Muhammad.

1952.title: George
1952.body: The State Funeral of King George VI of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland and the British Dominions and the last Emperor of India, takes place. George VI's coffin is brought in procession through London to Paddington Station where a royal train brings the King to Windsor, where his funeral and burial take place at St. George's Chapel at Windsor Castle.

1953.title: Dwight D. Eisenhower
1953.body: February 11 United States President Dwight D. Eisenhower refuses a clemency appeal for Ethel and Julius Rosenberg. The Soviet Union breaks diplomatic relations with Israel, after a bomb explodes at the Soviet Embassy, in reaction to the 'Doctors' plot'.

1953.title: Soviet Union
1953.body: The Soviet Union breaks diplomatic relations with Israel, after a bomb explodes at the Soviet Embassy, in reaction to the 'Doctors' plot'.

1953.title: James Watson
1953.body: February 28 James Watson and Francis Crick of Britain's University of Cambridge announce their discovery of the structure of the DNA molecule. Greece, Turkey, and Yugoslavia sign the Balkan Pact.

1953.title: Kingdom of Greece
1953.body: Greece, Turkey, and Yugoslavia sign the Balkan Pact.

1953.title: violent
1953.body: The Yenice–Gönen earthquake affects western Turkey, with a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (violent), causing at least 1,070 deaths, and $3.57 million in damage.

1954.title: 1954 transfer of Crimea
1954.body: 1954 transfer of Crimea: The Soviet Politburo of the Soviet Union orders the transfer of the Crimean Oblast from the Russian SFSR to the Ukrainian SSR.

1954.title: 26th Academy Awards
1954.body: March 25 The 26th Academy Awards Ceremony is held. The Soviet Union recognises the sovereignty of East Germany. Soviet troops remain in the country.

1954.title: Soviet Union
1954.body: The Soviet Union recognises the sovereignty of East Germany. Soviet troops remain in the country.

1954.title: Petrov Affair
1954.body: Petrov Affair: Diplomat Vladimir Petrov defects from the Soviet Union and asks for political asylum in Australia.

1954.title: Vietnam
1954.body: After authorizing $385 million over the $400 million already budgeted for military aid to Vietnam, President of the United States Dwight D. Eisenhower warns against his country's intervention in Vietnam.

1955.title: Winston Churchill
1955.body: April 5 Winston Churchill resigns as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom, due to ill-health, at the age of 80. Richard J. Daley defeats Robert Merrian to become Mayor of Chicago, by a vote of 708,222 to 581,555.

1955.title: EOKA
1955.body: EOKA starts a resistance campaign against British rule in the Crown colony of Cyprus.

1955.title: Presidium of the Supreme Soviet
1955.body: The Presidium of the Supreme Soviet of the Soviet Union announces the end of the war between the USSR and Germany, which began during World War II in 1941.

1955.title: Soviet Union
1955.body: Eight Communist Bloc countries, including the Soviet Union, sign a mutual defence treaty in Warsaw, Poland, that is called the Warsaw Pact (it will be dissolved in 1991).

1955.title: Mir mine
1955.body: Mir mine, the first diamond mine in the Soviet Union, is discovered.

1956.title: Pakistan
1956.body: Pakistan becomes the first Islamic republic, and a national holiday is observed in the country, including the state of East Pakistan.

1956.title: Marty
1956.body: The 28th Academy Awards Ceremony is held in Los Angeles. Marty is awarded Best Picture.

1956.title: Makarios III
1956.body: March 9 The British deport Archbishop Makarios from Cyprus to the Seychelles. The Soviet Armed Forces suppress mass demonstrations in the Georgian Soviet Socialist Republic, reacting to Nikita Khrushchev's de-Stalinization policy.

1956.title: Nikita Khrushchev
1956.body: Nikita Khrushchev attacks the veneration of Joseph Stalin, in a speech "On the Cult of Personality and Its Consequences", at a secret session concluding the 20th Congress of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union. This is not officially made public in the Soviet Union at this time but becomes known in the West in June.

1956.title: Porkkala
1956.body: Finnish troops reoccupy Porkkala, after Soviet troops vacate its military base. Civilians can return February 4.

1957.title: Israel
1957.body: Israel withdraws from the Sinai Peninsula (captured from Egypt on October 29, 1956).

1957.title: Iskander Mirza
1957.body: President Iskander Mirza of Pakistan lays the foundation-stone of the Guddu Barrage across the Indus River, near Sukkur.

1957.title: Around the World in 80 Days
1957.body: The 29th Academy Awards Ceremony is held in Hollywood. Around the World in 80 Days wins Best Picture.

1957.title: 1957 Fethiye earthquakes
1957.body: The 1957 Fethiye earthquakes occur on the Mediterranean coast of Turkey.

1957.title: Singapore
1957.body: The United Kingdom announces that Singapore will gain self-rule on January 1, 1958.

1958.title: The Bridge on the River Kwai
1958.body: The 30th Academy Awards Ceremony takes place in Hollywood; The Bridge on the River Kwai wins 7 awards, including Academy Award for Best Picture.

1958.title: Hashemites
1958.body: The Hashemite Kingdoms of Iraq and Jordan unite in the Arab Federation, with King Faisal II of Iraq as head of state.

1958.title: May 13 Crisis in France
1958.body: May 13 Crisis in France: French Algerian protesters seize government offices in Algiers, leading to a military coup. During a visit to Caracas, Venezuela, Vice President Richard Nixon's car is attacked by anti-American demonstrators.

1958.title: Caracas
1958.body: During a visit to Caracas, Venezuela, Vice President Richard Nixon's car is attacked by anti-American demonstrators.

1959.title: Soviet Union
1959.body: The Soviet Union conducts its first census after World War II.

1959.title: American Airlines
1959.body: January 25 American Airlines begins the first U.S. domestic jet service with a Boeing 707 airliner flight between New York and Los Angeles. Pope John XXIII announces that the Second Vatican Council will be convened in Rome.

1959.title: London and Zürich Agreements
1959.body: First of the London and Zürich Agreements under which the United Kingdom agrees to grant independence to Cyprus.

1959.title: Tuscaloosa
1959.body: March 1 The USS Tuscaloosa, USS New Orleans, USS Tennessee and USS West Virginia are stricken from the United States Naval Vessel Register. Archbishop Makarios returns to Cyprus from exile.

1959.title: Archbishop
1959.body: Archbishop Makarios returns to Cyprus from exile.

1960.title: Buenos Aires
1960.body: In Buenos Aires, four Mossad agents abduct fugitive Nazi German war criminal Adolf Eichmann in order that he can be taken to Israel and put on trial. This is announced on May 23 by Prime Minister of Israel David Ben-Gurion.

1960.title: March 6 Vietnam War
1960.body: March 6 Vietnam War: The United States announces that 3,500 American soldiers will be sent to Vietnam. The Canton of Geneva in Switzerland gives women the right to vote.

1960.title: Vietnam War
1960.body: Vietnam War: The United States announces that 3,500 American soldiers will be sent to Vietnam.

1960.title: Ben-Hur
1960.body: At the 32nd Academy Awards Ceremony, Ben-Hur wins a record 11 Oscars, including Best Picture.

1960.title: The U-2 incident
1960.body: The U-2 incident: Several Soviet surface-to-air missiles shoot down an American Lockheed U-2 spy plane. Its pilot, Francis Gary Powers of the Central Intelligence Agency, is captured.

1961.title: John F. Kennedy
1961.body: , President John F. Kennedy delivers the first live presidential news conference. In it, he announces that the Soviet Union has freed the two surviving crewmen from the July 1 1960 RB-47 shootdown incident involving a USAF reconnaissance aircraft and a MiG-19 over the Barents Sea.

1961.title: March 13 1961 Kurenivka mudslide
1961.body: March 13 1961 Kurenivka mudslide: A dam bursts in Kiev, USSR, killing 145. United States delegate to the United Nations Security Council Adlai Stevenson votes against Portuguese policies in Africa. United States President John F.

1961.title: Alfredo Fioravanti
1961.body: January 5 Italian sculptor Alfredo Fioravanti enters the U.S. Consulate in Rome, and confesses that he was part of the team that forged the Etruscan terracotta warriors in the Metropolitan Museum of Art. After the 1960 military coup, General Cemal Gürsel forms the new government of Turkey (25th government).

1961.title: 1960 Turkish coup d'état
1961.body: After the 1960 military coup, General Cemal Gürsel forms the new government of Turkey (25th government).

1961.title: Cyprus
1961.body: Cyprus joins the Commonwealth of Nations, becoming the first small country in the Commonwealth.

1962.title: West Side Story
1962.body: The 34th Academy Awards Ceremony is held in the United States; West Side Story wins Best Picture.

1962.title: Soviet Union
1962.body: The Soviet government changes all place names honoring Molotov, Kaganovich and Georgy Malenkov.

1962.title: March–May – Yi–Ta incident
1962.body: March–May – Yi–Ta incident: At least 60,000 Chinese citizens migrate from the People's Republic of China to the Soviet Union, predominantly (but not exclusively) ethnic Kazakhs.

1962.title: Samoa
1962.body: Western Samoa becomes independent from New Zealand.

1962.title: Harmelen train disaster
1962.body: Harmelen train disaster: 93 die in the worst Dutch rail disaster.

1963.title: John F. Kennedy
1963.body: President of the United States John F. Kennedy signs Executive Order 11110, authorizing the Secretary of the Treasury to continue issuing silver certificates.

1963.title: Baháʼí Faith
1963.body: The first election of the Supreme Institution of the Baháʼí Faith (known as the Universal House of Justice, whose seat is at the Baháʼí World Centre on Mount Carmel in Haifa, Israel) is held.

1963.title: Vietnam War – Battle of Ap Bac
1963.body: Vietnam War – Battle of Ap Bac: The Viet Cong win their first major victory.

1963.title: Lawrence of Arabia
1963.body: The 35th Academy Awards ceremony is held. Lawrence of Arabia wins Best Picture.

1963.title: Fidel Castro
1963.body: Fidel Castro visits the Soviet Union.

1964.title: Card
1964.body: May 2 Vietnam War: Attack on USNS Card – An explosion caused by Viet Cong commandos causes carrier USNS Card to sink in the port of Saigon. Some 400–1,000 students march through Times Square, New York, and another 700 in San Francisco, in the first major student demonstration against the Vietnam War. Smaller marches also occur in Boston, Seattle, and Madison, Wisconsin.

1964.title: Good Friday
1964.body: March 27 (Good Friday) – The Great Alaskan earthquake, the second-most powerful known (and the most powerful earthquake recorded in North American history) at a magnitude of 9.2, strikes Southcentral Alaska, killing 125 people and inflicting massive damage to the city of Anchorage.

1964.title: Limassol
1964.body: February 11 Greeks and Turks begin fighting in Limassol, Cyprus. The Republic of China severs diplomatic relations with France because of French recognition of the People's Republic of China.

1964.title: Soviet Union
1964.body: January 29 The Soviet Union launches two scientific satellites, Elektron I and II, from a single rocket. Ranger 6 is launched by the US space agency NASA, on a mission to carry television cameras and crash-land on the Moon.

1964.title: The Ed Sullivan Show
1964.body: The Beatles perform for the first time for an American audience on The Ed Sullivan Show to a record television audience of 73 million people, launching Beatlemania in the United States, as part of The British Invasion.

1965.title: Vietnam War
1965.body: Vietnam War: Operation Rolling Thunder – The United States Air Force 2nd Air Division, United States Navy and South Vietnamese air force begin a 31⁄2-year aerial bombardment campaign against North Vietnam.

1965.title: American occupation of the Dominican Republic
1965.body: April 28 U.S. troops occupy the Dominican Republic. Vietnam War: Prime Minister of Australia Robert Menzies announces that the country will substantially increase its number of troops in South Vietnam, supposedly at the request of the Saigon government (it is later revealed that Menzies had asked the leadership in Saigon to send the request at the behest of the Americans).

1965.title: Ranger 8
1965.body: February 20 Ranger 8 crashes into the Moon, after a successful mission of photographing possible landing sites for the Apollo program astronauts. Suat Hayri Ürgüplü forms the new (interim) government of Turkey (29th government).

1965.title: Suat Hayri Ürgüplü
1965.body: Suat Hayri Ürgüplü forms the new (interim) government of Turkey (29th government).

1965.title: Death and state funeral of Winston Churchill
1965.body: The state funeral of Sir Winston Churchill takes place in London with the largest assembly of dignitaries in the world until the 2005 funeral of Pope John Paul II.

1966.title: 1966 Iloilo City fire
1966.body: February 7 The Great Fire of Iloilo, Philippines, breaks out in a lumber yard and burns for almost half a day, destroying nearly three-quarters of the City Proper area and causing 50 million pesos in total property damage. Lyndon B. Johnson of the United States and Nguyễn Cao Kỳ of South Vietnam convene with other officials in a summit in Honolulu, Hawaii to discuss the course of the Vietnam War.

1966.title: Lyndon B. Johnson
1966.body: Lyndon B. Johnson of the United States and Nguyễn Cao Kỳ of South Vietnam convene with other officials in a summit in Honolulu, Hawaii to discuss the course of the Vietnam War.

1966.title: Indonesia
1966.body: March 8 Anti-communist demonstrations occur at the Indonesian Foreign Ministry. S. announces it will substantially increase the number of its troops in Vietnam.

1966.title: Vietnam War
1966.body: Vietnam War: The U.S. announces it will substantially increase the number of its troops in Vietnam.

1966.title: Pakistan
1966.body: January 10 Pakistani–Indian peace negotiations end successfully with the signing of the Tashkent Declaration, a day before the sudden death of Indian prime minister Lal Bahadur Shastri. The House of Representatives of the US state of Georgia refuses to allow African-American representative Julian Bond to take his seat, because of his anti-war stance.

1967.title: John F. Kennedy
1967.body: S. President John F. Kennedy is moved to a permanent burial place at Arlington National Cemetery.

1967.title: Alexei Kosygin
1967.body: Alexei Kosygin arrives in the UK for an 8-day visit. He meets The Queen on February 9.

1967.title: Operation Deckhouse Five
1967.body: Vietnam War: United States Marine Corps and Army of the Republic of Vietnam troops launch Operation Deckhouse Five in the Mekong Delta.

1967.title: Sierra Leone
1967.body: March 21 A military coup takes place in Sierra Leone. Vietnam War: In ongoing campus unrest, Howard University students protesting the Vietnam War, the ROTC program on campus and the draft, confront Gen. S.

1967.title: January 27 Apollo 1
1967.body: January 27 Apollo 1: U.S. astronauts Gus Grissom, Ed White and Roger Chaffee are killed when fire breaks out in their Apollo spacecraft during a launch pad test. The United States, Soviet Union and United Kingdom sign the Outer Space Treaty (ratified by USSR May 19; comes into force October 10), prohibiting weapons of mass destruction from space.

1968.title: Dakar
1968.body: Israeli submarine INS Dakar sinks in the Mediterranean Sea, killing 69.

1968.title: January 21 Vietnam War
1968.body: January 21 Vietnam War: Battle of Khe Sanh – One of the most publicized and controversial battles of the war begins, ending on April 8. 1968 Thule Air Base B-52 crash: A U.S. B-52 Stratofortress crashes in Greenland, discharging 4 nuclear bombs.

1968.title: Vietnam War
1968.body: Vietnam War: Battle of Khe Sanh – One of the most publicized and controversial battles of the war begins, ending on April 8.

1968.title: February 1 Vietnam War
1968.body: February 1 Vietnam War: Execution of Nguyễn Văn Lém – A Viet Cong officer is summarily executed by Nguyễn Ngọc Loan, a South Vietnamese National Police Chief. The event is photographed by Eddie Adams. S.

1968.title: March 16 Vietnam War – My Lai massacre
1968.body: March 16 Vietnam War – My Lai massacre: American troops kill between 347 and 504 unarmed civilians and rape women and children. S. efforts in Vietnam.

1969.title: Jews
1969.body: January 27 Fourteen men, 9 of them Jews, are executed in Baghdad for spying for Israel. Reverend Ian Paisley, Northern Irish Unionist leader and founder of the Free Presbyterian Church of Ulster is jailed for three months for illegal assembly.

1969.title: Golda Meir
1969.body: March 17 Golda Meir becomes the first female prime minister of Israel. The Longhope life-boat is lost after answering a mayday call during severe storms in the Pentland Firth between Orkney and the northern tip of Scotland; the entire crew of 8 die.

1969.title: March 3 Apollo program
1969.body: March 3 Apollo program: NASA launches Apollo 9 (James McDivitt, Rusty Schweickart, David Scott) to test the lunar module. In a Los Angeles court, Sirhan Sirhan admits that he killed presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy.

1969.title: Los Angeles
1969.body: In a Los Angeles court, Sirhan Sirhan admits that he killed presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy.

1969.title: Ayub Khan
1969.body: Ayub Khan resigns as President of Pakistan and transfers the power to Yahya Khan.

1970.title: Artemis
1970.body: The Apollo 13 crew sets the record for the farthest humans have traveled, 400,171 miles (644,013 km), which would stand until the Artemis II lunar flyby in 2026.

1970.title: Adolf Hitler
1970.body: Fragments of burnt human remains believed to be those of Adolf Hitler, Eva Braun, Joseph Goebbels, Magda Goebbels and the Goebbels children are crushed and scattered in the Biederitz river at a KGB center in Magdeburg, East Germany.

1970.title: May 6 Arms Crisis in the Republic of Ireland
1970.body: May 6 Arms Crisis in the Republic of Ireland: Charles Haughey and Neil Blaney are dismissed as members of the Irish Government for accusations of their involvement in a plot to import arms for use by the Provisional Irish Republican Army in Northern Ireland. Feyenoord win the European Cup in association football after a 2–1 win over Celtic.

1970.title: Feyenoord
1970.body: Feyenoord win the European Cup in association football after a 2–1 win over Celtic.

1970.title: McCartney
1970.body: In a press release written in mock-interview style, that is included in promotional copies of his first solo album, Paul McCartney announces that he has left The Beatles.

1971.title: Vietnam War
1971.body: Vietnam War: Backed by American air and artillery support, South Vietnamese troops invade Laos.

1971.title: Apollo program
1971.body: Apollo program: Apollo 14 (carrying astronauts Alan Shepard, Stuart Roosa, and Edgar Mitchell) lifts off on the third successful lunar landing mission.

1971.title: Extreme
1971.body: February 9 The 6.5–6.7 Mw Sylmar earthquake hits the Greater Los Angeles Area with a maximum Mercalli intensity of XI (Extreme), killing 64 and injuring 2,000. Satchel Paige becomes the first Negro league player to be elected into the Baseball Hall of Fame. Apollo program: Apollo 14 returns to Earth after the third human Moon landing.

1971.title: Brazil
1971.body: Seventy Brazilian political prisoners are released in Santiago, Chile; Giovanni Enrico Bucher is released January 16.

1971.title: OPEC
1971.body: Representatives of 23 western oil companies begin negotiations with OPEC in Tehran to stabilize oil prices; February 14 they sign a treaty with 6 Arab states of the Persian Gulf.

1972.title: Shoichi Yokoi
1972.body: Japanese soldier Shoichi Yokoi is discovered in Guam; he has spent 28 years in the jungle, having failed to surrender after World War II.

1972.title: January 30 Bloody Sunday
1972.body: January 30 Bloody Sunday: The British Army kills 14 unarmed nationalist civil rights marchers in Derry, Northern Ireland. Pakistan withdraws from the Commonwealth of Nations.

1972.title: Pakistan
1972.body: Pakistan withdraws from the Commonwealth of Nations.

1972.title: Libyan Arab Republic
1972.body: March 4 Libya and the Soviet Union sign a cooperation treaty. The Organisation of the Islamic Conference Charter is signed (effective February 28, 1973).

1972.title: Luna 20
1972.body: The Soviet uncrewed spaceship Luna 20 lands on the Moon.

1973.title: Libyan Arab Airlines Flight 114
1973.body: Libyan Arab Airlines Flight 114 (Boeing 727) is shot down by Israeli fighter aircraft over the Sinai Desert, after the passenger plane is suspected of being an enemy military plane. There are 108 fatalities, and only five people on board survive (one crew member and four passengers).

1973.title: April 10 Operation Spring of Youth
1973.body: April 10 Operation Spring of Youth: Israeli commandos raid Beirut, assassinating 3 leaders of the Palestinian Resistance Movement. The Lebanese army's inaction brings the immediate resignation of Prime Minister Saeb Salam, a Sunni Muslim. The Islamic Republic of Pakistan introduced its new constitution, its supreme law.

1973.title: Operation Spring of Youth
1973.body: Operation Spring of Youth: Israeli commandos raid Beirut, assassinating 3 leaders of the Palestinian Resistance Movement. The Lebanese army's inaction brings the immediate resignation of Prime Minister Saeb Salam, a Sunni Muslim.

1973.title: Vietnam War
1973.body: Vietnam War: Citing progress in peace negotiations, U.S. President Richard Nixon announces the suspension of offensive action in North Vietnam.

1973.title: Pakistan
1973.body: The Islamic Republic of Pakistan introduced its new constitution, its supreme law.

1974.title: Kiryat Shmona massacre
1974.body: The Kiryat Shmona massacre takes place in Israel.

1974.title: Bülent Ecevit
1974.body: Bülent Ecevit of CHP forms the new government of Turkey (37th government, partner MSP).

1974.title: Laju
1974.body: January 31 The South Vietnamese government and the Viet Cong announced that they would resume prisoner of war exchanges on February 8, after a suspension of seven months. Two members of the Japanese Red Army (JRA) and two members of the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP) made a failed attempt to blow up oil tanks at the Shell oil refinery complex on Pulau Bukom at Singapore. The terrorists then hijacked the ferry Laju and took its five crew members hostage.

1974.title: Greek military junta of 1967–1974
1974.body: The Greek military junta sponsors a coup d'état in Cyprus, replacing President Makarios III with Nikos Sampson. On that same day, reporter Christine Chubbuck commits suicide for the first-time-ever on live television.

1974.title: Watergate scandal
1974.body: S. President Richard Nixon announces his resignation on August 8, effective at noon on August 9. August 9 – Vice President Gerald Ford becomes the 38th president of the United States upon Nixon's resignation.

1975.title: March 6 Algiers Agreement
1975.body: March 6 Algiers Agreement: Iran and Iraq announce a settlement in their border dispute. A bomb explodes in the Paris offices of Springer publishers. The March 6 Group (connected to the Red Army Faction) demands amnesty for the Baader-Meinhof Group.

1975.title: Algiers Agreement
1975.body: Algiers Agreement: Iran and Iraq announce a settlement in their border dispute.

1975.title: April 13 Bus massacre
1975.body: April 13 Bus massacre: The Kataeb militia kills 27 Palestinians during an attack on their bus in Ain El Remmeneh, Lebanon, triggering the Lebanese Civil War which lasts until 1990. A coup d'état in Chad led by the military overthrows and kills President François Tombalbaye.

1975.title: Bus massacre
1975.body: Bus massacre: The Kataeb militia kills 27 Palestinians during an attack on their bus in Ain El Remmeneh, Lebanon, triggering the Lebanese Civil War which lasts until 1990.

1975.title: Sanyō Shinkansen
1975.body: March 10 Vietnam War: North Vietnamese troops attack Ban Mê Thuột in South Vietnam on their way to capturing Saigon. An extended portion of Sanyō Shinkansen between Okayama Station and Hakata Station opens, giving the Japanese Shinkansen high speed rail network access to the country's second island, Kyushu.

1976.title: Apple Computer
1976.body: April 1 Apple Computer Company is formed by Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak in California. S. government, to take control of 13 major Northeast Class-1 railroads that have filed for bankruptcy protection.

1976.title: Glenbrook, New South Wales
1976.body: A locomotive crashes into the rear of a commuter train in Glenbrook, Australia, killing one person onboard and injuring ten others in the 1976 Glenbrook rail accident.

1976.title: Lapua Cartridge Factory explosion
1976.body: April 13 The Lapua Cartridge Factory explosion in Lapua, Finland kills 40. The United States Treasury Department reintroduces the two-dollar bill as a Federal Reserve Note on Thomas Jefferson's 233rd birthday as part of the United States Bicentennial celebration.

1976.title: Argentina
1976.body: March 24 Argentina military forces depose president Isabel Perón. A general strike takes place in the People's Republic of the Congo.

1977.title: Likud
1977.body: The Likud Party, led by Menachem Begin, wins the national elections in Israel.

1977.title: Elizabeth
1977.body: Queen Elizabeth II opens the New Zealand Parliament in person, after Parliament is summoned for a special short session to allow her to deliver the Speech from the Throne.

1977.title: Parliament of Australia
1977.body: The Australian parliament is opened by Elizabeth II in her capacity as Queen of Australia.

1977.title: Soyuz 24
1977.body: The Soviet Union launches Soyuz 24 (Viktor Gorbatko, Yury Glazkov) to dock with the Salyut 5 space station.

1977.title: Aeroflot Flight 331
1977.body: May 27 The 1977 Aeroflot Ilyushin 62 airplane crash in Cuba kills 69 people. A demonstration and coup attempt in Angola takes place. Thousands are killed by the government and Cuban forces.

1978.title: Holy Crown of Hungary
1978.body: The Holy Crown of Hungary (also known as Stephen of Hungary Crown) is returned to Hungary from the United States, where it was held since World War II.

1978.title: March 11 Coastal Road massacre
1978.body: March 11 Coastal Road massacre: Palestinian terrorists kill 34 Israelis. Claude François, French entertainer born 1939, dies by electrocution in his home in Paris.

1978.title: Coastal Road massacre
1978.body: Coastal Road massacre: Palestinian terrorists kill 34 Israelis.

1978.title: Operation Litani
1978.body: Operation Litani: Israeli forces invade Lebanon.

1978.title: Pacific Western Airlines Flight 314
1978.body: February 11 Pacific Western Airlines Flight 314, a Boeing 737-200, crashes in Cranbrook, British Columbia, killing 44 of the 50 people on board. Somalia mobilizes its troops to deal with an apparent Ethiopian attack. The People's Republic of China lifts a ban on works by Aristotle, William Shakespeare and Charles Dickens.

1979.title: Mohammad Reza Pahlavi
1979.body: Shah Mohammad Reza Pahlavi flees Iran with his family, relocating to Egypt after a year of turmoil.

1979.title: Ayatollah
1979.body: Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini returns to Tehran, Iran after nearly 15 years of exile.

1979.title: Ruhollah Khomeini
1979.body: The Iranian Revolution ends with the Iranian army withdrawing to its barracks leaving power in the hands of Ayatollah Khomeini, ending the Pahlavi dynasty.

1979.title: 1979 Daytona 500
1979.body: February 18 The 1979 Daytona 500 is televised on CBS, the first ever full airing of a 500-mile race on US television, Richard Petty wins after Cale Yarborough and Donnie Allison battle for first place on the final lap and crash out, leading to a fist fight. This race brought NASCAR to a wider audience. The Khomeini government in Iran cuts diplomatic relations with Israel.

1979.title: Iran-Israel relations
1979.body: The Khomeini government in Iran cuts diplomatic relations with Israel.

1980.title: Canadian Caper
1980.body: Canadian Caper: Six United States diplomats, posing as Canadians, manage to escape from Tehran, Iran, as they board a flight to Zürich, Switzerland, on Swissair.

1980.title: Ayatollah
1980.body: Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini states that Iran's parliament will decide the fate of the American embassy hostages.

1980.title: Iran
1980.body: The United States severs diplomatic relations with Iran and imposes economic sanctions, following the taking of American hostages on November 4, 1979.

1980.title: Operation Eagle Claw
1980.body: Operation Eagle Claw, a commando mission in Iran to rescue American embassy hostages, is aborted after mechanical problems ground the rescue helicopters. Eight United States troops are killed in a mid-air collision during the failed operation.

1980.title: Israel
1980.body: Israel and Egypt establish diplomatic relations.

1981.title: Iran
1981.body: Iran releases the 52 Americans held for 444 days, minutes after Ronald Reagan is sworn in as the 40th President of the United States, ending the Iran hostage crisis.

1981.title: Trooping the Colour
1981.body: At the Trooping the Colour ceremony in London, teenager Marcus Sarjeant fires 6 blank shots close to Queen Elizabeth II, startling her horse.

1981.title: Riyadh
1981.body: In Riyadh, the Gulf Cooperation Council is created among Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.

1981.title: Pope John Paul II assassination attempt
1981.body: Pope John Paul II assassination attempt: Pope John Paul II is shot by Mehmet Ali Ağca, a Turkish gunman, as he enters St. Peter's Square in Vatican City to address a general audience. The Pope recovers.

1981.title: Attempted assassination of Ronald Reagan
1981.body: Attempted assassination of Ronald Reagan: U.S. President Ronald Reagan is shot in the chest outside a Washington, D.C., hotel by John Hinckley Jr.; two police officers and Press Secretary James Brady are also wounded.

1982.title: Israel
1982.body: Israel completes its withdrawal from the Sinai Peninsula in accordance with the Egypt–Israel peace treaty of 1979.

1982.title: Chariots of Fire
1982.body: March 29 Royal Assent is given to the Canada Act 1982, setting the stage for the repatriation of the Canadian Constitution on April 17. The 54th Academy Awards, hosted by Johnny Carson, are held at the Dorothy Chandler Pavilion in Los Angeles. Chariots of Fire wins Best Picture and 3 other Academy Awards.

1982.title: Michael Jordan
1982.body: Freshman Michael Jordan hits a jumper with 17 seconds remaining to lift the North Carolina Tar Heels to a 63–62 win over the Georgetown Hoyas in the 1982 NCAA Division I men's basketball championship game at the Louisiana Superdome in New Orleans.

1982.title: 1982 World's Fair
1982.body: A crowd of over 100,000 attends the first day of the 1982 World's Fair in Knoxville, Tennessee, which is kicked off with an address by President Ronald Reagan. Over 11 million people attend during its six-month run.

1982.title: Malaysia
1982.body: In Malaysia and Singapore, clocks are adjusted to the same time zone, UTC+8 (GMT+8.00).

1983.title: Michael Jackson
1983.body: Michael Jackson performs the Moonwalk for the first time during his performance of Billie Jean on Motown 25, as well he wore single white glove made of rhinestone gems. The step, would become one of the most globally recognizable dance moves.

1983.title: Lebanon
1983.body: Lebanon, Israel, and the United States sign an agreement on Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon.

1983.title: Hamburger
1983.body: Hamburger SV defeat Juventus 1–0 in the final of the European Cup.

1983.title: Strategic Defense Initiative
1983.body: Strategic Defense Initiative: U.S. President Ronald Reagan makes his initial proposal to develop technology to intercept enemy missiles.

1983.title: Seve Ballesteros
1983.body: Spain's Seve Ballesteros won the 47th PGA Masters Tournament.

1984.title: Iran
1984.body: Iran accuses Iraq of using chemical weapons; the United Nations condemns their use on March 30.

1984.title: Michael Jackson
1984.body: American singer Michael Jackson's hair caught on fire during the making of the Pepsi commercial.

1984.title: Steve Jobs
1984.body: Steve Jobs launches the Macintosh personal computer in the United States.

1984.title: Rahimuddin Khan
1984.body: General Rahimuddin Khan becomes the first man in Pakistan's history to rule over two of its provinces, after becoming interim Governor of Sindh.

1984.title: Operation Meghdoot
1984.body: India launches Operation Meghdoot, bringing most of the disputed Siachen Glacier region of Kashmir under Indian control and triggering the Siachen conflict with Pakistan.

1985.title: Israel
1985.body: February 16 Israel begins withdrawing troops from Lebanon. The ideology of Hezbollah is declared in a program issued in Beirut.

1985.title: Lebanon hostage crisis
1985.body: Lebanon hostage crisis: CNN reporter Jeremy Levin is freed from captivity in Lebanon.

1985.title: Amadeus
